text: Your attention works fine. You don't need to "fix" it. You just don't have a target that makes it easy to focus for months on end. You don't have a vision so strong that everything else becomes boring.
zh: 你注意的很好,你不会有事的 你不需要"修补"它。 你只是没有目标 使得它容易 专注于结束几个月。 你没有那么强的视力 以至于其他的都变得无聊

text: Save any post or link, research what's trending, schedule content, and more: eden.so
zh: 保存任何帖子或链接,研究什么是趋势,调度内容,以及更多:eden.so.

text: If you don't know what to learn, start writing. Writing teaches you how to think, how to learn, and how to attract an audience that supports your work. Those 3 skills are needed more than ever.
zh: 学无所学者,始写. 写作教你如何思考,如何学习,如何吸引支持你工作的观众. 这3种技能比以往任何时候更需要。

text: You can't focus because you never let yourself be bored. Actually bored. Not "bored" scrolling on your phone taxing your brain into oblivion with more dopamine in 10 minutes than people a thousand years ago would get in an entire month.
zh: 你无法集中精神 因为你从来不让自己无聊 其实无聊。 而不是在电话上"无聊"的卷轴 把你的大脑 与多巴胺在10分钟内被遗忘 比一千年前的人在一个月内得到。

text: Skills that have nothing to do with money but are worth dedicating an immense amount of practice to: - Charisma - Metacognition - Critical thinking - Sitting with discomfort - Articulating what you believe and why - Changing your beliefs when presented with new information Almost nobody actually practices these and it shows.
zh: 技能与金钱无关,但值得大量实践: 魅力 - 认识 - 批判性思维 - 与不适感坐在一起 - 描述你所相信的和为什么 - 展示你所信仰的,当你得到新的信息时,改变你的信仰 几乎没有人实际实践这些,它就显示出来。

text: If you don't wake up excited and go to bed tired, drop everything and think of what your future will look like if you keep repeating the same day for the rest of your life. Sit with that discomfort until a new direction appears.
zh: 如果你不醒来 兴奋起来去睡觉 疲倦, 放下一切 并思考你的未来 将是什么样子 如果你继续重复 当天 你余生。 坐在那不适处,直到出现新的方向。

text: The fastest way to change your life is to rip yourself out of your (physical and digital) environment. Change everything overnight. The places you go, the accounts you follow, the info you consume, etc. It's difficult but it absolutely works.
zh: 改变你生活最快的方法是 把自己从你(物理和数字)环境中撕裂出来。 一夜之间改变一切 所去之处,所循之户口,所消耗之资料等. 很难,但绝对有效

text: Stress is great for brute-forcing mindless tasks, but it absolutely destroys your ability to think spatially. It destroys your creativity, which is the one thing that will give you a competitive advantage over everyone optimizing for productivity.
zh: 压力是巨大的 野蛮的无脑任务, 但绝对摧毁了 你的能力 空间思考。 它会破坏你的创造力, 这是唯一能给你一个 优于每个人的竞争力优势, 以达到生产力的最佳。

text: You only need 1 hour. 1 hour of building. 1 hour of writing. 1 hour of lifting. 1 hour of studying. 1 hour of any form of bettering yourself, because it quickly compounds. 1 hour feels like nothing until you look back 365 hours later and everything's changed.
zh: 你只需要一个小时 建楼1小时. 写作1小时. 抬起来一个小时 学习1小时. 1小时的任何形式的改善, 因为它很快地复合。 一个小时的感觉就像什么都没有 直到你回头看365小时后 一切都变了

text: The most important decision of your life
zh: 你生命中最重要的决定

text: If you don't have a goal so meaningful it makes other people's opinions irrelevant, you will lose control of your life. You will adopt the goals assigned to you by your parents, peers, or society.
zh: 如果你没有目标那么有意义 别人的意见就变得无关紧要 你会失去对人生的控制 父母、同伴或社会赋予你的目标。

text: The 2-4 hours you spend scrolling each day (or 730-1460 hours each year) is more than enough time to write a book, build a business, or get in shape. In the moment, it seems like nothing. That's why it's so dangerous. Your time disappears without you being conscious of it.
zh: 每天2-4小时的滚动时间(或每年730-1460小时),足以写书,建立企业,或成型. 此刻,似乎没什么 故曰险也. 你的时间消失的时候你没有意识到。

text: Yes, anyone can build anything now. We know. But it wasn't that hard to do before. You execute a series of steps toward a goal. The problem is psychological. Most people will still fail to start, and even more will fail to iterate. Your competition isn't as large as you think.
zh: 是的,现在任何人都可以建造任何东西。 我们知道 但以前没那么难 你为达到目标而执行了一系列步骤 问题在于心理问题。 大多数人仍然无法开始,甚至更多的人无法完成。 你的比赛没有你想的那么大

text: How to master any skill fast: - stop studying - outline a project - start building it - hit a roadblock - figure out how to overcome it - repeat 4 and 5 for the rest of your life Most people don't get past 1, the rest spiral into complacency after 4.
zh: 如何快速掌握技能: - 停止学习 - 概述项目 - 开始建设 - 撞上路障 - 想办法克服它 - 重复4和5 度过你的余生 大多数人不会越过1, 剩下的在4之后就变得自满了.

text: Normalize disappearing for an extended period of time to do a factory reset on your focus and creativity.
zh: 将工厂的注意力和创造力 重新设定在工厂里 并让工厂在很长一段时间内消失

text: Read more here: letters.thedankoe.com/p/life-is-a-mi…
zh: 更多内容请参见: letters.thedankoe.com/p/life-is-a-mi...

text: Most people quit because they forget that you have to be bad at something before you can be good at it. It's so obvious. You suck. Of course you're not going to win in 2 weeks. But if you can learn to enjoy extended periods of failure, you will make it very, very far in life.
zh: 大多数人都辞职了,因为他们忘记了,你必须先做坏事,才能做到善. 如此显性. 你个烂人 当然,你不会赢 在两周。 但是如果你能学会享受长时间的失败, 你会在生活中做到非常,非常远。

text: You aren't going to get lucky repeating the same day. You need to go on more side quests. Weekly. Daily if you can. Even one tiny new decision can substantially increase your chances at luck.
zh: 你不可能在同一天就走运 你需要做更多的边征 周报. 每日若能请. 哪怕是小小的新决定 也能大大增加你的机会

text: If you feel lost, build something. A business. Your body. A skill set. Anything that gives you a reason to learn and focus. Don't worry about choosing the right thing. Don't think about how difficult it will be. Just start moving forward and you'll find a path that feels right
zh: 如果你觉得迷路了,就去造个东西 生意,我说,我需要你帮忙 A business. 汝身众生. 一套技能。 任何让你有理由学习和专心致志的东西 何虑择正之义. 别想会有多难 开始向前走 你会找到一条正确的道路

text: The greatest trait you can acquire is to work with tremendous intensity on things that matter to you, and more importantly, be strangely unbothered when those things don't work out.
zh: 你所能获得的最大特征是 以极大的强度 处理那些对你很重要的事情, 更重要的是, 当那些事情不成功的时候, 奇特地不发生任何关系。

text: If you want a rare life, you have to be delusional. Doubt can enter your mind, and it can sound reasonable, but if you entertain it too much it will slowly drag you down into stagnation. I'd rather reap the lesson from massive failure than do nothing because it's not "realistic."
zh: 欲得稀有之生,须妄想. 怀疑会进入你的思维, 听起来很合理, 但是如果你太过体会, 它会慢慢地拖累你陷入停滞。 我宁愿从大规模失败中吸取教训,也不愿因为"不现实主义"而无所作为.

text: At some point, usually in your 20s, you'll notice that the people around you stop believing in themselves. And no matter how hard you try, you can't save them. By all means, do not let it infect your mind. Stay on your path.
zh: 通常在20多岁的时候,你会发现周围的人不再相信自己. 不管你怎么努力,你都救不了他们 绝对不要让它影响你的心灵 随顺行故.

text: You need to read more, and you need to consume less. You need to set aside time to do a workout for the mind, and you need to give it time to recover. Especially now that it's so easy for your cognitive capacity to atrophy. Refuse to become mentally obese.
zh: 你需要多读些, 并且需要少消费。 你需要留出时间去为心灵做锻炼,需要给它时间来恢复. 尤其是现在,你很容易失去认知能力 拒绝成为精神肥胖。

text: If you want to achieve anything great, it needs to become your one true priority. The only thing on your mind. Nobody accidentally got rich from business. Nobody accidentally built a great physique. They were obsessed with it for multiple years until it became their default.
zh: 如果你想实现任何伟大的目标,它需要成为你真正的优先事项。 唯一能让你想到的事 没人会意外地从生意中发财 没人会意外地造出一个伟大的体格 他们沉迷于其中多年 直到它成为他们的默认。

text: A pattern I've noticed in stuck people: They're always busy. They never stop moving. They have 47 tabs open and a notebook-sized to-do list. But if you ask them what they accomplished this week that actually matters, their mind goes blank. Busyness isn't a badge of honor.
zh: 我注意到卡住的人的图案: 他们总是忙着 他们总是不停地移动。 他们有47个标签和笔记本大小的待办事宜清单。 但是如果你问他们这周的成绩 究竟有什么意义 他们的头脑就变得空白了 忙碌不是荣誉的徽章.

text: If you're a writer, be okay with repeating what's already been said, because most ideas have already been exhausted, and genuinely novel ideas are rare. Nobody follows you because you're talking about something new. They follow you because they want to read your point of view.
zh: 如果你是作家, 请不要重复已经说过的话, 因为大多数的想法已经用尽, 而真正的新颖的想法是罕见的。 没人会跟踪你 因为你在谈论新事物 他们跟踪你 是因为他们想读你的观点

text: You as a single person have more power today than a 20 person company of the past. That's insane. The internet gave you the ability to learn anything. Social media gave you the leverage to reach anyone. AI is giving you the ability to create almost anything. Please don't waste it
zh: 你作为一个单身的人,今天拥有比过去20个人公司更多的权力. 这太疯狂了 互联网让你有能力学习任何东西。 社交媒体给了你影响力 以达到任何人。 AI给你创造几乎一切的能力. 求你别浪费了

text: Write more essays: letters.thedankoe.com/p/im-begging-y…
zh: 写更多的散文: letters.thedankoe.com/p/im-begging-y...

text: You need to write more. Without AI. Without templates. Without knowing what you're writing about. Just you, an idea, and enough time to do the difficult cognitive work necessary to reach true understanding. If you don't, your ability to think will drastically decline.
zh: 你需要写更多。 没有AI。 没有模板。 而不识其文. 只有你,一个想法, 有足够的时间做困难的认知工作 达成真正的理解。 如果你不这样做,你的思想能力就会急剧下降。

text: The more "informed" you feel, the less capable of thinking you become.
zh: 你越是"知情",思考能力就越弱.

text: You can learn anything in 2 weeks. You can't master it, obviously, but if you obsess over it, you can become better at it than most people ever will. You'd be surprised how fast your life can change when you understand this.
zh: 两周后你就能学到任何东西了 显然你无法掌握它 但如果你沉迷于它 你就能比大多数人更擅长它 你会惊讶的 当你明白这一点,你的生活会改变多快。

text: It seems like everyone is obsessed with productivity and efficiency yet rarely get anything meaningful done. I'm convinced your best work is done when you're not working. When you have space for creative ideas to emerge that drastically change the trajectory of your life/work.
zh: 看来每个人都沉迷于生产力和效率,却很少得到有意义的成就。 我相信你没工作的时候 就能完成你最好的工作了 当你有空间提出创意 从而大大改变你的生活/工作轨迹时。

text: Competition is largely an illusion. 95% of people don't even try to do great things. 0.1% of the people are loud, so you overestimate how many people there are. The rest get stuck worrying about competition and quitting after 2 weeks.
zh: 竞争基本上是一种幻想。 95%的人甚至不尝试做伟大的事情. 0.1%的人很吵 所以你高估了有多少人 剩下的人担心竞争 两周后就辞职了

text: Habits so simple you think they’re not worth doing, but have a profound impact on your life: - Not touching your phone when you wake up - Not thinking about work after work is done - Putting a book down once you find an idea worth thinking about - Setting aside time to do nothing for 10 minutes a day - Going on a short walk after each meal - Eating a meal without a screen in front of you - Saying "I don't know" instead of pretending you do - Asking "What if this isn't actually a problem?" before trying to solve it - Letting yourself be bad at something instead of expecting perfection - Trying to understand something you disagree with instead of looking for flaws - Defaulting to "no" until you think through the commitment
zh: 哈比特人如此简单,你认为他们不值得做,但对你的生活有深远的影响: - 醒来时不碰手机 - 不考虑工作后的事就完成了 - 一旦找到值得思考的想法就放下一本书 - 留出时间每天不做10分钟 - 每餐后走一段短路 - 吃一顿饭,没有屏幕在你面前 - 说"我不知道"而不是假装你做 - 问"如果这不是个问题呢?"然后才试图解决 - 让自己在某件事上表现不好而不是期望完美 - 试着理解你不同意的东西而不是寻找缺陷 - 默认"不"直到你通过承诺来思考.

text: "I'm not a creative person." No, you are, everyone is, but your mind is just clogged by all of the podcasts and social media you ram into it without properly digesting it. You're conditioned to believe you can only take a certain path in life, and you don't daydream or entertain stupid ideas that could set you on an entirely new trajectory. You're so obsessed with being productive and efficient that you feel like you're always falling behind, and that stress prevents you from thinking outside the box. You need to slow the fuck down, allow yourself to be bored (actually bored, not so overstimulated that you find enjoyable things boring), and pursue a life that you design, not one that was assigned to you.
zh: "我不是一个创造力的人。" 不,你,每个人都是, 但你的头脑只是堵塞 所有播客 和社交媒体你挤进它, 没有适当的消化它。 你被设定为只能选择人生中某一条道路的信念, 并且你不会梦想或娱乐那些可以让你走上全新的轨道的愚蠢的想法。 你如此痴迷于生产力和效率,以致于感觉自己总是落在后面,压力也阻止了你思考在盒子之外. 你需要慢下来,让自己变得无聊(实际上无聊,不要太激动以致发现令人愉快的东西变得无聊),追求你设计的生活,而不是被指派给你的生活.

text: Your brain works against you until you give it a meaningful goal to wire itself around.
zh: 你的大脑会和你作对 直到你给它一个有意义的目标 来绕圈子

text: Copy-pasting into one chat box sucks. So we built a canvas for LLMs, content, and research. Create markdown docs, paste social links, upload files, and connect to AI chats. Agents can handle busy work, but creative work needs a visual space to connect ideas.
zh: 复制到一个聊天箱里很烂 所以我们为LLMS 内容和研究 做了一个画布。 创建标记下行的docs,粘贴社交链接,上传文件,并连接到AI聊天. 代理可以处理繁忙的工作,但创作工作需要视觉空间来连接各种想法.

text: The single most important thing you can do in today's world is to stop operating from the old paradigm. If you need to be told what to do next (go to school, get a job, retire at 65) the outcome of your life will always be in someone else's hands. You must learn how to direct your own work. You must learn how to tolerate and mitigate risk and uncertainty. You must figure out what you want and teach yourself everything necessary to get it. It's extremely difficult, but not as difficult as the silent suffering people learn to accept as "normal."
zh: 在当今世界上,你唯一能做的最重要的事情就是停止从旧的范式上运作. 如果你需要被告知下一步要做什么(去上学,找工作,65岁退休),你的生活结果将永远掌握在别人的手中. 你必须学会如何指导自己的工作。 你必须学会如何容忍和减轻风险和不确定性。 你必须想出你想要的 并教自己所有必要的 得到它。 极端困难,但并不像默默受苦的人们学会接受为"正常"那样困难.

text: It doesn't matter if it takes years. What the fuck else are you going to do? You aren't going to learn, create, experiment, grow, and everything that makes life enjoyable because you have something better to be doing? Your mind is playing tricks on you.
zh: 虽年不 takes. 你他妈的还打算怎么办? 你不会去学习,创造,实验,成长, 和所有能让生活快乐的东西 因为你有更好的事情要做? 你的脑子在耍你

text: The minute I become too frugal or money-conscious is the moment it starts to control me and becomes difficult to make. When I spend almost carelessly, it seems to find its way back. Money-induced stress narrows the mind and new opportunities become invisible.
zh: 我一变得太节俭或钱意识 就会开始控制我 变得很难做到 当我几乎不小心花掉的时候 它似乎又回到了过去 金钱引起的压力使思想狭窄,新的机会变得无形。

text: The problem is that you don't know what you want to do, and figuring out what you want to do requires learning, experimentation, and effort - so you do nothing.
zh: 问题是,你不知道你想做什么, 并且想出你想要做什么, 需要学习,实验,和努力, 所以你什么都不做。

text: Polymaths will dominate the next 5 years, but only if they practice the skill of knowing what to ignore. You can learn and do anything now, meaning that it will become increasingly rare for a person to put time, attention, and care into one thing.
zh: 波利玛斯在未来5年中将占据主导地位,但前提是他们要练习知道该忽略什么的技巧. 你可以学习和做任何事情, 这意味着一个人将时间,注意力, 和关心 放在一件事中, 将变得越来越少。

text: The secret is to have something you're excited to do when you wake up. It takes a few months of learning, failure, and experimentation to find what that thing is, but once you do, everything is just... better.
zh: 秘密是醒来后有你兴奋的事情要做. 需要几个月的学习,失败, 和实验才能找到那东西是什么, 但是一旦你做了, 一切都只是... 更好。

text: Focus is becoming the rarest skill on the planet. Talent, intelligence, marketing, prompting, vibe coding, and whatever else doesn't matter as much as being able to channel your attention for an extended period of time into one useful thing that you want to see in the world.
zh: 焦点正在成为地球上最罕见的技能. 才华 智力 营销 促动 气氛编码 其它的都无所谓 能够把注意力 引向一个你想要在世界上看到的有用的东西

text: Self-improvement is a gateway drug into entrepreneurship because you start seeing life as a system you can optimize. You start to believe that if you don't like something about your life, you can change it. And since the source of most people's problems is money, you stop waiting for a raise and start building something.
zh: 自修是进入创业的网关药物,因为你开始将生命视为一个可以优化的系统. 你开始相信,如果你不喜欢你的生活,你可以改变它。 既然大多数人的问题的根源是钱, 你停止等待加薪,开始建造一些东西。

text: The ultimate goal is getting paid to be yourself. To solve problems you can't help but solve. To study what you can't pull yourself away from. To eliminate any work that does not challenge you creatively.
zh: 最终目标是得到报酬成为你自己。 解决问题你只能解决. 以修习所不能离. 消除任何不以创造性方式挑战你的工作。

text: The easiest way to get ahead is to commit to a period of skill development. 6-12 months. Pure focus for 2-4 hours a day. Learning and building. Not just binge watching tutorials, but creating quality projects that you, others, or businesses could actually benefit from. But don't just let those projects sit around. Tell the world about them. See if people care enough to pay you. Fix what doesn't work until they do. That's it. That's what most people need to completely turn their life around.
zh: 前进的最简单方式是致力于一个技能发展时期。 6-12个月。 纯焦日照2-4小时. 学与建. 不仅在观看辅导课程上大吃一惊, 而且还创造了你,其他人,或企业可以实际获益的优质项目。 但不要让那些项目坐视不管 向世人说说他们. 看看人们是否在乎 支付你。 修无碍行等. 就是这样。 大多数人需要这样彻底地改变他们的生活.

text: Grab a notebook. Write out exactly what you want in your future, don't miss a detail. Then, break it down into goals. Decades, years, months, weeks, and days. Every day, write down the 3 levers you can pull to make actual progress. Then, forget about the goals and execute.
zh: 拿着笔记本 写出你未来的希望,不要错过细节。 然后,把它拆成目标。 十年,岁月,周,日. 每天,写下你能够拉的3个杠杆 以取得实际进展。 那就忘了目标,然后执行

text: I'll be creating a part 2 of this next week, but here's a good place to start: letters.thedankoe.com/p/how-to-think…
zh: 我下周将创作一个第二部分, 但这里有一个很好的开始地点: 字母。 com/p/如何思考... . . .

text: The best thing you can do in today's world is practice thinking. Very few people, especially smart people, do this. Snap yourself out of your automatic patterns and stretch your mind for once. This will only become more important as AI and social media distort reality.
zh: 今日世间最上妙法是修行思惟. 很少有人,特别是聪明人这样做。 振作起来,从自动模式中解放出来, 并舒展一下你的头脑。 随着AI和社交媒体歪曲现实,这一点将变得更加重要。

text: It'll be interesting to watch how it plays out that's for sure. I don't think there will "always be room" by any means, but I've seen quite a few people do well with tiny audiences. A tight knit little tribe can go a long way for those who think their only chances come from competing with the big dogs
zh: 看着它如何表现,肯定很有趣 我想无论如何都不会有"总是有空间",但我看到不少人用很小的观众来做得很好. 织织的部落对以为 唯一机会来自与大狗竞争的人来说 会有很长的路要走

text: I do think there can be a day that is an inflection point in your life that you think back on often as "the moment my life changed" - so yes, but I also attempt to make it very clear that many people may not reach that point just by reading the article. Much of the point of the article is that it can't always be forced That's the beauty of long form, its much more than a tweet or headline
zh: 我认为在你生活中, 会有一天是 一段不平的时期, 你经常回想起“我生命的瞬间” —— 是的,但我还是试图非常明确地说, 很多人可能无法仅仅读到文章, 文章的要点是不能总是强迫 长篇大论的美, 不只是推特或头条

text: In all seriousness it is a good take. That’s what some people need. But there’s also a reason why “just do the work” hasn’t solved all the world’s problems. My articles obviously don’t either, but I don’t think it’s a crime for me to find it interesting enough to write about and others to find it interesting enough to spend 20-30 min reading Elonbucks are nice too As is Coelho
zh: 认真的说,这是个好选择。 也有人需要这个。 也有理由「做工作」并未解决世界所有问题。 我的文章显然也不如此, 但我认为写文章和别人写文章也很有趣, 科埃略也一样

text: There are incredible new books, but I feel like books have become similar to social media. Authors are writing to hit NYT rather than for the love of the game - which is fine, I don't blame them at all - but when you write from that frame you make decisions to water things down, make them more attention grabbing for the sake of it, and strip the timelessness from it. Most (not all) are writing for a quick hit and a revenue spike so they can close speaking gigs, get on podcasts, etc. The book is more of a lever for more influence rather than something they think the world would benefit from.
zh: 有一些不可思议的新书,但我觉得书已经变得和社交媒体类似. 作者们写作是为了打击NYT,而不是为了游戏的爱——这很好,我完全不怪他们——但是当你从这个框架写作的时候,你决定把事情给水倒,让他们更加注意为它而抓,并剥离它的时间。 多数(并非全部)都在为快速打击和收入激增而写作,以便他们可以关闭演讲演出,上播客等. 这本书更是用来施加更多影响的工具 而不是他们认为世界会从中得到好处的东西

text: The good thing about X is that people are quick to call things out, much tighter feedback loop than other platforms I trust they'll be able to figure it out, but i also do think there will always be a market for that kind of content. Some people are very good at hijacking attention (not a bad skill to have) but I agree the value should be there to match
zh: X的优点是人们能很快地调出事物, 与其他平台相比, 更紧的反馈循环, 我相信他们能找出答案, 但我也觉得 总是会有市场 来购买这种内容。 有些人很擅长劫持注意力(但技巧不错) 但我同意应该有价值

text: $1 million dollar prizes aside, I think there's been a quiet hunger for articles. The pendulum swung too far in the direction of TikTok and endless scrolling and the internet has a chance to self-correct. I don't think the human brain was meant to be ripped in a thousand different directions as soon as you get on your phone. Too much chaos that has downstream affects on the quality of everyday life. Long form content allows you to focus on set of ideas. It brings order to the mind. You learn more, you feel better, and usually you're left with something you can actually apply to your life, even if it's just a better way of thinking. Especially with AI, we've gotten to the point where we think we want things as short as possible. We have this deranged desire to "get to the point" when the point is to sit with it. You can collect all of the tips and tricks you want but you're completely missing the context that allows you to turn those tips into results. A bullet list of actionable steps will do absolutely nothing for you. Pre-2015 books are the best option, but articles are a great step in what I think is the right direction.
zh: 奖金一百万 我想人们一直很渴望文章 网民有机会自我纠正。 我不认为人类大脑 被撕裂在千个不同方向 当你得到你的电话。 下游的混乱太多,影响了日常生活的质量。 长形式内容允许你专注于一套想法. 令众生心. 你学到更多,感觉更好,通常你留下的东西,实际上可以应用到自己的生活中,即使这只是更好的思维方式. 特别是人工智能 我们到达了我们认为我们想要的尽可能短的地步 我们有一个疯狂的愿望,"说到重点",当要坐到它的位置上. 你可以收集所有你想要的技巧和技巧 但你完全没有 允许你把这些技巧变成结果的背景。 一张可以行动的步骤的子弹清单绝对对你无济于事. 2015年前的书是最佳选择,但文章是我认为正确的方向的伟大一步.

text: The primary reason people don't achieve big goals is because they don't realize that the only source of truth is making mistakes. They soak up advice and theory, expecting it to be an exact match to their mind, experience, and situation just to fail once and give up completely.
zh: 人们没有实现大目标的主要原因是他们没有意识到唯一来源的真理是犯错误. 他们浸泡了建议和理论,期望这与他们的思维,经验和情况完全吻合,只是一次失败,彻底放弃.

text: You don't magically become focused. You practice it. You notice you're distracted and snap out of it, over and over again until it's second nature. Most people have not trained this muscle, and they are not anywhere close to as focused as they think they are.
zh: 你没有神奇地变得专注。 你来练习 你注意到你分心了 一次又一次地从中分心 直到它变成第二自然 大多数人没有训练这种肌肉,他们也没有像他们想象的那么集中。

text: be harder on yourself
zh: 自己更难受

text: Your life is determined by the amount of uncertainty you're willing to embrace.
zh: 你的生活是由你愿意接受的不确定性决定的

text: If you don't shamelessly promote your work, every day for the next 2-3 years, you will get stuck doing someone else's work until you realize that nobody is going to pay you if they have no idea who you are.
zh: 如果你不无耻地宣传你的工作, 接下来的两三年里, 你每天都会被困在别人的工作, 直到你意识到 没有人会付你,如果他们不知道你是谁。

text: If you have multiple interests, don't let anyone convince you that you should narrow your focus. You may be confused for a while, but if you stick it out, you will blow past everyone else.
zh: 如果你有多重兴趣,不要让任何人说服你,你应该缩小你的注意力. 你可能会被迷惑一段时间, 但如果你坚持它出去, 你会吹过其他人。

text: The deeper you fall into a rut the more fuel you have to launch out of it, but also the more excuses you have to stay the same. It is very important that you see through your own bullshit and choose the first option.
zh: 越深的地进入一个螺旋形, 你就越需要发射出燃料, 但也有更多的借口,你必须保持不变。 非常重要的是,你必须从你自己的废话中看出来,选择第一个选择。

text: The greatest focus hack is to know what you're working on, why you're working on it, when it needs to be done, and how exactly to get it done.
zh: 最集中的黑客是知道你在努力什么,为什么在努力,需要何时完成,以及如何确切地完成.

text: If you work so that you never have to work again, you'll be disappointed when retirement leaves you feeling empty. Find work that you would do after retirement as early as you can and you'll never feel the need to retire.
zh: 如果你工作到不再工作,退休后感觉空虚,你会失望. 尽快找到退休后会做的工作 永远也觉得需要退休

text: Don't try to learn the "best new skills." Build your own thing. Try to sell it. Fail over and over again. Learn what it means to adapt and iterate. Remove your dependency from others and hunt for your own survival. You will learn the exact skills you need, and you will learn them better than anyone else.
zh: 别试着学习"最佳新技能". 造自性之业. 试着卖掉它。 屡屡失败. 学其意以适应而弥足. 把你的依赖性从别人身上移除 并猎杀你自己的生存。 你会学会你需要的准确技能, 你会比任何人都更了解这些技能.

text: As someone who was against AI for a long time, AI is a skill just like any other, and the usefulness of AI increases as you get better at it. The first time you play a guitar, you literally can't do anything. You sound like shit. But you slowly get better. You can play more songs. You enjoy it so much more. We were made to think that AI would just solve all our problems and do everything for us, so people were disappointed when it didn't do those things, but if you actually try to learn it, then you will see how helpful it can be. That sounds like a cop out, but it's silly to think that AI is just going to solve all your problems out of the box. If you don't use it for an extended period of time then (as with every other skill) you will not be able to get the best results with it. It won't remove all of your work, or any of it for that matter if you don't want it to (I've been spending more time working, because it's fun), but it will give you a deeper, more enjoyable way of doing what you already do. You abstract out a layer from physical labor to mental labor. You spend more time in the realm of ideas and taste. It starts to feel more like a game or puzzle than it does "work." You have to be okay with moving from doing the manual grunt work of writing lines of code or prose to discerning whether or not it sounds like you want it to sound. I don't think anyone, aside from other writers who haven't detached from the labor, would argue that hiring a ghostwriter (i.e. James Patterson) is cheating. The end readers still love it. He is still in control of the plot, vision, and what actually gets published. Nobody would argue that the director of a film doesn't deserve praise because he is not manually filming it or building the set. There is going to be, and there already is, so much pushback on AI for valid reasons, but most of those reasons are psychological defense mechanisms tying you to how you've identified yourself with how you work. AI doesn't have to be a religion. You don't have to use it for everything. But I don't think it's a wise idea to willingly choose a side that is going to lose.
zh: 作为长期反对AI的人,AI就像其他任何一种技能一样,随着你越来越擅长,AI的实用性也随之提高. 第一次弹吉他时,你根本什么也做不了. 你听起来像狗屎。 但你慢慢地好起来。 你可以弹更多歌 你更喜欢它。 我们被逼以为AI只是解决我们所有的问题,为我们做一切事情,所以人们在它没有做那些事情的时候感到失望,但是如果你真的尝试去学习,那么你会看到它能有多大的帮助. 听起来像警察出局, 但认为AI只是解决你所有的问题 出柜是愚蠢的。 如果你长时间不使用它,那么(和所有其他技能一样),你将无法用它获得最佳效果。 它不会删除你的全部作品,或者任何你不想去的事物(我花了更多的时间工作,因为很有趣),但会给你一个更深,更令人愉快的方式做你已经做的事情. 你抽出一层 从体力劳动到精神劳动 你花更多的时间去思考和品味 它开始感觉更像是游戏或谜题 而不是"工作" 做手动的写字线或散文的工作 来判断它听起来是否像你想听的,你必须同意。 除了其他没有脱离劳动的作家外,我不认为有人会认为雇用一名鬼作家(即詹姆斯·帕特森)是作弊. 末日读者仍爱之. 他仍然控制着情节,视觉, 以及实际得到的出版。 没有人会认为电影导演不值得赞美 因为他没有手动拍摄 也没有建造剧集。 已经存在了, 这么多的推回AI 是有正当理由的, 但大多数这些原因都是心理辩护机制 把你与你如何认同自己是如何工作的。 AI不必是宗教. 亦不为一切用. 但我不认为这是明智的主意 自愿选择一方 将会失去。

text: If you want to avoid being replaced, stop worrying about which skills will be relevant 20 years from now. Nobody has a single clue what those are going to be. The world is changing so rapidly that you can’t even imagine what skills will be valuable in the future. Instead, you need to learn how to learn fast, because if you can become effective at any skill quicker than other people, it doesn’t matter what skill you learn. But that isn’t something you just sit down and study. You must step into the arena. You must learn by doing. You must build your own thing. Your own project. Your own product. Then you must share it with others to determine whether it’s valuable or not. Why? Because that forces you to learn efficiently. Everything you learn is directly applied to a meaningful project that allows you to not be dependent on an employer that requires you to learn a single skill that they will replace as soon as they can.
zh: 为了避免被替换,请不要担心20年后哪些技能将具有相关意义。 ⊿笵硂琌ぐ或 未来世界正在迅速变化, 而不是学习快速, 因为如果你比其他人更能有效地掌握任何技能, 也并非只是坐视不理。 你必须踏入竞技场。 你必须从实践中学习。 你必须自己造东西 你自己的项目。 你自己的产品。 也得与他人分享, 为什么? 因为那样你才能学得有效率 您所学的一切直接应用到一个有意义的项目上,它允许您不依赖一个需要您学习单一技能的雇主,他们将尽快地替代这些技能。

text: You don't achieve the life you want then become the "successful" person. You become the person first, which looks cringe until it becomes normal, and success eventually catches up to you. You choose the character you want to play, and only then do the right quests appear.
zh: 你没有实现你想要的生活 然后成为"成功"的人。 你成为第一人, 它看起来令人羡慕直到它变得正常, 成功最终赶上你。 你选择你想玩的角色,只有这样,才有正确的追求出现.

text: You easily quit your goals because there's nothing on the line. You don't have a vision for a better life that is so clear that your current life feels like losing if you don't reach it. Secretly most people don't actually want to achieve their goals. They want to impress others and get a dopamine hit from telling them their goals.
zh: 你轻易放弃了目标,因为线上没有东西 你没有更好的人生的远见, 如此清晰,你现在的生活 感觉就像失去,如果你达不到它。 秘密地,大多数人实际上并不想实现他们的目标. 他们想让别人印象深刻 让他们知道目标 就能得到多巴胺的打击

text: 7 more ideas and protocols on how to change: letters.thedankoe.com/p/how-to-fix-y…
zh: 7个关于如何改变的想法和协议: letters.thedankoe.com/p/how-to-fix-y...

text: The fastest way to change is to obsessively reflect on what you do every day and do not lie to yourself about what kind of life that is creating.
zh: 改变的最快方式是 沉迷于反思你每天做的事情 而不是欺骗自己 来描述你正在创造什么样的生活

text: Your life flips upside down when you realize that "who you are" is a program written by people who were also programmed, and unless you make the painful choice to rewrite that code, most of your thoughts and actions are not beneficial to your future, because they are not yours.
zh: 当你意识到"你是谁"是被编程的人所写的程式时,你的人生就颠倒了,除非你作出痛苦的选择来改写这个代码,否则你的大部分想法和行动对于你的未来没有好处,因为都不是你的.

text: You aren't where you want to be because you don't have the mind of the person who would be in that position. You're exactly where you should be for your level of development, and changing that is within your control. This goes deeper than you think: youtu.be/K8K09g9XR4s
zh: 你并不是想成为那种人 因为你没有那种人的想法 你正是适合你发展水平的地方 改变你所能控制的状况 这比你想的要深:yutu.be/K8K09g9XR4s

text: We're going through one of those historical shifts where there are 3 types of people: 1) People who cry and become violent about the fact that they can't "do what they've been doing" for the rest of their lives without getting left behind (avoid these people) 2) People who do nothing until they are forced to because they have to survive, but the penalty for starting late is so severe because everyone else is exponentially further ahead of them 3) People who stay curious, experiment, build, and figure out how to adopt the new way of doing things in a way thats useful (without attaching to the way things were done in the past) It has always been important that you choose option 3, but now is as good a time as ever.
zh: 经历了其中一种历史转变, 有三个类型:1) 哭哭闹闹的人, 2) 人不做任何事情,直到他们因为必须生存而被迫做,但因迟到的惩罚如此严厉,因为其他人都比他们更远 3) 人保持好奇,实验,建设,并想出如何用有用的方式(而不依附于过去的事情做的方式)来采取新的做事方式. 选择选项3一直很重要,但现在还是一个好时机。

text: Do everything in your power to never become helpless. It's a dangerous and lonely hole. Helpful people will stay as far away from you as they can because they know that you will not do anything with their help. Do not complain without being open to a solution. Do not complain if you know that you are going to do absolutely nothing to change. Do not expect anyone to give you anything. Nobody cares how bad your situation is if you clearly show that you do not want to escape it.
zh: 尽一切力不生无助. 险峻孤洞. 帮助者将尽可能远离你,因为他们知道你不会用他们的帮助做任何事情。 不得抱怨而不寻求解决办法。 如果你知道自己不会做任何改变的话,就不要抱怨。 别指望有人会给你任何东西 没人在乎你的处境有多糟 如果你清楚显示你不想逃避

text: You aren't where you want to be because you don't want to be there more than you want to be where you are. Your behavior is your confession. You say you want a better life (mostly for social approval), but when something more instant and pleasurable comes around, your actions show that you want that more.
zh: 汝非因缘不思议境界故. 你的行为就是忏悔 说想要更好的生活(主要是社会认可), 但当更迅速和愉快的事情出现时, 你的行为表明你更希望这样。

text: The future belongs to the high agency. Sounds cute. Everyone thinks they're high agency. But most people aren't. About 50% of the population do not have the cognitive development to be so. Here's why it's more important than ever and how to practice it: youtu.be/0XI_Xt0ci2Y
zh: 未来属于高官. 听起来很可爱。 大家都认为他们是高官 但大多数人不是。 大约50%的人口没有这种认知发展。 为何比以往更重要,

text: Your best ideas will never come from trying to have them. You must let the creative process play out automatically. You must read more, do more, experience more. Then you must give yourself time to do nothing because that's when your mind will put all the pieces together and shoot out an idea that may change everything.
zh: 你最好的主意永远也不会来自想要的东西。 您必须让创意进程自动运行 。 你必须多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多读多 那你必须给自己时间做任何事情 因为那时你的思想会把所有碎片都拼凑起来 并拿出一个可能改变一切的想法

text: When everyone has an advantage, it is no longer an advantage. When everyone can learn and create anything at the click of a button, your advantage comes from slowing down, focusing on your craft, doing the right things manually, and acquiring knowledge that is so specific to you that nobody can generate it with AI.
zh: 当每个人都有优势时,它就不再是优势。 当每个人都能在按钮的点击下学习和创建任何东西时,你的优势来自于减速,专注于你的工艺,手动做正确的事情,以及获得对你的特异性如此高的知识,以至于没有人能用AI生成.

text: Signs you're on the right track: Work energizes you Learning doesn't take effort Discipline doesn't need to be forced You think in decades, not days You're excited to wake up You relate to very few people, but the relationships you do form are irreplaceable.
zh: 证明你走在正确轨道上:工作激励你学习不费力 纪律不需要强迫 数十年来,你觉得醒来很兴奋 很少人能和你联系 但你们所形成的关系是不可替代的

text: If you can't articulate yourself, it will be difficult to get what you want in life. Customers won't care about your product. Employers won't give you a raise. Readers won't give you their attention. Try this: youtu.be/DKT6m_8vCkA
zh: 如果你不能表达自己, 很难得到你想要的生活。 顾客不会关心你的产品 雇主不会给你加薪的 读者不听. 尝试此操作: ytu. be/ DKT6m  8vCkA

text: When your days become too mundane, you need to change something fast. You need chaos. You need novelty. You need the experiences and ideas that inspire you to go on your next quest. Build something new. Rip apart your daily routine. Do anything but the same thing.
zh: 当你的日子变得太平淡的时候,你需要快速改变一些东西. 你需要混乱。 你需要新颖的。 你需要那些激励你继续你下一步探索的经验和想法。 盖新物. 拆开日常的日常活动。 做任何事情,但同样的事情。

text: If you aren't building something that leads to the life you want, even if it's "only" 30-60 minutes a day, you will by default stay in the same life you're in, building things that allow others to live the life they want. 30 minutes can, in fact, start the snowball.
zh: 如果你没有在建造一些能导致你想要的生活的东西, 即使每天只有"30到60分钟", 你默认会留在你所处的相同生活, 建造一些能让别人过上自己想要的生活的东西. 30分钟可以开始雪球了

text: If you're bored, create something. Anything. Software. Designs. Photos. Videos. Writing. A business. A body. A relationship. A mind. How can you not be excited to wake up when you have the ability to create.
zh: 如果你觉得无聊,就创造出一些东西。 什么都行,我爱她 软件。 设计. 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 视频. 写作。 生意,我说,我需要你帮忙 A business. 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 一种关系。 心智无边. 拥有创造能力时,怎么能够不兴奋地醒来.

text: You would learn faster if you actually started building the thing.
zh: 如果你真的开始建造它 你会学得更快

text: You can train your mind to achieve anything you want. Most of what you don't have is no more than a lack of skill, knowledge, action, and the logical sequence of steps it takes to achieve that thing. Deep down, everyone knows this, but their mind was trained by someone else to believe their potential is limited.
zh: 你可以训练你的头脑 实现任何你想要的。 你所没有的大多只是缺乏技能,知识,行动,以及实现该事物所要采取的逻辑步骤的顺序. 深处,每个人都知道这一点,但他们的头脑被别人训练,认为他们的潜力有限.

text: (There is also a normal chat interface... and a workspace for all your writing, footage, and files). eden.so
zh: (还有正常的聊天界面... 和所有写作,录像,文件的工作空间). 以达. 所以

text: Chat interfaces suck. And you need to copy paste between 10 tabs to do anything worthwhile. So we created a canvas for notes, files, YouTube videos, and chats that can branch off of each other. Eden is only open to the public through black friday weekend.
zh: 聊天界面很烂。 还要复制10个标签之间的粘贴,做任何值得做的事. 所以我们创建了一个画布, 记录,文件,YouTube视频, 和聊天,可以相互分枝。 伊甸园仅通过黑色星期五周末向公众开放.

text: Subtle reminder that you're supposed to feel overwhelmed. It means you're doing something new. You're taking a new path. You're learning. You're growing. You branched into the unknown, and that's admirable.
zh: 提醒你应该感到不堪重负 意思是你在做一些新事情 说不定你还得去 你走的是一条新路 汝等学道. 你长大了 你分化进未知的,这是令人钦佩的。

text: Being able to articulate yourself is a magnetic trait. And it's not that difficult to do, if you understand how to practice it. Here's 3 ways: letters.thedankoe.com/p/how-to-artic…
zh: 能够表达自己是一个磁性特征。 如果你明白如何练习,那就没那么难了。 这里有三种方式: 字母. thedankoe.com/p/如何对艺术...

text: Self-discipline isn't difficult, you're just distracted. The mind craves order. That’s why you feel out of control. Your attention is not focused on a singular vision for the future so potent that distractions can’t penetrate it. Feel deep into your situation. Realize where your life will end up if you keep going down this path. Become absolutely fed up with the lack of progress you are making. Use that negative energy to slingshot toward a meaningful goal you’ve been putting off. I know you can hear that voice that keeps nagging at you saying, you were meant for more than this. Discipline comes from clarity, not force. Discipline is a feature of identity. You aren’t disciplined because you aren’t the type of person who seamlessly does what they need to do. It isn’t difficult for a writer to sit down and write. It isn’t difficult for a bodybuilder to eat healthy. In fact, it’s difficult for them not to eat healthy. They hate it. They do it because that’s who they are. They don’t have options. They deeply understand the impact of being distracted from their goal. Success is automatic. If you want to be disciplined, stop trying to be. Instead, remove every single distraction that prevents you from being disciplined. Give yourself one option. Shut your mind off and execute.
zh: 自律并不难,你只是分心。 心欲令众生. 也因此你感到失控。 未来独具一格的愿景, 无法让注意力分散。 深入了解你的处境 知道你的生活会到哪里去 如果你继续走这条路。 完全受够了你没有取得进步。 利用这种负面能量, 向一个有意义的目标投弹, 我知道你听到那个声音 一直对你说 你本该为这个而死 纪律来自清晰,而不是武力。 纪律是身份特征之一。 而不是那种无缝的做事的人。 写作的作家坐下来写作并不困难。 健美的健美者吃得健康并不难。 也很难不吃健康的食物。 他们讨厌它。 因为他们就是这个样子。 也无从谈起。 他们深刻理解被分散注意力对其目标的影响。 成功是自动的。 欲受戒戒戒戒戒戒戒戒戒. 而不是去掉所有让你无法受纪律约束的分心 给自己一个选择。 闭上你的心,执行。

text: You can't improve what doesn't exist. The ideas in your head. The business you dream of building. The plans you keep putting off. All are worthless until you test them against reality. You're afraid of failure and glorify perfection when the opposite is what determines success.
zh: 无法改善不存在的东西. 脑海里的想法 你梦想的生意 你一直拖延的计划 这一切都是没用的,直到你测试他们与现实的对比。 你害怕失败, 美化完美 当相反的 才是决定成功。

text: Why the 40 hour workweek is a joke: youtu.be/mA0P-5_QvH8
zh: 为何40小时工作周是一个笑话:youtu.be/mA0P-5 QvH8

text: You only need 1 hour. 1 hour of building. 1 hour of writing. 1 hour of lifting. 1 hour of studying. 1 hour of anything but distracting yourself from the life you are trying to change. 1 hour feels like nothing until you look back 365 hours later and everything's changed.
zh: 你只需要一个小时 建楼1小时. 写作1小时. 抬起来一个小时 学习1小时. 一小时的一切都让你 无法改变你正在尝试的生活 一个小时的感觉就像什么都没有 直到你回头看365小时后 一切都变了

text: If you don't have something important to care about, you will naturally care about things that make you stupid, angry, and depressed.
zh: 如果你没有重要的事情需要关心,你自然会关心那些让你愚蠢,愤怒,沮丧的事情.

text: Most people's lives are determined by how they choose to cure their boredom.
zh: 大多数人的生活是由他们选择如何治愈自己的无聊决定的.

text: If you understood that you become what you read, watch, listen to, or the people you surround yourself with, you wouldn't be doing 90% of what you do.
zh: 如果你明白你成为你所读的, 看着,听, 或者你身边的人, 你就不会做90%你做的事。

text: It's pretty simple. Wake up early. Complete the single highest priority task. Avoid the destination society set for you at birth. Make giant mistakes. Mistakes are the launchpads toward your ideal life. Always be building a project. Youth is the greatest currency, it would be wise to invest it. If you wasted your youth, crying about it isn't going to speed up the process. You're supposed to feel overwhelmed, it means you're learning. Set aside 30 minutes daily for deliberate boredom (to unfuck your dopamine sensitivity). Walk more. Read more. Write more. Writing is how you practice thinking. Thinking is how you make better decisions. The outcome of your life is determined by the sum of your decisions. Force yourself to do something you've never done before, at least once a week. Learn something new. Talk to someone new. Book a one way plane ticket. Move into that place you can't afford. Because you need pressure. Real pressure. Launch yourself into the situation of your future self and see if you have what it takes.
zh: 很简单 早点起来 完成单一的最高优先任务。 避免你出生时的目的地社会。 犯大错 错误是发射板 对你的理想生活。 总是在建一个项目。 青年是最大的货币,投资是明智的。 如果你浪费了你的青春,哭着说这不会加快进程. 你应该感到不堪重负,这意味着你在学习。 每天留出30分钟 故意无聊(去你的多巴胺敏感度)。 更走动。 更读. 写多点. 写作是你们如何练习思考的。 思考是怎样做更好的决定的 人生的结局由你决定的总和决定. 强迫自己做你以前从未做过的事 至少每周一次 学点新东西 和新来的谈谈 订一张单程机票 搬进你买不起的地方 因为你需要压力。 真正的压力。 让你自己进入未来自我的境地 看看你是否拥有它所需要的东西

text: The glaring problem with schools is that a topic could take 1 week to learn, but it’s stretched across 3-4 months because that’s how long the course must be. Even worse, the learning doesn’t involve doing, so most people don’t learn a thing until they graduate.
zh: 学校的显著问题是,一个话题需要一周的时间才能学习,但这个话题需要3-4个月的时间,因为课程必须这样长。 更糟糕的是,学习并不涉及做,所以大多数人在毕业之前都什么也学不到。

text: You don't need to focus harder. Focus is the byproduct of a lack of distractions. You simply need to be in the right environment (mentally and physically) and have a clear set of tasks that you have the knowledge and skill to execute. Ignore everything but that.
zh: 你不需要更集中的注意力。 焦点是缺乏分心的副产品. 你只需要在正确的环境中(精神上和身体上),拥有一套你有知识和技能可以执行的清晰的任务. 忽略一切,但那个。

text: The best way to stand out as a creator, in my eyes, is to take a weird topic you're passionate about and make it palatable to a larger audience. Don't follow the trends, but take the principles of human nature that make the trends work and apply them to your personal interests.
zh: 以创作者的身份出众的最佳方式,在我眼中,就是拿一个你充满激情的怪异话题,让更多的观众喜欢. 不要跟随趋势,而是把使趋势发挥作用的人性原则应用到个人利益上.

text: I don’t know of a single successful person who doesn’t have the skill of figuring it out. They don’t need to ask how to do something, because they deeply understand that they will have to fail, change direction, fail again, and repeat that process even if someone gave them the exact roadmap.
zh: 也没有任何成功者能掌握自己的能力。 因为他们深知自己必须失败、改变方向、再次失败, 并重复这个过程, 即使有人给他们精确的路线图。

text: The worst life is to wake up and have nothing you want to do. The best life is not being able to stop yourself from launching out of bed, working on a project that's important to you, and having so much clarity that stress and worry can't even find their way into your brain.
zh: 最糟糕的生活是醒来,你却无所欲为. 最好的生活是无法阻止自己从床上跳出来, 做一个对你很重要的项目, 并且有如此清晰的清晰, 压力和担心甚至无法进入你的大脑。

text: Learn how to do it yourself before you let AI do it for you. If you don't, you will not understand why it was done that way, why it got results, or why it didn't. You will become more and more dependent on a tool rather than your own creative ability.
zh: 学会自己做,然后让AI为你做。 否则,你将无法理解为什么这样做,为什么结果,或者为什么没有结果。 你会越来越依赖一种工具 而不是你自己的创造力

text: If you struggle to think clearly, or struggle to generate big creative ideas, give yourself permission to fuck off for 3 days. No work. No laptop. Just sit around. Discover what it’s like to feel bored again. Have a pen and paper ready.
zh: 如果你挣扎于清晰思考,或努力产生巨大的创意,那么就允许自己滚蛋三天. 没有工作。 没有笔记本电脑。 随便坐 发现再次感到无聊的感觉。 准备好笔和纸吧

text: You don’t need to dream big. You need to imagine a life so depressing that the only option in your mind is to achieve big.
zh: 也不必做大梦。 你需要想象一个如此令人沮丧的生活 让你心中唯一的选择就是实现大。

text: Rare traits I’ve noticed in people who go on to do great things: 1. They were expected by their parents to enter a specific career, and it felt extremely limiting 2. They could clearly see that others achieved great things even when the odds were stacked against them, so they rightfully assumed they could too 3. They naturally questioned what was possible, and when teachers, family, or friends couldn’t give them good answers, they took it upon themselves to find them
zh: 也只是一些在做伟大事情的人身上发现的少许特征: 1. 父母期望她们进入某一职业,认为这限制了2个职业。 他们可以清楚地看到,其他人即使面临重重困难,也取得了伟大的成就,因此他们正确地认为他们可能太过3。 他们自然质疑什么是可能的,当教师、家人或朋友无法给出好答案时,他们自己就找了他们。

text: I love entrepreneurship because it allows you to apply anything you learn. At school, you’re told what to learn. At a job, you’re told what to work on, and you only learn until you are good enough to do the job (unless you self-educate out of genuine interest, but being honest, an abysmally low amount of people do that, because those who do realize that in order to learn they must grow, and you can’t grow in someone else’s box). When you do your own thing, the only way to survive is to do something you’ve never done before, which requires you to learn what can’t be taught in school.
zh: 我喜欢创业,因为它让你能够应用你学到的一切。 在学校,人们会告诉你该学什么。 工作上,人们会告诉你该做什么,你只学会直到你做好工作(除非你出于真正的兴趣自我教育,但诚实地说,是极低的人这样做,因为那些意识到为了学习他们必须成长,而你不能在别人的盒子里成长). 需要你学习无法在学校教的。

text: How to know if you need to make a big change: You stay up late, every night, because you feel like you haven't gotten enough out of the day.
zh: 如何知道,如果你需要做出一个大改变: 你每天晚上都睡着 因为你觉得白天没睡够

text: When you combine the internet (access to opportunity), social media (access to people), and AI (access to intelligence) you give high agency people the ability to achieve more, faster, and low agency people a reason to complain, more.
zh: 网络、社交媒体(接触民众)和AI(获取情报)的结合, 让高机构人员有能力实现更多、更快、低机构人员的投诉。

text: Your potential would substantially increase if you understood that the limits of your perspective are not the limits of what's possible.
zh: 如果你明白你的观点的局限性 不是可能的极限 你的潜力就会大大增加

text: The obvious trap here is mental masturbation. This probably won't work if you aren't already intensely committed to building out your vision for the future. That being said, more work isn't always the answer. You need to unfuck your psychology.
zh: 这里明显的陷阱是精神自慰. 如果你还没有大力致力于 建立你对未来的展望,这也许不会成功。 说到这个,更多的工作并不总是答案。 你得去你妈的心理

text: A pattern I’ve noticed in successful people - that didn’t sacrifice their life for success - is that they physically worked very little, yet people see them as hard workers. Mentally, they were always thinking, plotting, and scheming. They worked in their mind. And once they were clear on their idea, they executed with speed that others couldn’t compete with.
zh: 也有些人认为自己是勤奋的工人。 精神上,他们总是在思考,策划,和阴谋。 他们的思维很有效 也因此无法与他人竞争。

text: The best way to learn something is to write about it, in public, but only if you care about learning. When you write, you are forced to organize thoughts into words. When you teach someone else, you feel obligated to understand the topic.
zh: 学习东西的最好方法就是在公众场合写作,但只有在关心学习时才会写作. 写作时,被迫将思想组织成文字. 当你教别人的时候,你觉得有义务理解这个话题.

text: You need to go through an extended period of investing their time into self education and skill acquisition before you can expect any kind of unaverage success. The sooner you stop putting off the 1-3 years of trial and error the better.
zh: 需要花很长时间 来完成自我教育和技能培养 才能期望任何不平均的成功 越快停止1-3年的试运行 越快越好

text: One of the greatest feelings is seeing your faith in yourself pay off. To keep experimenting even when nothing has yet gotten results. To see the first bit of effort turn into success. Once you get that taste you can never go back to your old life.
zh: 最大的感觉之一是 看到你对自己的信心有所回报 即使没有结果,也要继续试验 以观初一品功德成. 一旦你得到那种味道 你永远无法回到你以前的生活。

text: It’s crazy how you can just learn anything you want and become so good people can’t ignore you if you stop getting distracted.
zh: 也成为好人, 不再分心,

text: If you want to do something that has never been done before, don't choose a career. Choose a great work. A mission. Something that you can dedicate decades of your life to. People won't believe in you, and it may feel lonely at times, but for some, it is the only life worth living.
zh: 如果你想做以前从未做过的事,就不要选择事业. 选一棒功. 任务 某种你可以奉献 几十年的生命。 人们不会相信你,有时可能感到孤独,但对于有些人来说,这是唯一值得生活的生命.

text: Why it's so hard to change your life: letters.thedankoe.com/p/you-wont-be-…
zh: 为什么改变你的生活这么难: letters.thedankoe.com/p/you-wont-be-.

text: Sometimes, you need to become so disgusted with yourself, your life, or your situation that you can’t help but change. The work you didn’t care about doing in the first place. The relationships that have grown to be repetitive and stale. The body that doesn’t feel electric anymore. There’s a big difference between being hard on yourself and being so easy on yourself that it becomes the hardest thing in the world. Nobody is going to do it for you. In fact, most people don’t want you to change. Don’t focus on the positive. Focus on the negative, then ruthlessly redirect that energy toward a meaningful future.
zh: 也得改变自己的处境。 一开始你并不在乎的工作。 关系已经发展到重复和僵化。 身体不再有电感。 与自我的艰难与容易, 没人会为你做这个的 许多人不希望你改变。 也不要关注正面。 专注于消极的,然后无情地将能量转向一个有意义的未来。

text: You need 4 habits. One that builds your mind One that builds your body One that builds your money One that builds your relationships Here's how to maximize all domains of your life: youtu.be/SL5lwg1wYaE
zh: 你需要4个习惯。 一个让你心智更加清醒的 让你身体健壮的 一个能赚你钱的人 一个建立你的关系 以下是如何实现你生活所有领域的最大化: yetu.be/SL5lwg1wYaE

text: If you were secretly recorded during your next "deep work" session and had to watch it after, you'd probably find that 90% of the work you're doing is unnecessary, and most of the needle-moving tasks could be done in 2-3 hours, maximum.
zh: 如果你在下一次"深活"会话中被秘密记录,之后不得不观看,你可能会发现你90%的工作是不必要的,大部分的针移任务可以在2-3小时之内完成,最多.

text: Wrote a dopamine detox letter: letters.thedankoe.com/p/a-dopamine-d…
zh: 写一个多巴胺解毒信:字母.thedankoe.com/p/a-dopamine-d...

text: Your brain thinks normal life is boring because it's gotten used to constant euphoria. You see more on your phone in 30 minutes than people used to in their entire lives. You eat foods that are so sweet that, once you stop, they become disgusting. If you dropped any and all sources of instant pleasure for the next 30 days, you'd feel like a child again. Life would feel electric.
zh: 你的大脑认为正常的生活很无聊,因为它已经习惯了不断的兴奋. 30分钟内你手机上看到的比人们一生中看到的要多 你吃的食物是如此甜美, 一旦停止,它们变得恶心。 如果你在接下来的30天里 放弃了任何和所有即时的快乐来源 你会再次感觉自己像个孩子 生活会觉得电动。

text: You don't have ideas because you don't read. You can't articulate ideas because you don't write. You can't leverage ideas because you don't build. You feel bored, anxious, or overwhelmed because your mind wants ideas to flow but you won't let them.
zh: 你没有想法是因为你不读书。 你无法表达想法 因为你不写。 你不能利用想法 因为你不建设。 你感到无聊、焦虑或不知所措 因为你的头脑想流出一些想法 但你不会让他们得逞

text: It's liberating once you realize that the mind can be trained to do anything you want. That most of what you lack can be changed with knowledge, skill, and a logical sequence of steps that victims will attempt to convince you don't exist.
zh: 一旦你意识到 思想可以被训练成任何你想做的事, 就会解放 大部分你缺少的东西都可以用知识,技巧, 和逻辑顺序来改变, 受害者会试图说服你不存在。

text: You're not struggling with productivity. You're struggling with not having a project worth being productive for. If you were working on the right thing, you wouldn't be able to stop thinking, working, obsessing.
zh: 你没有在生产力方面挣扎 你正在努力争取一个值得我们做的项目 如果你在做正确的事情, 你就不会停止思考,工作,迷恋。

text: You're allowed to be motivated by money. Scratch that, you are motivated by money. But you hide it because your parents, teachers, and peers convinced you it was a bad thing to pursue. You work, right now, for money, to survive. Don't apologize for wanting to live well.
zh: 允许你以金钱为动机 刮一下,你是为了钱 但你隐瞒了它,因为你的父母,老师, 和同伴说服你,这是一件坏事 追求。 你现在工作是为了钱 是为了生存 不想过上好日子就别道歉了

text: The article letters.thedankoe.com/p/how-to-becom…
zh: 文章字母. thedankoe.com/p/如何将becom. .

text: A bit off brand, but I've been experimenting with peptides for a year or two now. Peptides are to health what Bitcoin is to finance and what AI is to business. I tried them for the anti-aging, longevity, and healing benefits, but soon found that I was the most energetic and focused I'd ever been. I wrote an article about it:
zh: 有点不合适 但我已经用Peptides做了一两年的实验 佩皮迪士是健康, 比特币是 融资和AI是 商业。 我尝试过抗衰老,长寿,疗伤的疗效,但很快发现我是我曾经最精力充沛,最专注的. 我写了一篇关于它的文章:

text: One thing that would be super helpful and I’m not sure if this is a bug on my end: The search doesn’t seem to work at all when trying to search through my own tweets (or others). It’s helpful for being able to see 10 or so of my best tweets, but that’s all I can really use it for haha
zh: 可能还有一点特别的帮助, 搜索似乎毫无效果, 也因此能透过推特,

text: Your past is just an image in your mind. But you decide to live as if that image were your reality. Even worse, you Photoshop that image. You make it worse than it was and it slowly dims the future image you could create.
zh: 你的过去只是你心中的一幅画 但你决定活得像那幅画 就是你的现实 更糟的是,你把图像处理掉 你让它比以前更糟, 它慢慢地淡化了你能够创造的未来形象。

text: The matrix is real. Kinda like the movie. It's hiding in plain sight. It's a part of human nature. We've built ourselves into it. By the time we turn 18, we're clones of everyone else. Here's how to escape and take back control of your mind: youtu.be/JeaBetH8UUc
zh: 矩阵是真实的。 有点像电影 隐居于明堂. 谓人性之分. 我们已把自己融入其中了 到了18岁,我们是其他人的克隆人。 如何逃脱并夺回对心灵的控制:youtu.be/JeaBetH8 铀浓缩系数

text: If you're trying to build your own thing, you need to focus on the fact that nobody knows you exist and work until that's not the case. Your logo, LLC formation, and website are not going to make people know you exist.
zh: 如果你想自己造东西,你需要专注于没有人知道你的存在,并且工作到没有做到这一点. 你的标志,专卖局长的组建,和网站不会让人们知道你的存在.

text: Most people haven't practiced thinking. When you talk to them, you aren't talking to them. You're talking to the TV. Social media. Their parents. Their teachers. Their insecurities and trauma. It's very robotic. The definition of inauthentic. Disconnect and think more.
zh: 大多数人没有练习过思考。 当你和他们说话时,你不会和他们说话的. 你在和电视说话 社交媒体. 他们的父母。 他们的老师 它们的不安全和创伤。 机器人很厉害 非正统的定义. 断开再想.

text: The problem isn't that you don't know what to work on. It's that you haven't tried anything outside of what your parents, teachers, and boss have assigned to you. You haven't experimented with life to the point of finding that one thing you can't help but work on.
zh: 问题不在于你不知道该做什么 就是你没有尝试过 除了你父母、老师和老板给你分配的外的任何事 你还没有尝试过生命 以至于发现一件事 你无法不努力地工作。

text: My biggest fear is waking up to live the same life as everyone else over and over again. Doing work I don't care about for people I don't care about. Never understanding, always reacting. No energy or time to think, plan, build, or change. Stuck on the surface as decades pass by.
zh: 我最大的恐惧是醒来后 与其他人一样 过着与众不同的生活 工作我不在乎那些我不在乎的人 工作我不在乎那些我在乎的人 永远不能理解,总是反应 没有任何精力或时间来思考、计划、建造或改变。 沉积在地表上, 几十年过去了。

text: How to master any skill fast: - Stop studying - Outline a project - Start building it - Hit a roadblock - Research how to overcome it - Repeat until completed Most people get trapped in tutorial hell and have nothing to show for years of "learning."
zh: 如何快速掌握任何技能: - 停止学习 - 大纲项目 - 开始建设 - 击出路障 - 如何克服它 - 重复到完成 大多数人被困在教程地狱里,在多年的"学习"中没有任何东西可以展示.

text: Building a project will teach you more than watching a tutorial ever will.
zh: 建一个项目会教给你更多的东西 比看一个教程永远。

text: Do whatever you want. When you do, you will make mistakes. At that point, if you are smart, you no longer want it, and begin to slowly course correct toward what you really want. If you always do what others want for you, you will never discover what you want.
zh: 随所欲行. 当你这样做,你会犯错误。 那时,如果你聪明,你不再需要它, 开始慢慢地正确走向你真正想要的。 如果你总是为别人做你想要的事, 你永远不会发现你想要的。

text: You solve a problem by obsessing over it (without letting it destroy your mind). Any problem, huge or small, can eventually be solved if you sit with it long enough. When a problem is your lens for reality, you subconsciously pick up information and piece together the solution.
zh: 你通过沉迷于它来解决一个问题(而不让它破坏你的心灵). 任何问题,无论大小,只要坐得够久,最终都能解决. 当一个问题是你的镜头 现实,你潜意识中 收集信息 并拼凑解答。

text: The solution to imposter syndrome is to produce so much that your body of work speaks for itself.
zh: 假冒综合症的解决方法是产生这么多,以至于你的工作体能说明问题.

text: A meaningful goal + clarity on how to achieve it + a real deadline closing in = the greatest nootropic stack known to man
zh: 有意义的目标 + 明确如何实现 + 真正的最后期限 关闭在 = 人类已知的最古老的 新热带堆栈

text: You can become unrecognizable in 5 years
zh: 五年后你就会变得无法辨认

text: Here's the psychology of why it's so hard to change and what to do about it: letters.thedankoe.com/p/you-wont-be-…
zh: 如下心理学中,为什么改变如此困难,以及对此如何做: 字母. the: the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the, the,dan, the,e,dan,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,e,dan,dan,dan,dan,dan,dan,be,be,dan,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be,be.

text: You're distracted because you don't have responsibilities, priorities, or goals so important that you have no choice but to focus on them. You're afraid to commit so you drown in options.
zh: 你分心是因为你没有责任, 没有优先级, 或目标如此重要, 你别无选择, 你害怕犯罪 所以会淹死在选择中

text: You don't set a goal to achieve something, you set a goal to become someone. Most people fail because they want the reward without the change that comes with it. If you have a burning desire to change, you'll achieve any goal you want.
zh: 你没有设定目标去实现某些目标, 你设定了一个目标去成为某人. 大多数人都失败了,因为他们想要的奖励没有随之而来的改变. 如果你有强烈的改变欲望,你就会实现任何你想要的目标.

text: The most powerful habit you can adopt: Using the first hour of your day to build, write, or produce something of value.
zh: 使用你每天的第一个小时来构建、写作或制作有价值的东西。

text: If you don't have anyone to talk about your interests and passions with, start writing on the internet and you'll find them.
zh: 如果你没有任何人来谈论你的兴趣和激情, 开始在互联网上写作,你会找到他们。

text: Everyone needs to learn about survival. Humans are unique because (unlike animals) we survive on the psychological level. We feel threatened when our beliefs, values, ideas, or worldview are challenged. We literally feel the fear and pain even when nothing is there.
zh: 人人得学求生. 人类之所以独一无二,是因为(与动物不同)我们是在心理层面上生存的。 当我们的信仰、价值观、思想或世界观受到挑战时,我们感到威胁。 我们确实感受到了恐惧和痛苦,即使那里什么都没有。

text: You aren't where you want to be because you don't have the mind of the person who would be in that position. You don't have the knowledge, skill, or experience, obviously. Changing that is in your control.
zh: 你并不是想成为那种人 因为你没有那种人的想法 显然你没有知识、技能或经验。 改变这是你控制。

text: If you're lost, start solving problems. The problems that are staring you straight in the face. Your fitness, health, finances, mindset, work, or anything preventing you from having control over your life. Experiment until you become obsessed and watch your life change.
zh: 如果你迷路了,就开始解决问题. 问题正直盯着你的脸。 你的健身、健康、财政、思维、工作,或者任何妨碍你控制你生活的东西。 实验直到你变得痴迷 并看你的生活改变。

text: You don't need experience to start because you only gain experience by starting.
zh: 你不需要经验开始 因为你只有从开始才获得经验

text: A bit of info about us: Our mission is to pioneer the future of creative work and create a tool that allows people to be more creative with AI, not less. Eden is a knowledge management app centered around 3 key features: 1) Capture anything with one shortcut – notes, links, files, videos, etc. 2) Remember everything with visual search – search for exact frames or quotes within videos, PDFs, or any of your files. 3) Do your best creative work on an intelligent canvas – visually organize any file, connect to and branch between AI chats in one view, and experience a new way to build out creative projects. Previously we built Kortex, but due to a few problems with how we built it (full story for later, Kortex users have already received multiple updates about this), we decided to rebuild the app from scratch. Luckily, our plan has gone according to plan, and over the past few months we've built something that blows the last version of Kortex out of the water. We have the base of the mobile app built, but need another dev so we can stop splitting focus. If you'd like to work with a great team and myself in making Eden a household name for creatives, apply above.
zh: 对我们的一点信息:我们的使命是开拓创作工作的未来,并创建一个工具,让人们能与AI相比更有创意,而不是更少. Eden是一个知识管理应用软件, 1) 以一个快捷键捕捉任何东西 — 注释、链接、文件、视频等。 2) 用视觉搜索记起一切 — 搜索视频、PDF或任何文件的精确帧或引文。 3) 在智能画布上做你最好的创作工作 — 视觉上组织任何文件,在一种观点中连接和分机AI聊天,并体验到一种构建创意项目的新方式. 此前我们建造了Kortex,但由于我们建造它时遇到一些问题(后来的完整故事,Kortex用户已经收到了有关这个事件的多个更新),我们决定从零开始重建该应用. 幸运的是,我们的计划已经按照计划进行, 在过去几个月里,我们建造了一种东西,把Kortex的最后一个版本从水中吹走. 我们已建立了移动应用程序的基地, 但需要另一个dev 这样我们就可以停止分裂焦点。 如果你想和伟大的团队和我合作 使伊甸园成为创意的家喻户晓,请在上方申请.

text: Soft requirements / skills / experience: - Pref based in Toronto - Typescript + bun monorepo - Experience w microservices - Experience w react-native Link to short application: docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAI…
zh: 软要求/技能/经验: - Pref基于多伦多 - Typescript + bun monorepo - 经验w microservices - 经验w – 反应-内在链接与短应用程序:docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAI...

text: We're looking for a mobile developer for the next iteration of Kortex (called Eden, more on that later). Please pass the link below around if you know someone who'd be a good fit. Competitive pay, potential equity, preferably startup-pilled:
zh: 我们为Kortex的下一个迭代(称为Eden,以后更多关于这个)寻找移动开发商. 请把链接传递到下面 如果你知道谁会是一个好合身。 竞争性薪酬、潜在股本,最好是启动股:

text: When it's time to work, work. When it's time to rest, rest. Most people are always thinking about rest while working and work while resting. An empty life is one lived in your head.
zh: 时值工夫. 当其休歇时,休息. 大多数人总是在休息时考虑休息,在休息时考虑工作和工作. 空虚的生命就是在你的脑海中活下来的

text: Formal education serves society's goals, self-education serves your goals. One is assigned to you, the other is chosen by you. One demands attendance, the other requires curiosity. The difference between the two can drastically change the outcome of your mind and life.
zh: 正规教育服务于社会目标,自我教育服务于你的目标。 一个是给你的,另一个是给你选择的 其中一个需要出席,另一个需要好奇心。 两者的区别会大大改变你的思想和生活的结果.

text: Most people suck at speaking, being social, and relationships because they see people as the static image they've constructed in their head rather than human beings who are just as stupid as you.
zh: 大多数人都不喜欢说话, 社交, 和关系,因为他们把人看作是 他们所构建的静态形象 在他们的脑海,而不是像你们一样愚蠢的人。

text: Personally, I have never felt so close to being radicalized or such a desire in the pit of my stomach to make sure she feels safe. She will be getting both, and I'll need a few gym sessions to think clearly. I'd like to think that social media surfaces the worst of people right now, but even just one video out of thousands celebrating Charlie's death makes me fucking sick. There should be zero.
zh: 我个人从未感到如此接近激进 或胃的深处有如此渴望 确保她感到安全 她会得到两者, 我需要一些健身课 清楚思考。 我想社交媒体是目前最糟糕的媒体 但即使是千人中只有一段 庆祝查理的死 让我恶心 应该没有

text: My innocent little wife now wants a gun + classes for self protection and is deeply questioning the mental health of much of the population. She has never been the type to get wrapped up in this stuff. Make of this what you will. Something changed yesterday.
zh: 我无辜的小老婆现在想要一个枪+课来自我保护,并且正在深深质疑大部分人口的精神健康. 她从来不是那种被这玩意包起来的人 随你便吧 昨天有些事情变了

text: Do what you love, but study psychology, human behavior, and persuasion so people actually care about what you do enough to pay you for it.
zh: 做你喜欢的事,但学习心理学,人类行为, 和说服, 所以人们实际上关心你做什么 足以支付你的费用。

text: Procrastination is nature's way of telling you that you aren't working on the right thing.
zh: 拖延是大自然告诉你 你没有在做正确的事情的方法

text: Short form to attract minds. Long form to change minds.
zh: 短形以吸引众智. 长形以变化智.

text: Build your own thing for the first hour every day. See where it takes you. Commit to it. Produce something of value that you weren't forced to produce to pay the bills or because your parents or teachers told you to. Your future self is begging you and I know you hear that voice.
zh: 每日第一小时自建自用. 看看你的车路 承诺吧 生产一些你没有被迫生产来支付账单的有价值的东西 或者因为你父母或老师叫你做的 你的未来是求你 我知道你听到那个声音

text: Learn a lot of stuff. Build a lot of stuff. Identify problems. Solve them. Discover new paths. Take them. Reflect on the journey. Do it better the next time. Don't get distracted. Don't turn on autopilot. Take risks. Fail until you can afford to succeed. That's it.
zh: 学诸法. 积集众生. 找出问题 解决他们。 发现新路径. 拿着 思考一下旅程 下次再做比较好 别分心了 别开自动驾驶机 冒险吧 失败直到你能够承受成功。 就是这样。

text: The internet is slowly dying. AI content. TikTok enshittification. Posting solely to milk as much dopamine and attention as possible. But there's a subset of people who are getting tired of it: youtu.be/ni04Zp7mUMM
zh: 互联网正在慢慢消亡。 AI内容. 帝克托克即兴. 寄给牛奶的多巴胺 并尽可能关注。 但有一群人对此感到厌倦:Youtu.be/ni04Zp7mumM.

text: The answer to 99% of your questions is "try it"
zh: 回答你99%的问题是"试一下"

text: If you feel lost, write down: 1) Who you want to become 2) Who you don't want to become 3) The 3-5 things you can do today You're lost because you don't have a direction to work toward, an enemy to work away from, and tasks that move the needle toward your goals.
zh: 如果你觉得迷路,写下:1)你想成为谁 2)你不想成为谁 3)你今天可以做3-5的事情 你迷失了 因为你没有方向去工作 一个敌人去工作 以及任务 使针向目标前进

text: You don't find what you're passionate about. You create it. You build it. You invest energy into a goal. You become great at something. You stick with it whether you feel like it or not. You aren't going to be passionate about something you have zero experience in.
zh: 你找不到你对什么充满激情 你创造它。 你来建造它。 你投入能量去达到目标 你变得很出色 无论你是否喜欢,你都要坚持下去 你不会对一些你没有经验的东西充满激情的

text: Most people aren't afraid to fail. They're afraid to succeed because that would require them to change. It would require them to become the person who was capable of success. It would require them to let go of the pleasures they are silently addicted to.
zh: 大多数人不怕失败. 他们害怕成功,因为那需要他们改变。 它将要求他们成为能够取得成功的人。 这需要他们放弃他们默默沉迷的乐趣。

text: Pay close attention to the times when you can't stop having ideas, thinking about a project, or obsessively taking notes. You're being pulled into a new chapter of life and the worst thing you can do is let that spark die out.
zh: 密切注意那些无法停止思想,思考某个项目,或者执着地做笔记的时代. 你被拉进生命的新篇章 和最糟糕的是你能够做的是 让火花熄灭。

text: You used to want it. You used to be hungry. You used to love the challenge. But then you got soft. Life became dull. You became numb. At some point you're going to have to get so sick of it that your spark comes back.
zh: 你以前想吃这个的 你以前饿了 你以前很喜欢挑战 但你却变得软弱 生活变得无聊。 你变得麻木了 某时你不得不厌倦它 以至于你的火花又回来了。

text: Everyone is playing one of three money games: - Survival – Make enough to not die. Money as master. - Success – Make enough to matter. Money as scoreboard. - Sacred – Make enough to authentically create. Money as energy. Money is evil at level 1. Money is good at level 3.
zh: 所有人都在玩三个金钱游戏之一: 生存 足够不死 钱为主. -成功 -足够重要了 钱作为记分牌. - 神圣的 - 足够真实的创造。 金钱如能量. 钱是邪恶的,在一级。 钱在3级是好的.

text: Just solve problems. Your problems first. Then others' problems. That's how you learn, become valuable, become high-agency, earn an income, practice creativity, and improve the quality of your mind, body, spirit, finances, relationships. Get distracted and life starts to crumble.
zh: 解决好问题 先解决你的问题吧 别人的问题。 这就是你学习的方式,变得有价值,成为高机构,赚取收入,实践创造力,提高你的思想,身体,精神,财务,关系的质量. 分心,生活开始崩溃。

text: The desire to succeed is more important than knowing how to succeed because the person with the strongest desire will figure it out no matter the cost.
zh: 成功的愿望比知道如何成功更重要,因为最强烈的愿望的人无论付出多少代价都会明白.

text: Full knowledge base with scientific validation, historical context, civilizational impact, assessment questions and practices, and deep nuances Skim through it like a glossary or feed to AI and talk about any topic / problem letters.thedankoe.com/p/a-complete-k…
zh: 具备科学验证、历史背景、文明影响、评估问题和做法以及深层细微差别的知识库,通过它,如词汇表或资料提供给大赦国际,并讨论任何专题/问题信。 the( the) the( the)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(k)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(k)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(k)e)e(e)e(k)e(k)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)e(e)k(k)e(k)e)e(k)-(k(k)e)e(k)e(k)e(k)e(k)e(k)e(k)e(e)e(k)e)(k)e(k)(k

text: The longer you spend studying business, health, relationships, etc the more you see that everything is the wrong thing to do yet everything is the right thing to do until you reach the point where you ignore it all and figure it out for yourself.
zh: 你越是费时间研究生意,健康,关系等等, 你就越看到,一切事情都是错的, 事情都是正确的事情,直到你完全忽略这一切,自己去想。

text: My editor has hit that point where he finally gets it. Started writing a fiction book and can’t stop thinking about it. Got on a real training program and dialed in his diet. Complete mentality and energy shift. Video games became boring overnight. Fell back in love with life.
zh: 我的编辑终于到了他终于能拿到的地步 开始写一本小说, 参加真正的训练计划,并拨打他的饮食。 彻底的心态和能量转移。 电子游戏一夜之间变得无聊. 爱上了生活

text: Most people have no clue how bad they feel because that’s “normal” to them. Train, walk, and eat well for 2 weeks (vertical diet for simplicity) and your mind will be blown by how much different you feel.
zh: 大多数人不知道自己有多难过, 火车、步行、吃好两周(垂直饮食简单),

text: The best thing about a writing habit is that it makes you realize how stupid you really are. Most people think they have clear ideas, but since they’ve never written them out in physical space, they’ve never thoroughly examined their thinking.
zh: 写作习惯的最好之处在于 它让你意识到自己有多蠢 许多人认为他们有明确的想法,

text: Read this to master mind, body, spirit, money: letters.thedankoe.com/p/human-30-a-m…
zh: 读此以通心术,体,灵,钱:字母. thedankoe.com/p/human-30-a-m...

text: Master your mind. Read more. Consume less. Produce more. React less. Focus intensely on one thing. Zoom out and let go of everything. Train. Recover. Your mind is the operating system for reality. If you don't program it, others will, and that never ends well.
zh: 师作么生会. 更读. 少说些 生产更多。 反应少一点 专心于一件事 放大一切解脱门. 火车。 复还. 你的思想是现实的操作系统 如果你不编程,其他人会的, 永远也不会好结束。

text: I created a prompt to find your Metatype, map your development across all quadrants, and identify the next core problem in your life: letters.thedankoe.com/p/prompt-human…
zh: 我创建了一个快速找到您的Metatype, 绘制您在所有四重体上的开发图, 并识别您生活中的下一个核心问题: letters.thedankoe.com/p/promst-human...

text: Since I was young I've always wanted to max out all of my stats. I didn't just want to be fit and muscular, but multidimensionally jacked. All domains developed. Mind, body, spirit, relationships, money. So, I created a map to reach your full potential: letters.thedankoe.com/p/human-30-a-m…
zh: 我从小就想把数据都加到最大 我并不想被身体健全 身体强健 并且被多方面的打击 所有域都开发了。 心灵,身体,精神,关系,金钱。 我创造了一张地图来达到你的全部潜力: letters.thedankoe.com/p/human-30-a-m...

text: Just posted a video about this: youtu.be/SutAIv68yNk
zh: 照片来自Youtu.

text: Pro bodybuilders don't force themselves to train and eat healthy, they can't imagine a life where they don't. That doesn't mean it isn't hard or uncomfortable, but their relationship with pain is completely different from the average person. Discipline is an identity problem.
zh: 亲身健美者不会强迫自己训练,吃得健康,他们无法想象自己没有生活的地方. 这并不意味着这不难或不舒服,但他们与疼痛的关系与一般人完全不同. 纪律是身份问题

text: If you have to force yourself to do hard things, you're not going to make it. I can't think of one successful person who hated their entire way to the top. Their desire to succeed was so much stronger than their desire for comfort that they couldn't help but do the hard thing.
zh: 若得逼迫自己作苦行,则不进不进. 我无法想象一个成功的 讨厌他们一路上到顶端的人 他们成功的愿望比他们对舒适的渴望要强烈得多,以至于他们无法不做艰难的事情.

text: The only thing standing between where you are and where you want to be is a series of decisions that most people refuse to make.
zh: 站在你所在的地方和你想在的地方之间的唯一的东西就是大多数人拒绝做出的一系列决定.

text: If you feel lost, the solution is counterintuitive. Throw yourself into the unknown and become even more lost. Move locations. Read new books. Upskill for a new job. Force yourself into a place where you CAN gain clarity and interest. Then, discover your way out.
zh: 如果你觉得自己迷路了, 解法就是反直觉的. 投身未知的境地 变得更加迷茫 移动位置。 读新书. 高技能的新工作。 强迫自己进入一个可以让人变得清晰和感兴趣的地方. 那就去发现你的出路吧

text: Nobody is coming to save you. Your parents, friends, or government may try, but then you don't learn a thing. You aren't actually saved. And you'll need to be saved over and over again until you decide to take control of your life.
zh: 没人会来救你的 你的父母,朋友,或者政府可能尝试, 但你什么也学不到。 你其实没有被救出来 并且你需要反复地被拯救 直到你决定控制你的生活

text: 11 more rules to change your life: letters.thedankoe.com/p/12-rules-to-…
zh: 11条规则改变你的生活: letters.thedankoe.com/p/12-rules-to-.

text: Learning is one of the few superpowers that everyone has, but most people view it as a way to prepare themselves to earn an income, then they become too busy, or attach themselves to rigid beliefs, and stop. It's simple, but learning one new thing every day can change your life.
zh: 学习是每个人拥有的少数超能力之一,但大多数人认为这是一种准备自己挣钱的方法,然后他们变得太忙,或者沉迷于僵硬的信仰,然后停止. 很简单,但每天学一门新东西可以改变你的生活.

text: I actually copied this one from a book. But your content has absolutely given me ideas because they were good ideas that I’ve also experienced. Surprisingly I don’t use ai for my writing, but I’ll take the hit Happy to talk about it because I do think you’re missing the mark here
zh: 我从一本书上抄了这个 因为他们也是我所体验到的好想法。 却会接受乐乐的打击来谈论, 因为我觉得你错过了这个标记。

text: I get unnecessarily mad when people don't stick to their goals. You say you want to start a business, lose weight, meet new people, whatever. You say you want to change your life because you're tired of where you are. Someone tells you exactly what to do, gives you the entire roadmap. Then you don't do it. And you can't give any good reason why. "Oh I have to work and got sidetracked." "I didn't go to the gym because I had something else do to." What the fuck are you talking about? You say you "have" to work and "have" to do whatever else you're doing in the life you just said you don't want to live, but you don't HAVE to do the only things that lead to the life you want? Everyone is obsessed with getting "actionable advice" when that clearly doesn't matter at all. Your psychology is in the toilet. Your priorities are nonexistent. You need to take a day off and really think about what you are doing. "Oh Dan be more empathetic!" That IS empathy. Empathy is wanting the best for someone even if it hurts their little feelings. Most people will simply pander to you so that they feel good about not making you feel bad. That's nowhere close to empathy. Do better.
zh: 别人不坚持目标,我就发火了 你说你想创业,减肥, 认识新人,随便什么。 汝说欲改生因缘厌离处. 有人告诉你该怎么做, 给你整个路线图。 那你就别这么做了 且不能说出什么好理由来. "哦,我必须工作,并得到了 被转移。" "我没有去健身房 因为我有别的事要做" 你他妈的在说什么? 你说你"必须"工作,"必须"做你刚说过的生活中你不想活的别的事,但你不必做你想要的生活的唯一的事情? 所有人都沉迷于得到"可诉建议" 当这显然无关紧要时. 你的心理在厕所里 你没有优先级 你需要休息一天 真正思考你在做什么。 "哦,丹,你更爱戴!" 这是一种同情心。 同情心是希望一个人得到最好的 即使这伤害了他们的小感情 大多数人会只是对你妥协 这样他们就会觉得好不让你觉得不好 这离同情心还差得远呢 做得更好,我们得去见见他

text: Just copy what works and make it slightly better. If you're starting out, it is so incredibly unlikely that you have the experience to do something that's 100% new. If you look around, you will see copies upon copies with one tiny thing that's different. Just do that. Imitate, then innovate. The reason most beginners fail is because they have this weird to desire to be original when they haven't earned the right to be original. Drop your ego and do what works. Save yourself the pain.
zh: 只要复制什么工作,并让它稍好一些。 如果你开始,这是如此不可思议的 不太可能,你有经验 做一些100%的新事情。 如果你四处看看,你会看到复制品 上面有一个小东西,这是不同的。 照做就对了 模仿,然后创新。 大部分初学者失败的原因是,他们有这种奇特的欲望,在他们没有获得原创权时,想要成为原创. 放下你的自尊心,做有用的事。 省省苦恼.

text: If you don't know what to learn, start writing. Not because writing is a great skill, but because writing teaches you how to think, how to learn, and how to inspire people to care about what you do. Those skills are much less likely to be replaced.
zh: 学无所学者,始写. 不是因为写作是一种伟大的技巧,而是因为写作教你如何思考,如何学习,如何激励人们关心你的工作. 这些技能不太可能被取代。

text: Everyones racing to 'make it' right now because they think it's their last chance to get rich. AI was supposed to make it easier, but that's why it became substantially harder: youtu.be/ve1L21l1GW4
zh: 每个人竞相"现在就做",因为他们认为这是他们最后的发财机会. AI本来是想让它更容易,但正因为如此,它变得相当困难:youtu.be/ve1L21l1GW4

text: You'll learn a lot more by saying "fuck it," shutting your mind off, and doing the thing rather than reading 10 new books that fill your mind with noise and make it easy to do nothing.
zh: 你会学到更多,说"去他妈的", 关闭你的心灵, 做事情而不是阅读十本新书 让你的头脑充满噪音, 并让它容易做任何事情。

text: If you're the type of person who has a lot of ideas, your mind is your worst enemy, because you also have a lot of excuses. The good news is: making excuses is a habit, and if you can replace that habit with execution, you become unstoppable.
zh: 如果你是那种有很多想法的人,你的头脑就是你最糟糕的敌人,因为你也有很多借口. 好消息是: 找借口是一种习惯, 如果你能用处决来代替这种习惯, 你就变得不可阻挡。

text: Depends on what you need really. Both send emails and beehiiv is industry standard for that and has other features like automations (substack doesn't have those which is annoying but understandable). Substack has the short form social media tacked on, so if you're good at that, it can help you grow faster.
zh: 要看你到底需要什么了 需要什么吗? 无论是发送电子邮件还是蜂窝, 都属于行业标准, Substack有社交媒体的短版,所以如果你擅长,它可以帮助你更快地成长.

text: That being said, I don't know how it looks for beginners starting out, but I've heard good things. I transferred and cleaned my list beforehand, but gave it a few months before gauging open rates. They've stayed consistent
zh: 这么说,我不知道初学者的初衷是什么样子 但我听过好听的 我提前转移和清理了我的名单,但在计算开放费率之前几个月就给出了名单。 他们一直坚持下去

text: I slept on Substack for a while and am pretty baffled that (1) I've been getting more monthly views there than on YouTube and (2) open rates are higher than they've ever been Bullish on written long form
zh: 我睡了一段时间 相当困惑 (1) 我在那里每月的浏览量比YouTube上要多 (2)公开率比他们写成的Bullish要高

text: Read the latest letter if you want to know what skills to learn: letters.thedankoe.com/p/learn-these-…
zh: 想要了解学习技能,请阅读最新信件: letters.thedankoe.com/p/learn-thes-.

text: Most people don't have money problems, they have emotional management and focus problems. You need a product or service, people to buy it, and the ability to learn and iterate. That's it. You know that. You just can't sit down and do it, and you can't see through the illusions your mind constantly fabricates to try and keep you the same.
zh: 大多数人没有金钱问题,有情绪管理,有焦点问题. 你需要一种产品或服务,人们购买, 以及学习和运动的能力。 就是这样。 你知道的,你应该知道 你只是不能坐下来做,你无法透过幻觉看到 你的思想不断编织 试图保持你一样。

text: 18-year-olds are expected to know exactly what they want to do with their life when the only thing they've learned is how to imitate their parents and teachers. The greatest thing young people can do is admit they don't know what they want, begin self-education, and take as many risks as possible while they have their youth.
zh: 18岁的人应该知道他们想要怎样生活,当他们学到的唯一东西就是如何模仿父母和老师. 年轻人所能做的最大的事情是承认他们不知道自己想要什么,开始自我教育,在他们有青春的时候尽可能地冒风险.

text: This is somewhat counterintuitive, because you have to be confident and polarizing. That's what gives people the clarity to act. Nuance absolutely has its place, but it often just leads to people being overwhelmed with options and provides justification to do nothing.
zh: 这有点反感,因为你必须自信和两极化。 令众生得清净行. 努恩斯绝对有它的地位,但它往往导致人们被选择所压倒,并为无所作为提供理由。

text: The more I write and create stuff on the internet, the more I'm realizing that my job isn't to help people succeed with my super special advice. It's to help them fail by giving them something to try. That way they can start to understand what *they* want and take their own path.
zh: 我越是在网上写作和创作东西, 我就越意识到我的工作不是靠我特别的建议来帮助人们成功。 是为了让他们尝试一些东西来帮助他们失败. 这样他们就能开始理解 他们想要的 并走上自己的道路

text: A sign of a well designed life is that you don't want to take a day off because that would be far less enjoyable than your average day.
zh: 设计良好的生活的一个标志是,你不想休假一天,因为那会比一般的一天更不愉快。

text: I am once again telling you if you can spend 8 hours building someone else's dreams you can spend 1 hour building your own.
zh: 我再次告诉你 如果你能花8个小时去创造别人的梦想 你就能花1个小时去创造你自己的梦想

text: If you're bored, create something. Anything. Software. Designs. Photos. Videos. Writing. A business. A body. A relationship. A new way of thinking. How can you not feel electric to wake up and have the ability to do whatever you want.
zh: 如果你觉得无聊,就创造出一些东西。 什么都行,我爱她 软件。 设计. 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 视频. 写作。 生意,我说,我需要你帮忙 A business. 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 一种关系。 一种新的思维方式。 醒来后怎么感觉不到电 还能做你想做的事

text: If you can't learn anything in 2 weeks you're trying to learn too much. Here's how to learn fast without any science tactics that just distract you even more from achieving your goal: youtu.be/oGSHKXEKT2Q
zh: 如果你两周内什么都学不出来,你就会想学太多. 如何快速学习, 没有任何科学策略 只会分散你对目标实现的注意力: yetu.be/oGSHKXEKT2Q

text: If you don't choose your own game, society will assign you one, and society's games are incredibly boring. Same quest. Same routine. For 40 years until you win a mediocre prize. You don't want freedom from rules. You want the freedom to create your own.
zh: 如果你不自己选择游戏,社会会给你分配一个, 社会游戏会令人难以置信的无聊。 同样的追求。 同样的常规。 四十年直到你赢得平庸奖 你不想摆脱规则 你想自由创造你自己

text: I am once again asking you to start and never stop. What the fuck else are you going to do? You AREN'T going to reach your potential? You're just going to sit in front of your little screen all day? You're just going to keep making life hard instead of doing something hard?
