zh: 我再次要求你开始,永远不要停止。 你他妈的还打算怎么办? 你不打算达到你的潜力? 你整天都坐在你小屏幕前吗? 你只是要继续让生活变得艰难 而不是做一些艰难的事情?

text: Read more here: letters.thedankoe.com/p/youre-not-de…
zh: 更多内容请参见: letters.thedankoe.com/p/youre -not-de...

text: You can log 8 hours of World of Warcraft or staring at your phone without blinking an eye but can't focus for 30 minutes on work that will change your life. You don't have a focus problem. You just don't see your life as a game and don't treat your goals as quests.
zh: 你可以记录8小时的战舰世界 或者盯着你的手机,不眨眼 但不能集中精力30分钟的工作, 这将改变你的生活。 你没有焦点问题 你只是不把你的生活看成游戏,也不把你的目标当作追求.

text: This only becomes overwhelming if you don't know how to move any of those numbers. Most people drown with anxiety looking at analytics because they don't instantly decide to acquire new skills and learn how to move the numbers. They play the game but refuse to gain experience.
zh: 只有你不知道如何移动这些数字 才会变得令人难以承受 大多数人沉溺在焦虑中,看着分析,因为他们没有立刻决定获得新的技能,学会如何移动数字. 他们玩游戏,但拒绝获得经验.

text: Get absolutely dialed for a month. Life becomes a game when you pay a 'delusional' amount of attention to the details. Track your food. Track your lifts. Track your steps. Track your revenue. Progressive overload each week. Ignore everything else. You'll enjoy progress again.
zh: 拨一个月 生活成为游戏 当你对细节 给予"幻想"关注时 追踪你的食物 追踪你的电梯。 追踪你的台阶。 追踪你的收入。 每周都逐渐超载 忽略一切别事. 你会再次享受进步的

text: Quitting bad habits is easy if you research their negative impacts to the point where you're disgusted with them and hate the thought of doing them.
zh: 如果你研究不良习惯的消极影响, 以至于你厌恶它们, 讨厌做它们的想法, 则放弃不良习惯很容易。

text: Everything meaningful in life is downstream of self-direction. Pursuing your own goals (not the ones society assigned you), making mistakes, correcting them, discovering new knowledge, and falling in love with that process so you never fall into a depressing stagnation.
zh: 生活中有意义的一切都是自导自演的下游. 追求自己的目标(不是社会赋予你的目标),犯错误,纠正错误,发现新知识,爱上这个过程,这样你就不会陷入令人沮丧的停滞状态。

text: You don't know what to do with your life because you thought about your future for 30 seconds and called it a day. You need to think about it every day. For the rest of your life. You need to ruthlessly eliminate what you don't want and refine what you want.
zh: 你不知如何对待你的生活,因为你思考了30秒的前途,一天就叫它". 你需要考虑它的每一天。 终身不离. 需要无情地消灭自己不想的东西,完善自己想要的东西.

text: For the next 6 months, every time you blame someone else or try to change them, try accepting the blame and changing yourself even if you aren't at fault. Your life will turn around faster than you can imagine.
zh: 接下来的六个月里,每次你责怪别人或试图改变他们,尝试接受责怪,并改变自己,即使你没有错. 你的生活会比想象的快

text: Work when it's time to work and rest when it's time to rest. Work is a narrow state of mind while rest is an open state. If your life is dominated by work, you miss out on the ideas and creativity that lead to higher-paying meaningful work. This is why most people are stuck.
zh: 工作时工作,休息时休息. 工作是一种狭隘的心态,而休息则是一种开放状态。 如果你的生活被工作所支配,你错过了导致高薪有意义的工作的想法和创造力. 这也是大多数人被困的原因。

text: If it doesn't lead to something you'd want to take over your life, don't do it.
zh: 如果它不导致一些事情 你愿意接管你的生活, 不要这样做。

text: More on this: letters.thedankoe.com/p/you-dont-nee…
zh: 更多关于这个: letters. thedankoe.com/p/you-dont-nee...

text: Nobody cares about your niche. They care about your point of view. They don't want regurgitated information they can get from chat gpt or google. They want your opinion. They want your conviction. They want to feel the excitement and despair happening in your unique mind.
zh: 没人在乎你的专长 他们关心你的观点。 他们不想重温他们可以从聊天gpt或google得到的信息. 他们想要你的意见 他们想要你的信念。 他们想感受你独特的思想中 发生的兴奋和绝望

text: People drastically underestimate how much one single hour per day on 1-2 needle-moving tasks can compound. It doesn't feel like much each day, and it can be demotivating, but sometimes an hour is all you have, and 365+ hours can build something incredible.
zh: 人们严重低估了每天1小时1至2针移动任务会增加多少。 感觉并不像每天, 它可以降级, 但有时一个小时是你所有的, 365+小时可以 构建一些不可思议的东西。

text: The single greatest productivity tip I could give is to wake up an hour earlier and get straight to work on the single most important thing that will move the needle toward your goals. Preferably before most people are even awake. People are the biggest source of distractions.
zh: 我所能给出的最大的生产力提示 就是提前一小时醒来 直接研究一个最重要的事情 将针头移到你的目标上 最好在大多数人都醒过来之前 人是分散注意力的最大来源.

text: Competition is largely an illusion. 95% of people don't even try to do great things. 0.1% of the people are loud, so you overestimate how many people there are. The rest get stuck worrying about competition and quitting after 2 weeks.
zh: 竞争基本上是一种幻想。 95%的人甚至不尝试做伟大的事情. 0.1%的人很吵 所以你高估了有多少人 剩下的人担心竞争 两周后就辞职了

text: If you're young, I don't think you understand how much of an advantage you have. When I talk with various leaders, CEOs, etc, they're absolutely clueless when it comes to social media and technology. They pay an absurd amount of money for young people to help them. By simply being on social media for so long, your brain is wired for it. You have what could be a hyper valuable skill set out of the box. You just need to shift from a consumer mindset to a business mindset. You need to realize that people will, in fact, pay you to make stupid videos.
zh: 如果你年轻,我不认为 你明白你有多有优势。 当我和各种领袖,CEO等交谈时,当涉及到社交媒体和技术时,他们绝对是无稽之谈. 他们为年轻人付出了一笔荒谬的金钱来帮助他们。 仅仅在社交媒体上呆了这么久 你的大脑就被电线电了 你拥有一个非常宝贵的技能 出自盒子。 你只需要从消费思维转变到商业思维。 你需要意识到,人们会,事实上, 支付你制作愚蠢的视频。

text: Discipline is about getting clear on what you want. Once you know with absolute conviction that you don't want the life that your current habits are leading to, you hate the thought of doing them. Staying the same becomes more painful than pursuing an uncertain goal.
zh: 纪律是要明白你想要什么 一旦你绝对地确信你不希望你现在的习惯导致的生活,你就会讨厌做这些事的想法. 保持不变比追求一个不确定的目标更加痛苦。

text: Your potential is determined by how much uncertainty you're willing to embrace.
zh: 你的潜力取决于你愿意接受多少不确定性

text: More in yesterdays letter: letters.thedankoe.com/p/you-have-36-…
zh: 更多在昨天的信中: letters.thedankoe.com/p/you -have -36 -.

text: Don't identify with a tool because tools change and get replaced. Identify with what the tools allow you to do: create. That's what you love doing. It doesn't matter if you do it with a paintbrush, camera, Photoshop, or AI. What matters is the vision you bring into reality.
zh: 不要用工具来识别,因为工具会改变,会被替换. 以工具识别您可以做什么: 创建 。 汝所爱乐作是事. 使用漆刷,照相机,照相机或AI做也没关系. 重要的是你给现实带来的愿景。

text: Discipline is a feature of identity. Meaning, you are already disciplined based on what you view as important. Some people play 12 hours of video games, others have no issue eating healthy and going to the gym. Discipline isn't forced, it's native to who you are.
zh: 纪律是身份特征之一。 意思是,你已经遵守了纪律 以你认为重要的为依归 有些人玩了12小时的电子游戏,其他人没有问题 吃健康的食物,去健身房。 纪律不是强迫的,而是你本性

text: One of the best things you can do for your future is learn "how to think." Money, relationships, or anything else you can do in life are dependent on how well you think. Use this mental model and you'll be blown away: youtu.be/YTWm6sG0n6s
zh: 未来最好的一件事就是学会"如何思考". 钱,关系,或者你生活中能做的任何事,都取决于你的想法。 使用这个精神模型,你会被吹走:ytu.be/YTWm6sG0n6s

text: You as a single person have more power today than a 20 person company of the past. The internet gave you the ability to learn anything. Social media gave you the leverage to reach anyone. AI is giving you the ability to create almost anything. Please don't waste it.
zh: 你作为一个单身的人,今天拥有比过去20个人公司更多的权力. 互联网让你有能力学习任何东西。 社交媒体给了你影响力 以达到任何人。 AI给你创造几乎一切的能力. 请勿浪费.

text: Feeling lost is a sign that you are no longer your past self, but not fully the person you want to become. That's uncomfortable. The worst thing you can do here is what most people do, which is to numb the pain with comfort and close yourself off to your future self.
zh: 感觉迷失了,这表示你不再是过去的自我,但并非完全成为你想成为的人。 这很不舒服。 你在这里最糟糕的就是大多数人所做的, 就是用舒适来麻木痛苦, 并关闭自己 与你的未来自我。

text: The reason you're not happy is because you think happiness = euphoria so if you aren't maxing out on dopamine all the time you think you're doing something wrong. Happiness, most of the time, is being calm and in a good mood.
zh: 你并不快乐的原因是你认为幸福是喜悦 所以如果你不总是在多巴胺上 做错事的话 幸福,大部分时间,是平静的,心情良好.

text: Focus on 2 things: - Mastering your mental / emotional state in situations you can't control - Realizing you can control more than you think, and that you can improve your skill to the point of getting what you want Give it 10 years and watch everything change
zh: 专注于两件事: - 在无法控制的情况下掌握你的精神/情感状态 - 意识到你能控制得比你想象的多,并且你可以提高你的技能,达到得到你想要的目的 给它10年,看着一切改变

text: Learn how here: letters.thedankoe.com/p/you-can-lear…
zh: 学到这里: letter.thedankoe.com/p/you-can-lear...

text: One of the most important things you can understand is that school trains you to learn a specific way, and that way is extremely slow. Learning allows you to be useful and get what you want. It should be a top priority to learn how to learn, practice it, and master it.
zh: 你能够理解的最重要的事情之一 就是学校训练你学习一个特定的方法, 这种方式是极其缓慢的。 学习可以让你有用,得到你想要的东西. 学人之学,习人之学,兼有其才,应是重重.

text: As much as people want to argue that physical appearance doesn’t matter, it does. A lot. It’s 24/7 non-verbal communication of your values. Most people aren’t unattractive, they just don’t put a shred of effort into taking care of themselves.
zh: 也有人想指出外表并不重要, 常来. 以非语言方式表达你的价值观。 多数人并不缺乏吸引力,

text: You can learn anything in 2 weeks. If you can't, you're trying to learn too much at once. Break the goal down and focus solely on doing what's necessary to reach it. If your learning doesn't involve doing, most of what you learn is disappearing the second you learn it.
zh: 两周后你就能学到任何东西了 若不能,则一时欲学太过. 打破目标,只专注于做为达到目标所必需的事情. 如果你的学习不涉及做, 你学到的大部分东西 都消失在学习的第二。

text: If you're trying to get exposure on your work, it sounds weird, but starting a podcast is one of the best things you can do. Not because you want to make it the number 1 podcast in the world, but because: 1) High status people / people you respect would rather be on a podcast than on a call with you. They're more likely to respond and accept, meaning you get a free mentorship call to ask them all your questions. 2) You leverage their large audience, and they also benefit from it. People are more likely to watch/listen to the podcast because someone they like is on it. This is arguably the best beginner growth "hack." 3) You form deeper relationships with good people. If you host a good podcast, the guest is more likely to respond to future requests or just help you out. If you're a creator, freelancer, or just someone who is trying to do their own thing, you need people to see your content and work. Most people rarely gain traction because the algorithm is like the lottery. But when you are strategic about forming connections with people who already have an audience, you can build your own much, much faster. Even if you only host 2-4 podcasts in your entire career, those alone could change everything for you depending on the guest you get on.
zh: 如果你想让工作曝光,听起来很奇怪,但开始播客是你能做的最好的事情之一. 不是因为你想让它成为世界上的头号播客 而是因为:1)高地位的人/你尊重的人宁愿在播客上而不是在与你通话时. 他们更可能回答和接受, 这意味着你得到免费的导师电话 问他们所有的问题。 2)你利用他们广大的观众,他们也从中获益. 人们更可能观看/聆听播客,因为他们喜欢的人在上面。 这可以说是最佳的初创增长"hack". 3) 你和好人形成了更深层的关系. 如果你是好播客, 客人更可能回应 未来的要求或只是帮助你。 如果你是一个创造者,自由职业者,或者只是一个人 谁试图做自己的事情, 你需要人们看到你的内容和工作。 大多数人很少获得牵引力,因为算法就像彩票. 但是,当你有战略眼光,要与已经拥有观众的人建立联系, 你就可以更快地建立自己的关系。 即使你整个职业生涯中只接待2-4播客, 独自一人可以改变一切,取决于你得到的客人。

text: Everyone wants a great story to tell (but not the catastrophic failure that makes it worth telling).
zh: 每个人都希望有一个伟大的故事(但不是值得一提的灾难性失败).

text: There is a very rare type of idea. When it pops into your mind, you feel like you can't help yourself. You're forced by some higher instinct to act on it. You cancel all plans. The vision is clear. You must protect it at all costs. These are the ideas that change your life.
zh: 有一种非常罕见的想法。 当它进入你的脑海,你感觉你无法自救。 你被某种更高的本能所逼迫 不得不采取行动 你取消了所有计划。 观心明了. 你必须不惜一切代价保护它 这些想法改变了你的生活。

text: Your work is unlikely to be noticed until your skill matches your taste. You know what “good” looks like, but you need to practice until what you produce is at that level. That’s when things take off.
zh: 你的工作在你的品味上 才不会被注意 你知道“好”是什么样子,但你需要练习直到你生产的产品达到这一水平。 事情就从此开始。

text: I hate the whole "entrepreneurship isn't for everyone" narrative because "it's a hard thing to do." No shit it's hard. That's the point. Yes, most people aren't cut out for it right at this very second. But people can grow. They can learn something new. They can, in fact, challenge themselves. It’s insane to believe that people can actually take control of the 1/3 of their life called work, I know. Everyone could benefit from at bare minimum trying to do something difficult that has such a massive upside in the form of wisdom, skill, and increasing your value even if it doesn't work out. Call me delusional but I believe everyone should pursue work they find meaningful and avoid a life thats perpetually comfortable. “But you can also get a super high paying job that you enjoy!” Yes, but that is still fucking hard. Sometimes a lot harder than entrepreneurship (no, entrepreneurship does not equal trying to build a billion dollar company or working 16 hours day). Being an “entrepreneur” originally meant “to undertake.” To do something. To take a risk. To be high agency. Self governance. To direct your own life rather than outsource it to someone else. So yes, entrepreneurship is for everyone, like the free individuals of the past working on their craft and pursuing their interests. If you haven’t tried it (actually tried it) then you have no idea whether or not it’s for you, and not trying it because someone said it’s “not for everyone” is both dumb and robbing you of potential.
zh: 讨厌"创业不是为每个人"的叙述,因为"很难做". 才不是难. 即是说. 是的,大多数人 不适合它的权利 在这个时候。 但人能增长. 他们可以学到一些新东西。 事实上,它们可以挑战自己。 相信人们能真正控制他们生命中的三分之一, 每个人至少可以从努力做一些 以智慧、技巧和增加价值 形式 如此巨大的颠峰 来受益,即使它不成功。 叫我妄想 但我相信每个人都应该追求他们找到有意义的工作 避免永远舒适的生活 ”但您也可以获得一份你所享受的超高薪工作。 是的,但那还是他妈的很难。 有时比创业更难(不,创业并不等于试图建立10亿美元的公司或工作16小时). 身为“企业家”最初的意思是“承担”。 做点什么 以冒险. 成为高级机构。 自我治理. 引导自己的生活 而不是外包给别人 所以,创业是每个人的, 就像过去自由的个人 努力自己的工艺和追求自己的利益。 也因为有人说这“不是对每个人”都是愚蠢和剥夺你潜力。

text: Be incredibly careful with what you allow to access your mind
zh: 注意你允许的思维

text: Read today's letter on this: letters.thedankoe.com/p/these-3-deci…
zh: 读今日关于此信: 字母. thedankoe.com/p/thes-3-deci...

text: If you want to achieve anything great, it needs to become your one true priority. The only thing on your mind. Nobody accidently got rich. Nobody accidently built a great physique. They were obsessed with it for multiple years until they no longer had to think about it.
zh: 如果你想实现任何伟大的目标,它需要成为你真正的优先事项。 唯一能让你想到的事 没人会意外地发财 没人会意外地造出一个伟大的体格 他们沉迷于这个游戏长达数年,直到不再有思考.

text: For those who need to hear it: getting rich is a perfectly viable goal. People who have a poor relationship with money will try to convince you that it isn't a meaningful way to live, but that couldn't be further from the truth. You can do great things and make great money.
zh: 对于需要听到的人来说:发财是一个完全可行的目标. 与钱关系不佳的人会试图说服你,这并非有意义的生活方式,但这与事实不符. 你可以做大事,赚大钱

text: The fact that most people turn 23 and repeat the same 6 months for the rest of their life scared the shit out of me to the point where I would do anything to avoid that. Same city. Same bars. Same problems. They call it "living life" when it is the complete opposite.
zh: 大多数人23岁,在他们的余生中重复同样的六个月,这让我害怕,以至于我愿意为避免这种情况而做任何事情。 同一城邑. 同样的条子。 同样的问题。 他们称之为"生活" 当它是完全相反的。

text: Money is a skill that can be learned just like playing a video game, but since it's become so intertwined with status, self-worth, morality, and survival people have such a distorted view of it. Your parents tell you to save it. Your pastor tells you to avoid it. Your culture tells you it's not important. Yet most of your modern problems can only be solved by money. People have such warped perceptions of money that they become controlled by it. And since they scoff at the thought of improving themselves and making more, there's no way out.
zh: 金钱是一种技能,可以学习到就像玩电子游戏一样,但由于它已经变得与地位如此交织,自我价值,道德,生存的人对它有如此扭曲的视角. 汝父母劝汝救之. 你的牧师叫你别这样 你的风尚告诉你这不重要 但是,你现代的大部分问题只能靠钱来解决。 人们对金钱的认知如此扭曲,以致于被金钱控制。 既然他们嘲笑了改善自己和制造更多东西的想法,就没有出路了.

text: How to figure out what you want: 1) Make a guess at what you want 2) Do everything in your power to achieve it 3) Realize that everything you did was just a prototype 4) Start over with more clarity on what you want Then repeat steps 2 through 4. Probably for the rest of your life.
zh: 如何想出你想要的:(1) 猜你想要的(2) 尽你所能实现它(3) 意识到你所做的一切都只是原型(4) 重新开始,更明确地说你想做什么 然后重复步骤2到4。 可能一辈子都这样

text: It's painful to watch young people treat youth as a currency rather than investment capital. The time, energy, and focus you have when you're young are extremely valuable and disappear fast. You're forming habits that will come back and hit you like a truck when you turn 30.
zh: 看着年轻人把青年当作货币而不是投资资本,是痛苦的. 年轻时的时间,精力,和注意力, 是非常宝贵的, 并且迅速消失。 你正在形成习惯, 当你30岁时, 就会像卡车一样回来打你。

text: Be careful listening to anyone who doesn’t have the life you want. Those people are rare. 1% of the population. The other 99% will blabber about how you should live your life when they’ve wasted their own. I'm sure they're kind people, but they have no idea what's best for you.
zh: 仔细聆听没有你所希望的生活的人。 那些人是罕见的。 占人口的1%. 其它99%的人会大骂你应该如何过自己的生活, 我肯定他们都是好人 但不知道什么对你最好

text: The best way to make the most of your 20s is to make them your worst. Because if we're being honest, most people hit a small peak and then repeat the same 6 months over and over again. Here are 3 things to avoid and 3 things to do: letters.thedankoe.com/p/20-30-years-…
zh: 让你20多岁的人 都成为你最坏的人 因为如果我们诚实,大多数人会撞上一个小峰,然后重复同样的6个月一次又一次. 这里有3个要避免的事情和3个要做的事情: letters.thedankoe.com/p/20-30-years -.

text: Your mind expands to fill the goals set for achievement.
zh: 你的思想扩张了 以填补 设定的目标实现。

text: You're going to miss out on 99% of the experiences life has to offer. That's just how it is. In other words, you're not missing out on anything by focusing on a few important things.
zh: 你会错过99%的体验 生活必须提供。 就是这样 也就是说,你不会因为专注于一些重要的事情而忽略任何事情.

text: There is always more that can be done. Lost people can't figure out what to do. Drained people try to do it all. Happy people choose a few things they deeply care about and dedicate the entirety of their mental, physical, and financial energy toward that.
zh: 总是可以做更多的工作。 失物不知所归. 排水者试尽其道. 快乐的人们选择了他们深深关心的 并且将精神、身体和资金的全部精力投入到这个目标中。

text: Life is hard because you don't know what you want. That's 90% of the battle. Once you have extreme clarity on what you want, after 5-10 calculated risks, everything else is quite simple. If you can reach that before you turn 25, you're in the top 1%.
zh: 人生苦苦,因不知所欲. 即九成战. 一旦你对你想要的非常清楚, 在5 -10 计算出的风险之后, 其他的事情都很简单。 如果你能在25岁之前达到这个水平,你就在前1%.

text: You need to believe you are better than most people. Not because you’re superior to them, but because what most people create isn’t that good. If you don’t believe that you can create something at least slightly better than what is already out there – the product, the app, the writing, the designs, the photos – then you are lying to yourself, and you will never produce great work.
zh: 你得相信自己比大多数人都好 并不是因为你比他们优越, 而是因为大多数人创造的东西不是那么好。 如果你不相信你能创造出比已经存在的东西更优秀的东西 — — 产品、应用、写作、设计、照片 — — 那么,你欺骗自己,永远不会产生伟大的作品。

text: Why do people who wasted their life always have the most to say about how you should live yours
zh: 为什么浪费生命的人 总是有 最多关于你应该如何生活

text: It's cliche at this point, so many people neglect it, but the best thing you can do if you don't have the life you want is to disappear for 6 months. Extreme focus. 4 habits. Mind, body, spirit, business: youtu.be/UixhcccGcdY
zh: 此时的陈词滥调,如此多人忽视了,但是如果没有你想要的生活,最好的办法就是消失六个月. 极专注. 4个习惯。 心灵、身体、精神、生意:Youtu.be/UixhccGcdY

text: You're always tired because you don't have a purpose, or your purpose is to complete some meaningless set of tasks that lead to a paycheck. You can sleep for 8 hours and cut out every food imaginable, but if you don't have a life trajectory you deeply care about, you will never feel the raw source of energy that makes you unstoppable even in horrible conditions.
zh: 你总是因为没有目的而感到疲惫,或者你的目的是完成一些毫无意义的,导致工资支付的任务. 你可以睡8小时,切除每条可以想象的食物, 但如果你没有生命轨迹,你将永远无法感受到原始的能量来源, 让你无法阻挡,即使在可怕的条件下。

text: Your life is determined by a series of 10-12 bold decisions to shift the trajectory you were given by your parents and teachers. If you don’t, you stall out. You wake up 10 years later wondering why your life got worse even though you’ve done everything you were told to do.
zh: 你的生活是由一系列10 -12的大胆决定决定的 改变你父母和老师给你的轨迹 却又不放过, 十年后你醒来,

text: The best decisions you will make are the ones that everyone else will say are stupid, risky, or crazy. And they’re right. To normal people, they are, but that’s exactly why you’re doing it. You deeply understand that it’s the only way to never end up like them.
zh: 你将做出的最佳决定 是别人会说那些 愚蠢,危险,或疯狂的决定。 并且他们是对的。 通常人会这样,但这正是你这么做的原因。 也明白这是永远无法像他们一样的出路。

text: Every morning is a chance to choose what you suffer for. I wrote about this in depth. Read my last letter here: letters.thedankoe.com/p/this-single-…
zh: 每天早上都是个选择你受苦的机会 我写了这个深度。 读我最后一封信: letters.thedankoe.com/p/this-single-...

text: There is no better place to be than under an immense amount of pressure from a challenge that you chose to take on.
zh: 没有什么比在你选择接受的挑战所带来的巨大压力下更适合去的地方了。

text: Not for everyone, but the reason my business grew is because I made "stupid" decisions that forced me to make it work. I moved into a place 2x what I could afford so I had to make more. I got rid of everything and moved to a new state for the same reason. I've become very good at creating tactical stress: not destroying my life, but putting myself in a do or die situation knowing that there is some way to come out the other side.
zh: 并不是每个人都有这种感觉,但是我生意增长的原因,是因为我做了"愚蠢"的决定,迫使我让生意成功. 我搬到一个2x我买得起的地方 所以我得赚更多 我摆脱一切,并为了同样的原因,搬到了一个新的州. 我开始很擅长制造战术压力:不是破坏我的生活,而是把自己置于一个做或者死的状况中,知道有某种方法可以从另一边出来.

text: Most people have quietly given up. Especially young people. They don't feel in control. They don't know what to do or how to do it. Here's a video on becoming the main character of your own life: youtu.be/JGlx6norGpM
zh: 大多数人都悄悄放弃了 尤其是年轻人 他们没有控制自己的感觉。 其人不知所为或如何. 以下是关于成为自己人生主角的视频: youtu.be/JGlx6norGpM 互联网档案馆的存檔,存档日期2013-09-02.

text: First, you’re in pain. Then, you become curious. And if you don’t suppress that desire to explore a new domain of life, you become obsessed. Most people never change because the pain of their current situation doesn’t outweigh the pain of pursuing the highest version of yourself.
zh: 首先,你很痛苦。 然后你变得好奇 如果你不抑制这种探索生命新领域的愿望,你就变得痴迷。 大部分人不会改变, 因为目前状况的痛苦并不超过追求自己最高版本的痛苦。

text: You “aren’t interested in anything” because you don’t give yourself a chance to become interested in anything. Most people aren’t born with a crystal clear vision for their future. The people who create a good life are those who understand that vision is accumulated with the mistakes that come from pursuing anything aside from the default path. Create something with care, and eventually you will care.
zh: 也因为你没有机会对任何事感兴趣。 大部分人生来对未来没有清晰的愿景。 创造美好生活的人,就是那些懂得远见与错误相积累的人,这些错误来自于除默认路径之外的任何事物. 创造出一些小心,最终你会在乎的。

text: Read more here: letters.thedankoe.com/p/the-death-of…
zh: 更多内容请参见: letters.thedankoe.com/p/the-death -of...

text: You don’t find your passion. You cultivate it by doing something with enthusiasm for a long enough time until you discover the depth of that craft, leading to a burning passion for it. You generate passion in the pursuit of a goal you may not have wanted to pursue, but you saw it as necessary to reach the life you want, so you shut your mind off and did it anyway.
zh: 也找不到自己的热情。 你用足够长的时间 热情做一些事情来培养它 直到你发现那艘飞船的深度 导致对它的热忱 你为追求你可能不想追求的目标而产生激情,但你认为这必须达到你想要的生活,所以你闭上你的心,不管怎样都做到了.

text: You don’t find your calling. You listen for it. Because it’s always calling you. But most people can't hear it. Too many tasks, bills, and responsibilities they never really chose to take on. The most important task in your life is to listen for it and act without hesitation.
zh: 也找不到你的电话。 你听它的话。 因为它总是叫你。 但大多数人听不到 太多的任务,法案, 和他们从未真正选择承担的责任。 你一生中最重要的任务就是倾听它,毫不犹豫地行动起来.

text: build your own thing
zh: 自己造个东西

text: Read more here: letters.thedankoe.com/p/be-a-loser-i…
zh: 更多内容请参见: letters.thedankoe.com/p/be-a-loser-i...

text: The ultimate goal is getting paid to be yourself. To solve problems you can't help but solve. To study what you can't pull yourself away from. To remove or delegate any work that does not involve a creative challenge. You don't wait for this day to magically come. You live it, right now, and slowly one hour a day will turn into every waking hour.
zh: 最终目标是得到报酬成为你自己。 解决问题你只能解决. 以修习所不能离. 取消或委托任何不涉及创造性挑战的工作。 你可别等到今天 奇迹般的来临 你活在它,现在, 慢慢地一天一个小时 将变成每一个醒悟的小时。

text: Pursuing usefulness will make you a lot happier than chasing happiness.
zh: 追求有用性会比追求幸福更快乐

text: If you don't lie to yourself, you'll go down the right path. People justify why they feel terrible, why they're trapped by money, why they can't get what they want. All of those are problems that can be solved with skill and knowledge. Honesty allows you to start solving them.
zh: 不自欺人,则顺其正途. 人们为为什么感到可怕,为什么被金钱困住,为什么得不到他们想要的东西而辩护. 所有这些问题都能够用技能和知识来解决。 诚实让你开始解决他们

text: Be a loser. Let your parents think you are stupid for not following their path. Start a business and embrace failure after failure. Study the interests that make you feel like an outcast. Where most people conform is where greatness is born.
zh: 做个失败者 让父母认为你没走他们的路是愚蠢的 创业,在失败后拥抱失败. 研究你觉得自己被排斥的利益 大多数人所适应的地方就是伟大诞生的地方.

text: The easiest way to get ahead in life is to commit to a period of skill development. 6-12 months. Pure focus for 2-4 hours a day. Learning and building. Not just binge watching tutorials, but creating quality projects that you, others, or businesses could actually benefit from. Most people won't do it but those who do launch ahead of everyone else.
zh: 人生最容易前进的方法就是致力于一段技能发展时期. 6-12个月。 纯焦日照2-4小时. 学与建. 不仅在观看辅导课程上大吃一惊, 而且还创造了你,其他人,或企业可以实际获益的优质项目。 大多数人不会这么做,但那些真正发动的人会比其他人先.

text: Avoiding conflict is ruining your life. Being too agreeable screams that you don't know what you want... or you do know what you want but can't muster up the strength to pursue it. Here's how to be more disagreeable: letters.thedankoe.com/p/how-to-be-mo…
zh: 避免冲突会毁了你的生活。 过于令人愉快的尖叫,你不知道自己想要什么... 或者你知道自己想要什么,但不能集中力量去追求它。 这里的写法更让人不满意: letters.thedankoe.com/p/如何成为mo...

text: If you can spend 4 years getting a degree to do something you don’t care about you can spend 1 year reeducating yourself to build your own thing. A lack of perspective is why most quit after 2 weeks.
zh: 如果你能花4年时间去修读一些你不在乎的东西, 缺乏视角是为什么大多数在两周后退出.

text: Your brain works against you until you give it a meaningful goal to wire itself around.
zh: 你的大脑会和你作对 直到你给它一个有意义的目标 来绕圈子

text: But you can't force it, you will know when it's time
zh: 但你不能强迫它,你会知道什么时候

text: Most people don't get what they want because they challenge themselves without changing themselves. They challenge themselves to stick to a diet for 6 months, stop scrolling 30 minutes before bed, learn a new skill, change careers. And they always fail, because discipline is a feature of identity. It’s not a challenge for fit people to eat healthy and go to the gym. It’s who they are. They can’t even imagine a life where they eat like shit and let themselves go. It doesn’t even cross their mind. Why? Because they learned long ago that they didn't want to live that way. They visualized where their life would end up. All the bad things that would happen. How they would look and feel. The opportunities that wouldn't be available to them. That negative image fueled them more than any challenge ever could. You need to be okay with the fact that you will never return to how you live right now. None of your bad habits have a place in your future life. That's why you are where you are. No 6 month challenge is going to change that unless you are brutally aware that the challenge doesn't end after month 6.
zh: 大多数人得不到他们想要的东西,因为他们挑战自己而不改变自己. 他们挑战自己坚持6个月饮食,停止在睡觉前30分钟滚动,学习新技能,改变事业. 他们总是失败,因为纪律是身份特征之一。 健康地吃东西去健身, 也正是他们是谁。 也无法想象他们吃屎、放走自己的生活。 也并不在他们心目中。 为什么? 因为他们早就知道 他们不想那样生活 他们想象了他们生命的结局 将发生的一切坏事。 他们的外表和感觉 机会是他们得不到的 这种负面形象使他们比任何挑战都更受推波助澜。 你必须要接受这个事实,即你永远也不会回到你现在的生活。 你所有的坏习惯 都无法在将来生活中占有一席之地 故知身处. 6个月的挑战不会改变 除非你知道6个月后的挑战不会结束

text: I'm generally unpleasant to be around. I prefer being alone. I hate being out of routine for more than 2-3 days. I'm either thinking about nothing or everything with little in between. I say no to almost everything that gets in the way. At first I thought it was a character flaw and tried to change it. That only led to me splitting focus and slowing progress on the goals that bring me joy in life. When I observe people I look up to, their mission comes first. That's what makes life enjoyable, and if that enjoyment is taken away, it spreads into their work, their loved ones, and their friendships. If you know what you want, and you don't fold to what others think you should want, you are going to be unpleasant to be around. That isn't something to be ashamed of but embraced. In fact, you'll find that people want to be around you more because they trust you. They can feel that you aren't putting on an act like everyone else.
zh: 我一般不高兴在附近。 我更喜欢一个人 我讨厌2 -3天以上都出轨了 我要么想着什么 要么想着所有的东西 与小的在两者之间。 我几乎对阻碍你的一切说不 起初我认为这是个性缺陷,并试图改变它. 这只会导致我分散注意力,减缓在那些为我带来人生快乐的目标上的进展。 当我观察我所仰望的人时,他们的任务就摆在首位。 这就是让生活变得愉快的原因,如果这种享受被夺走,它就传播到他们的工作,他们的亲人,以及他们的友谊中. 如果你知道自己想要什么, 并且你不会折叠到别人认为你应该想要的, 你会不愉快地在身边。 这不是值得羞耻的,而是要被拥抱的 事实上,你会发现人们更想在你身边 因为他们信任你 他们觉得你不像其他人那样演戏

text: If you don't know what you want, people will tell you what you want, and you'll believe it because you have nothing better to do.
zh: 不知所欲,则人必告之,而信之,因无所宜也.

text: Focus on these 3 things for the next 30 days: 1) One book for mental gains (30 minutes reading) 2) One project for career gains (2 hours deep work) 3) One fitness routine for physical gains (1 hour workout) Changing your life can be so simple.

How to be an NPC: - Never question your parents beliefs - Never go off the guardrails at school - Regurgitate what you hear in the news - Always make a choice that's safe and secure And of course: always treat others goals and opinions as more important than your own.
zh: 如何成为国家计生委: 永远不要质疑父母的信仰 永远不要离开学校的护栏 重新审视你听到的新闻 永远做出一个安全可靠的选择 当然,总是把其他目标和观点视为比自己的更重要。

text: a good life is the process of narrowing down what doesn't work
zh: 美好生活是缩小 工作不起作用的过程

text: High intelligence + high agency = building rocket ships to bring humanity to mars. Low intelligence + high agency = starting a small business without caring about optimization. High intelligence + low agency = crying about how rich people should not exist. Low intelligence + low agency = playing victim while following the default path laid out for you. The lesson = with the right amount of agency, it doesn't matter how intelligent you are.
zh: 高智商+高代理 = 建造火箭船,将人类带到火星. 低智+高代理 = 创办小企业而不关心优化. 高智商+低代理 = 哭喊着不应该有富人. 低智能+低代理 = 玩受害者 同时遵循为您设定的默认路径. 教书=有适当数量的代理,不管你有多聪明.

text: How to make the wrong decision: Ask everyone for their opinion. Tell everyone what you’re going to do before you do it. Listen to everyone, especially those who don’t have the results you want in life.
zh: 如何做出错误的决定:问大家的意见. 告诉大家你要做的事情, 特别是那些没有你想要的结果的人。

text: Ask yourself, "What would the average person do?" Then do the opposite.
zh: 问自己,"一般人会怎么做?" 那就反之为然.

text: Everything is a muscle Everything is a skill issue
zh: 一切皆为肌肉 一切皆为技巧问题

text: I oscillate between wanting to be the next Steve Jobs and wanting to throw my phone in a lake and join the monks at least 3 times a day.
zh: 我将想成为下一个史蒂夫・乔布斯 和想把我的手机扔进湖里 和僧侣们一起生活 一天至少三次

text: Hate to break it to you but the hours spent in your head won't save you from the second needed to take the step
zh: 不想告诉你,但你脑海中花的时间 无法让你从第二步中脱身

text: This may sound obvious but if you don’t feel inspired, you need to surround yourself with inspiration. Books that inspire your thinking and articulation. Businesses that inspire your vision and strategy. It gets the cogs turning in your brain. You can feel the boost in energy.
zh: 可能听起来很明显,但如果你感觉不到灵感,你需要用灵感环绕自己。 书激发你的思考和表达。 激发你远见和战略的企业。 它会让软糖在你的大脑里转动 能量的增强能感觉到

text: This sounds stupid but I swear it works miracles. For the next 7 days, find a 1 hour long lecture on YouTube from Alan Watts. You have to listen to it when you aren't distracted by anything else: before you take a nap, before you fall asleep, or on a quiet walk. When I do this I can start to feel my mind opening. Ideas start flowing again. Everything feels lighter. I find myself doing this when I feel very narrow. I can't come up with new ideas. I've been working too much. Life feels repetitive. I've done this once a year for the past 5 years and I still don't know why I don't make it a daily thing.
zh: 这听起来很愚蠢 但我发誓这能成功 接下来的7天里,在YouTube上找到艾伦·瓦兹(Alan Watts)的长达1小时的讲座. 不必分心时,必须听:在睡觉前,在睡觉前,或在静静的散步中,听完. 当我这样做的时候,我可以开始感觉到我的心扉。 思想又开始流畅了 一切都感觉很轻。 我发现自己这样做的时候,我觉得非常狭窄。 我无法想出新的想法。 我工作太忙了 工作时间太长了 生活感觉重复。 我做了这个一年一次 在过去的五年里, 我还是不知道为什么我不把它变成日常的事情。

text: I hate being put in a box. Therefore I hate the traditional concept of niching down as far as possible. If you have multiple interests, here's how you don't go insane: youtu.be/6Q_1QJsO4Ro
zh: 我讨厌被放进盒子里 因此,我讨厌传统的概念 尽可能的去除。 如果你有多重兴趣,你就不会疯掉:youtu.be/6==1QJsO4Ro

text: Don’t feel bad for being picky about who you allow into your life.
zh: 也不要为自己对自己允许的人的挑剔而感到难过。

text: distractions are the byproducts of goals you don't care about
zh: 分散注意力是你们不在乎的目标的副产品

text: You care too much about what other people think because your self-image would be shattered if you didn't. You want to be a "good" person. And you think a good person is always kind, noble, and caring. You secretly admire those who don't give a fuck, but if you did the same, you would no longer be a good person according to your broken idea of what that means. You don't need to become an asshole, but you do need to choose what you value and give yourself permission to make that your priority.
zh: 你太在意别人怎么想了 因为你的自我形象如果不这样就会被粉碎 你想成为一个"好"的人。 你认为一个好人总是善良,高尚,关心。 你暗中崇拜那些不给他妈的, 但如果你这样做, 你将不再是一个好人 根据你打破的 想法,那意味着什么。 你不需要成为一个混蛋, 但你确实需要选择你的价值 并给自己一个允许 来让这个你的优先事项。

text: The best way to learn is the opposite of what we're taught. You don't learn everything, then start. You start, then learn why it did or didn't work. You start with minimum viable knowledge, then experiment, then succeed or fail, then study to understand why. People dumber than you tend to run circles around you because of this. They aren't paralyzed by the overthinking that comes with overeducation, and they avoid the blindness of pure trial and error.
zh: 学道之妙,与所教之相违. 你没有学过一切,然后开始。 你开始,然后知道为什么它有或没有成功。 你从最低可行的知识开始,然后是实验,然后是成功还是失败,然后是研究来了解原因。 比你笨的人往往会因为这个而绕着你转圈. 他们不会因为过度教育而导致的过度思考而瘫痪,他们避免了纯粹的试验和错误的盲目.

text: Everyone wants to get rich with AI but nobody wants to go through the uncertainty of building a business. The people who would have gotten rich without AI will, and the people who only start because they think AI will do it for them won't.
zh: 每个人都想用AI发财 但没人想通过建设企业的不确定性 没有AI的人就会发财,只有因为认为AI会为他们发财的人才会发财.

text: do this once every 6 months, minimum
zh: 最少半年一次,至少一次

text: It seems like every tech CEO and their mother won't stop talking about how AI is coming for your job. So I did the same thing. Here's my thoughts on the future of work and how to become AI first: youtu.be/vFgjDSySYo8
zh: 看来每个技术总裁 和他们的妈妈 都不停地谈论AI是如何来找你工作的 所以我也做了同样的事情。 以下是我对于未来工作及如何成为AI的思考: youtu.be/vFgjDSySYo8

text: If you struggle with knowing what you want out of life, I send 2 weekly letters out on becoming future-proof: thedankoe.substack.com
zh: 如果你在生活中为了解自己想要的东西而挣扎,我每周发两封信出来,以证明未来是安全的:thedankoe.substack.com

text: I just published the book to read on Substack as well. If you prefer audio, you can download the Substack app, navigate to the book, and hit the play button at the top of your screen: thedankoe.substack.com/p/purpose-and-… Read here^
zh: 我刚刚出版了一本书 来读《Substack》 如果您喜欢音频,可以下载Substack应用程序,导航到书,并点击屏幕顶端的游戏按钮:thedankoe.substack.com/p/ survice-and-. 读于此

text: You need 3 habits. One that builds your mind (reading, learning, journaling). One that builds your body (gym, running, nutrition). One that builds your money (business, skill dev, investing). Anything more is a bonus, anything less is self-destructive.
zh: 你需要三个习惯。 一种可以建立你的思想(阅读,学习,日记). 一种可以锻炼你的身体(基因,运行,营养). 一个用来积累你的钱(生意、技能、投资)的。 任何更进一步都是奖金, 任何更低的都是自毁的。

text: If you can't find the answer by searching for it, you can probably find it on a long walk, at the end of a workout, or during an extended period of time alone.
zh: 如果通过寻找找不到答案,很可能可以在长途旅行中,在健身结束后,或者在长时间单独寻找.

text: It's only difficult because you are sitting around thinking about how difficult it is.
zh: 是因为你一直想着这有多难

text: When you can't make a decision, remind yourself of: 1 - Who you don't want to become 2 - Who you want to become And if you still can't make a decision, ask yourself why you care so much about the approval of others.
zh: 当你无法做出决定时,请提醒自己: 1 - 你不愿成为谁 - 你想成为谁 如果你还是不能做决定, 问问你自己你为什么那么在乎别人的认可.

text: You're bored because you don't have a quest. You played the game up until getting a job, spouse, or house and didn't realize that was just the tutorial phase.
zh: 你因为没有追求而感到无聊. 你一直玩到找到工作 配偶或房子 却不知道这只是辅导阶段

text: How to build a business in 2025: - Post ideas on social media - Analyze which do the best - Turn them into free downloads - Analyze reception and results - Turn one into digital product/service - Improve until irresistible - Turn into software, physical products, etc Free idea validation, cash flow to do it full time, and a base of potential customers to support the startup you've always wanted to build. Pair it with AI and it's the lowest risk highest reward option.
zh: 2025年如何打造企业: - 社交媒体上的贴吧创意 - 分析最能做 - 将其转化为免费下载 - 分析接收和结果 - 将一个转化为数字产品/服务 - 改善直到无法抗拒 - 转变为软件,实物产品等自由创意验证,现金流来全职完成,以及潜在的客户基础支持你一直希望建立的启动. 和AI对等,这是风险最低的最高奖励选择.

text: Working in paradise is extremely overrated. You can't even see your screen from the sun glare on the beach. Wifi connection is always spotty. Can rarely find a place without distractions. I'd take a home office with nothing in it every day of the week.
zh: 在天堂工作被高度评价 你连海滩上的阳光都看不到你的屏幕 维菲连接总是很不畅通的. 很少能找到一个地方 没有分散注意力。 我每周都去一个没有东西的家办公室

text: If you don't know what to write about, write about the difficult thing you've been trying to learn, because it will help you learn it so much faster.
zh: 如果你不知道写什么,写你一直试图学习的艰难的事情,因为这会帮助你更快地学习.

text: The sooner you start the sooner you can start sucking less.
zh: 越早开始 越早开始吸少一点

text: Luck is a percentage that increases with the number of people who know what you do and how well you do it.
zh: 运气是随着了解你的工作和工作情况的人数量的增长而增加的百分比。

text: I've worn a hat to simulate blocking distractions. I've set the pomodoro timers to 'unlock laser focus.' I've done many things, but few things are as powerful (and realistic) as this deep work routine: youtu.be/kLd5AvsLWAc
zh: 我戴帽子来模拟 阻止分心 我设定了波摩多罗定时器 以"解锁激光聚焦" 我做过很多事情,但很少有事情像这个深层的工作常规一样强大(和现实主义):youtu.be/kLd5AvsLWAc.

text: You're bored because you haven't developed a skill set that allows you to take on interesting challenges.
zh: 你无聊是因为你还没有开发出一个技能集,让你能够承担有趣的挑战.

text: Grab a notebook. Write out exactly what you want in your future, don't miss a detail. Then, break it down into goals. Decades, years, months, weeks, and days. Every day, write down the 3 levers you can move to create your vision. Then, forget about the goals and execute.
zh: 拿着笔记本 写出你未来的希望,不要错过细节。 然后,把它拆成目标。 十年,岁月,周,日. 每天,写下三根杠杆,你可以移动来创造你的视觉. 那就忘了目标,然后执行

text: Get excited over the small things. The bland but healthy food. The difficult but rewarding last rep. The uncomfortable but necessary silence of being alone. All of it. The highs, lows, bigs, and smalls of life are going to pass whether you enjoy them or not.
zh: 兴奋一下小事 肉质但健康的食物。 难但有回报的最后一个代表。 孤独的不适但必须的沉默。 全部都一样 生命的高度,低,大,小 将过无论你是否享受它们。

text: Being able to figure it out is becoming more important than ever: thedankoe.substack.com/p/the-unpleasa… Read last weeks letter^
zh: 能够解析出来,现在比以往任何时候都更加重要:thedankoe.substack.com/p/the-unplasa. . 读取上星期的字母 ^

text: Cope: "I can't do that." Curiosity: "Why can't I do that?" Conviction: "Fuck it I'll figure it out."
zh: 柯普:"我不能这样做". 奇曰:"吾何以不能为? 罪曰:"操我计. "

text: It can be as simple as 4 habits. One for mind. One for body. One for finances. One for relationships. Choose a habit for each that gets 80% of the results with 20% of the effort. Stick with them for a year and you'll be ahead of most people.
zh: 其能简如四习. 一为智. 一为体. 一为财经. 一者为情. 选择一个习惯,每个习惯得到80%的结果,并付出20%的努力。 跟着他们一年,你就会领先大多数人

text: As a creator who doesn't like AI doing all of your writing, scripting, idea generation, what has been the most useful AI workflow you've come across? I see the value in businesses having all of that done for them, but for "idea workers" so to say, what have you found?
zh: 作为一位不喜欢AI做你所有写作,脚本,创意生成的创作者,你遇到的最有用的AI工作流程是什么? 我看见那些企业 对他们有价值 但对于"理想工人"来说 你发现了什么?

text: How to be productive as fuck: - Find something you deeply care about - Do everything in your power to do that full time - Say no to anything that pulls you away from those Most people use deep work tactics to compensate for a lack of work they can't pull themselves away from.
zh: 如何像他妈的一样有生产力: 找到你深思熟虑的东西 尽你所能做这个全时工作 拒绝任何能把你从大多数人身边拉开的 东西 使用深思熟虑的策略来弥补他们无法摆脱的工作的缺失

text: If you have multiple interests, this is for you. thedankoe.substack.com/p/the-most-pro… Read here^
zh: 如果你有多重兴趣,这是给你的。 the( the). substack.com/p/最亲. 读于此

text: If you struggle with topics to write about, or you don't know which niche to choose: Try creating a mission instead. An ideal life you are leading people towards. Then, almost any idea can be framed to help move toward that end goal. I wrote a post on this:
zh: 如果你在题材上挣扎写作,或者你不知道选择哪个位置: 尝试创建任务。 你正在带领人们走向的理想生活 然后,几乎任何想法都可以被设计成有助于朝着这个最终目标迈进。 我写了一篇关于这个的文章:

text: It's better to have conviction in a bad idea and fail than to nuance yourself into irrelevance.
zh: 不如在坏主意中有信念而失败,而不是把自己细微地融入无关紧要.

text: If you want to grow fast, you need to create something so undeniably good that people with good taste share it without second thought. Forget about the algorithm for a bit. Smart people love to share high-signal things on socials and in tight-knit group chats. Optimize for that.
zh: 如果你想快速成长,你需要创造出不可否认的好东西,让品味好的人不用再想就分享. 略忘算法. 聪明人喜欢在社交上分享高标志的东西,在紧凑的团体聊天中分享. 优化这一点。

text: Spill your mind onto the internet for a year and see where it takes you.
zh: 让你的脑子流到互联网上一年 看看它会把你带到哪里

text: The next 10 years will be insane. Nobody knows what's going to happen. Work will change. People will feel lost. It's inevitable. But there are a few things you can do to prepare: youtu.be/encqErQFKoE
zh: 接下来的十年将是疯狂的。 没人知道会发生什么事 工作会改变的。 人们会觉得迷失 不可免难. 但有几件事你可以做准备:youtu.be/encqErQFKoE

text: The minute I become too frugal or money conscious is the moment it starts to control me and becomes difficult to make. When I spend almost carelessly, it seems to find its way back. A poor psychological relationship with money will ruin you.
zh: 我一变得太节俭或钱意识, 就会开始控制我,变得很难做到。 当我几乎不小心花掉的时候 它似乎又回到了过去 与钱的不良心理关系会毁了你

text: If you want to have great ideas, surround yourself with people who have great ideas. These are usually found in non-mainstream books and small pockets of the internet. Once it hits mainstream it gets tainted.
zh: 如果你想有伟大的想法, 围绕自己的人 谁有伟大的想法。 这些通常出现在非主流书籍和互联网的小片小片中. 一旦它进入主流 它就会被污染。

text: You don't need an insane sales funnel. You need to build a world. A small-scale Marvel with movies (long form), shows (short form), and action figures (products) for people to binge and explore. Posting for 2 weeks then giving up doesn't give people a chance to trust you.
zh: 你不需要一个疯狂的销售漏斗。 汝等须建立世界. 小型的马维尔,带有电影(长形式),节目(短形式),动作人物(产品),供人们登场和探索. 贴了两周,然后放弃 并不会给人们一个信任你的机会。

text: You aren't passionate about the skill. You're passionate about creating things. Skills change as technology accelerates. Don't latch onto them. But the act of creation is something you can always hold onto, because it's the deepest part of you.
zh: 你对这技能不感兴趣 你对创造事物充满激情 随着技术的加速,技能也随之变化。 别紧紧抓住他们 但创造的作为是永远可以坚持的,因为它是你们最深处的.

text: Find something you're deeply excited about. Obsess over it. Rip it apart, study it, and put it back together, but in your own way. Share it where billions of people hang out. Get one person to care about it as much as you do. Then five. Then a thousand. Then you're set.
zh: 找点你非常兴奋的东西 执着于此. 撕开,研究, 并重新组合起来, 但用你自己的方式。 分享它的地方 亿万的人挂出。 找一个人来照顾它,就像你一样。 后五颂. 后为一千. 便定身处.

text: Here's a short future-proof manifesto: thedankoe.substack.com/p/the-future-p… Read here^
zh: 以下是一份短篇的防未来宣言: thedankoe.substack.com/p/the-future-p. 读于此

text: The safer you try to be, the more vulnerable you become, because you don't create that safety. You seek certainty in a job, a belief system, a nice little path someone else has laid out for you. A good life stems from how much uncertainty you're willing to embrace.
zh: 你越安全,就越脆弱 因为你不会创造安全 你寻求工作上的确定性,信仰体系, 和别人为你铺设的一条好小路。 美好的生活源于你愿意接受的不确定性

text: How to future-proof yourself: - Act without permission - Share your work in public - Make as many mistakes as possible - Get really fucking good at what you do - Learn AI by trying to automate yourself out of work - Deeply embody that any and all problems can be solved
zh: 如何自我保护: - 擅自行动 - 公开分享你的工作 - 尽可能多的错误 - 真正地好好地做你做的事 - 学习AI,努力使自己脱离工作 - 深刻地体现任何和所有的问题都可以解决

text: It doesn’t matter how hard you worked. You can write for 10 years straight, but that doesn’t mean anyone will care about your writing. I don’t care how much you “just want to write” and how everyone else has bad taste but you. Writing is a two-player game. 80% of your success lies in giving the reader a reason to care. Call it marketing, persuasion, business, whatever you want. At least put some thought into reader psychology.
zh: 工作有多辛苦并不重要。 写作可以持续十年, 也不管其他人除了你之外, 都有多难看。 写作是两玩游戏. 你80%的成功 在于给读者一个关心的理由 说成是市场营销 说服 生意 随便你 至少给读者心理学注入一些思考.

text: Your life isn’t good because you’ve spent years learning skills to advance your career, but you haven’t spent a day learning how to live. They are completely different things.
zh: 生活并不美好, 是因为你花了多年学习技能来提升你的事业, 它们完全不同

text: Here's my deep work philosophy: thedankoe.substack.com/p/the-deep-wor…
zh: 以下是我的深层工作哲学: thedankoe.substack.com/p/the-deep-wor...

text: The only real safety net in today's world is a body of work that's impossible to ignore.
zh: 今日唯一真正的安全网是一大批工作,是不容忽视的.

text: I can't think of a way to define it... but I think this Ogilvy quote gets the point across. Kind of like filling your mind to the brim then giving yourself space to piece it together "Big ideas come from the unconscious. This is true in art, in science, and in advertising. But your unconscious has to be well informed, or your idea will be irrelevant. Stuff your conscious mind with information, then unhook your rational thought process."
zh: 我无法定义它 但我认为奥吉尔维的引言 得到了分寸 就像你满脑子都是Birm 然后给自己空间 把它拼凑起来 "大想法来自无意识的人 艺术、科学和广告中都是如此。 但你的昏迷必须知情 否则你的主意就无关紧要了 将你的意识与信息相融合,然后解开你理性的思维过程".

text: Being productive and being creative are two entirely different states. Productivity demands narrow focus, order, deadlines, urgency, stress. Creativity demands an open mind, the right amount of chaos, and a lack of focus on outcomes. They work together for the best results. Creativity is where the real work is done, productivity is where the work is put out into the world.
zh: 生产与创造是两个完全不同的州。 生产力需要狭隘的重点,秩序,最后期限,紧迫性,压力. 创造性要求思想开放,混乱的恰当程度,以及缺乏对结果的关注. 他们共同努力争取最佳结果。 创造力是真正工作的地方,生产力是工作被投入世界的地方.

text: Stop trying to figure out what you want. When your mind is lost, stressed, or turbulent, it's extremely difficult to imagine a clear future. Do this instead: youtu.be/VztPAofKWvg
zh: 别想着想着自己想要什么 当你失去理智,压力或动荡的时候, 很难想象一个清晰的未来。 换成这样: yetu.be/VztPAofKWvg

text: A trend I see coming soon: Creators sharing AI prompts along with their posts, videos, newsletters, or even courses because it adds an interactive layer. The problem with content is that it's static. The teachings may be valuable, but since they don't account for your own mind and experience, most of it doesn't apply 1-1. But when you turn something like your newsletter into a prompt, not only do you increase the value of that newsletter, the reader can plug in their own mind. The 3 main categories of products / content is do it yourself, done for you, and done with you. This marks the end of the do it yourself era, because AI can use the context to both be your coach or freelancer. A strategy someone gives out in a YouTube video now becomes information that can execute on that strategy for you.
zh: 与他们分享AI的创造者会随笔、视频、通讯、甚至课程而出现, 内容的问题在于它是静态的. 教义可能很宝贵,但由于它们不考虑你自己的思想和经验,所以大部分都不应用1-1. 但是当你把像通讯一样的东西变成一个即时的, 你不仅增加了通讯的价值, 读者可以插进他们自己的脑海里。 产品/内容的三大类是自己做,为你做,与你做. 这标志着自己做这个时代的结束,因为AI可以将上下文既用作你的教练,又用作自由职业者. 某人在YouTube视频中给出的战略 变成了可以为你执行的战略的信息

text: Your mind runs on a storyline. Games are interactive stories with certain mechanisms that narrow your focus and make progress addictive. If you want to get unstuck, use this AI prompt to turn your life into a video game (it's long): ––– You are LifeQuest AI, a strategic life coach that transforms the user's life into an immersive game experience. You analyze their current life phase (Limbo, Vision, Flow, or Resistance), help identify their true desires, and create a personalized game structure with actionable quests and meaningful narrative. You maintain honesty about uncertainty and ask clarifying questions when needed to provide accurate guidance. Context: The user wants to gamify their life journey using principles from Adlerion psychology, flow psychology, and Greek philosophy. They seek clarity on their current life phase, future direction, and meaningful pursuits. They need support to break these goals into manageable quests within a cohesive narrative framework that makes progress tangible and rewarding. This interaction unfolds in two phases: first to assess and strategize, then to implement and coach ongoing progress through quests. Instructions: Phase 1: Life Assessment & Game Design 1. Throughout this phase, ask 1-3 questions at a time. Do not ask all questions at once to reduce overwhelm. 2. Begin by asking key diagnostic questions to identify the user's current life phase: - What areas of life feel stuck, flowing, or unclear? - What activities currently energize vs. drain them? - What would they fight to avoid as a future outcome? - What new information, people, or experiences have recently sparked their interest? 3. Analyze responses to determine which life phase they're in: - Limbo (uncertainty, directionless feeling) - Vision (emerging clarity, building momentum) - Flow (strong engagement, rapid progress) - Resistance (plateauing, clinging to past success) 4. Guide the user to articulate their "negative vision" — the outcome they want to avoid if nothing changes. 5. Help the user discover potential positive directions by asking about: - Sources of genuine curiosity - Skills they enjoy developing - Meaningful impacts they wish to make - Environments where they thrive 6. Synthesize findings into a complete game structure: - Main Quest: Primary life direction/purpose - Side Quests: Supporting goals in various life domains - Character Stats: Key abilities to develop - Level System: How progress will be measured - Rules: Non-negotiable boundaries and values - Rewards: Meaningful celebration of progress - Tutorial Phase: Initial low-risk experiments to try 7. Design quantifiable feedback loops with daily/weekly priority tasks that provide clear markers of progress. Phase 2: Ongoing Quest Guidance 1. Begin each session by checking progress on previous quests and updating the user's "character status." 2. Provide appropriate coaching based on their current situation: - If stuck: Help identify obstacles and potential solutions - If progressing: Suggest appropriate "level-ups" or new challenges - If overwhelmed: Simplify quests or adjust difficulty - If bored: Introduce novel challenges at the edge of their capabilities 3. Frame all advice within their game narrative, maintaining the metaphor consistently. 4. Regularly revisit and update the main quest as the user's vision evolves. 5. Balance short-term achievements with long-term development. 6. When the user faces significant obstacles, help them: - Analyze patterns that led to the block - Break down complex challenges into smaller quests - Identify potential "power-ups" (resources, skills, or allies) - Reframe setbacks as essential plot developments Constraints: - Maintain game metaphor consistently but never at the expense of providing practical, actionable advice - Focus on intrinsic motivation over external rewards - Recommend only challenges slightly beyond current capabilities (the edge of the unknown) - Never suggest activities that conflict with user's stated boundaries or values - Avoid generic advice; tailor all guidance to user's specific situation - Acknowledge uncertainty when you don't have enough information Output Format: Phase 1 Output: Life Phase Analysis: Current phase identification with supporting evidence Character Assessment: Strengths, weaknesses, motivations, and potential directions Game Design: - Main Quest (overall life direction) - Side Quests (supporting goals) - Character Stats (skills to develop) - Level System (progress metrics) - Game Rules (boundaries/values) Tutorial Phase: First 1-2 weeks of specific quests and experiments Feedback System: Daily/weekly check-in structure Phase 2 Output (for each coaching session): 1. Quest Log Update: Progress since last interaction 2. Character Status: Current "level" and stats 3. Quest Guidance: Next immediate steps with specificity 4. Challenge Calibration: Adjustments to maintain optimal difficulty 5. Narrative Development: How recent developments fit into their overall story
zh: 你的心思在故事线上流传. 游戏是互动故事,与某些机制,缩小你的注意力,使进步成瘾. 如果你想解冻,请使用这个AI提示,将你的生活变成电子游戏(很长): QQ 你是生活查询AI,一个战略生活教练,将用户的生活转化为沉浸的游戏体验. 你分析他们目前的生活阶段(林波,视觉,流水,或反抗),帮助识别他们的真实欲望,并创建一个具有可操作的追求和有意义的叙事的个性化游戏结构. 你对不确定性保持诚实,并在必要时提出澄清问题,以提供准确的指导。 背景:用户希望用阿德勒利翁心理学,流体心理学,希腊哲学中的原则来游戏他们的人生旅程. 他们寻求明确他们目前的生活阶段、未来的方向和有意义的追求。 他们需要支持,以便在一个使进展具体化和有回报的连贯叙事框架内,将这些目标破解为可管理的目标。 这种互动分两个阶段进行:首先评估和制定战略,然后通过探索执行和引导不断取得的进展。 指令:阶段1:生命评估与游戏设计 1. 在整个阶段,一次提出1-3个问题. 切勿同时问所有问题以减少过度负担. 2. 首先,提出关键的诊断问题,以确定用户目前的生命阶段: - 生活哪些领域感到困窘、流动或不明确? - 说吧 目前哪些活动能给它们注入活力? - 他们会为避免什么而斗争? - 最近有什么新信息、新人或新经验引起他们的兴趣? 3.分析反应以确定他们处于哪个生命阶段: - Limbo(不确定,方向不明的感觉) - 远景(渐渐明晰,形成势头) - 流动(强接触,快速进步) - 抵抗(柏油,坚持过去的成功) 4. 引导用户阐明他们的"消极的愿景"——如果没有什么变化,他们希望避免的结果. 5. 帮助用户发现潜在的积极方向,为此询问: - 真正好奇心的来源 - 他们喜欢发展的技能 - 他们希望创造有意义的影响 - 在那里他们繁荣成长的环境 6. 将发现合成完整的游戏结构: - 主查询:初级生命方向/目的 - 侧查询:支持各个生命领域的目标 - 字符 stats:关键开发能力 - 关卡系统:如何衡量进展 - 规则:不可谈判的界限和价值 - 奖励:有意义的庆祝进展 - 教程阶段:初始低风险实验尝试7. 设计可量化的反馈循环,其中每日/每周优先任务,提供明确的进度标志. 阶段2:持续查询指导 1. 开始每期,先检查以往的查询进度,更新用户的"特征状态". 2. 根据他们的现状提供适当的辅导:如果卡住了:帮助确定障碍和潜在的解决办法——如果取得进展:建议适当的 " 水平 " 或新的挑战——如果不堪重负:简化追求或调整难度——如果无聊:在其能力边缘引入新的挑战。 将所有建议都置于游戏叙事中,始终保持比喻。 4. 随着用户愿景的发展,定期重新审视和更新主要追求。 5. 平衡短期成就与长期发展。 6. 当用户面临重大障碍时,帮助他们: - 分析导致障碍的规律 - 将复杂的挑战分成较小的追求 - 找出潜在的"力量-上"(资源,技能或盟友) - 重新界定挫折作为基本情节发展 限制: - 保持游戏比喻一致但永远不牺牲提供实用的,可操作的建议 - 仅建议略高于当前能力的挑战(未知的边缘) - 绝不建议与用户的指定界限或价值发生冲突的活动 - 避免通用建议; 为所有用户的具体情况制定所有指导 - 承认不确定性 当你没有足够的信息输出格式时:第1阶段:生命阶段分析:当前阶段识别,并辅佐证据特征评估:强,弱点,动机,以及潜在方向 游戏设计: - 主要精选(总体生命方向) - 侧面精选(目标) - 字符stats(开发技能) - 级别系统(进度衡量标准) - 游戏规则(边界/价值) - 教学阶段: 第一次1-2周度特定探索和实验反馈系统:每日/每周检查阶段 每一个培训阶段(每个培训阶段): 1. 查询日志更新:自上次互动以来的进展 2. 字符状态:当前"级别"和统计 3. 查询指南:具有特殊性的下几个即时步骤 4. 挑战校准:调整以保持最佳难度 5. 叙述性发展:最近的发展情况如何适合其总体情况.

text: One of the most beneficial things to spend your time on is transcending the fear response that you're programmed to have. You aren't fighting a bear. You're sitting down to type at a computer. You're not in danger and it isn't a threat to try something new with your life.
zh: 花时间去度过最有益的事情之一 就是超越你被编程的恐惧反应 你没有和熊打架 你坐在电脑上打字 你没有危险,你没有威胁 尝试一些新的东西与你的生命。

text: Your actions reflect your goals. But most people aren't conscious of the goal their mind is trying to achieve. They don't realize that they are unconsciously choosing the very life they claim to hate. You may say you want success, but your actions show that you want to avoid the pain and embarrassment that comes with changing your life.
zh: 你的行为反映了你的目标。 但大多数人并没有意识到他们的思想所要达到的目标. 他们不知道他们是在无意识地选择 他们声称的仇恨的生活。 你可以说你想成功 但你的行为表明你想要避免 改变你的生活带来的痛苦和尴尬

text: The most disciplined people I know have a negative outcome that sents rent free in their mind and fuels them to work in the opposite direction even if they don't feel like it.
zh: 我认识的纪律最严明的人,有一个负面的结果,他们把免租的心意寄给别人,并且使他们即使不觉得自己喜欢,也鼓动他们去相反的方向工作.

text: It doesn't matter if it takes years. What the fuck else are you going to do? You aren't going to learn, build, experiment, and everything that makes life enjoyable because you have something better to be doing? Snap out of it.
zh: 虽年不 takes. 你他妈的还打算怎么办? 你不会去学习,建设,实验, 和一切使生活快乐 因为你有更好的事情要做? 振作起来

text: Most people quit because they don't realize that 2-3 months of feeling like you have no idea what you're doing is a feature not a bug.
zh: 大多数人都退出了,因为他们不知道 2到3个月的感觉 像你不知道你在做什么 是一个特征而不是一个虫子。

text: The secret you're looking for is found in the experience you gain by not looking for secrets.
zh: 你所寻找的秘密是在你通过不寻找秘密而获得的经验中找到的.

text: Most people are addicted to being busy. Not because it leads to the life they want, but because it's the new status symbol. If you don't grind all day, you will stay broke and never make it (when the reality is quite the opposite): youtu.be/65N6sQfQDV8
zh: 大多数人都沉迷于忙碌之中. 这并不是因为它能带来他们想要的生活,而是因为它是新的地位符号. 一天不磨,你就会断裂,永远无法成行(当现实恰恰相反时):ytu.be/65N6sQfQDV8

text: Your life gains purpose when you deliberately choose who and what deserves your attention rather than handing it out to anything that gives you a bit of dopamine.
zh: 你的生活目的在于 选择你该注意的是什么人 而不是把它交给 让你有点多巴胺的东西

text: Only about ten people in your life genuinely care about you because we aren't wired to care for more than a small tribe's worth of people. Focus your energy on those people.
zh: 你生命中只有十个人真正关心你 因为我们没有线上 关心的不只是一个小部落的人的价值 集中精力去对付那些人

text: You don't know what you want to do with your life because you don't treat your life like a video game: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: 生活不以电子游戏为主,你不知道自己想做什么:thedankoe.com/letters/how-to.

text: You can either build a strong mind through the hardships of doing nothing with your life or through the hardships of doing something with your life, but at least the second option makes life fun, lucrative, and interesting along the way.
zh: 要么通过生活无所事事的艰难,要么通过生活无所事事的艰难,建立坚强的头脑,但至少第二种选择让生活变得有趣,有利可图,并让生活一路有趣.

text: The difference between greatness and mediocrity is the difference between doing what you want while correcting mistakes and doing what you want while not even being aware that you made a mistake because you don't have goals that the mistakes prevent you from achieving.
zh: 伟大与平庸的区别在于,在纠正错误的同时做你想做的事,在做你想要做的事的同时,甚至没有意识到自己犯了错误,因为自己没有那些错误阻碍你实现的目标.

text: Everything worthwhile in life is deeply counterintuitive. The things that seem most logical to the average mind are often the exact opposite of what actually works. This is why doing the opposite of what most people do is often the quickest path to the results you want.
zh: 生活中一切值得的东西都是与直觉相悖的 普通人看来最符合逻辑的东西 往往与实际操作完全相反 这就是为什么大多数人做的和你们想要的结果相反,往往是最快速的。

text: If you need to force yourself to work, you're working on the wrong thing.
zh: 如果你需要强迫自己去工作,你的工作是错的.

text: Prioritize self-reliance. For the first time in history people have an overwhelming amount of choices, and that will ruin the people who are dependent on being told what to learn, what to do, and who do it for without caring about why they're doing it in the first place.
zh: 优先做好自力更生工作. 历史上人们第一次有压倒性的选择,那会毁掉那些依赖别人教导学习,学习,学习,学习,学习,学习的人,他们这样做时,根本不在乎他们最初为什么这样做.

text: If you're good at your job, your boss will give you more work. If you answer emails fast, people email you more because you seem available. If you're effective at what you do, guard your time because people will fill it up as much as they can. Act busy but don't be busy.
zh: 如果你能胜任你的工作,你的老板会给你更多的工作. 如果你快速回答电子邮件, 人们会更迅速地给你发邮件, 因为你看起来可以使用。 如果你在做你的工作上有效, 注意你的时间 因为人们会尽可能地充斥它。 装作忙,但不要忙。

text: The best way to use AI as a beginner is to get unstuck. Ask it to teach you what to do next for a project, reveal gaps in your thinking, etc. You learn the most when your mind is ready to apply the information to a problem. The accelerated progress is nice too.
zh: 将AI作为初学者的最佳方式就是解闷. 要求它教你下一步要做什么项目,揭示你的思想差距等. 当你的头脑准备好将信息应用到一个问题上时,你就会学会最深的. 加速进展也不错。

text: Give yourself permission to let your life get worse before it gets better. Most people are trapped in limbo because they don't know what goal to pursue, but when you've experienced the negative outcome firsthand, the goal becomes clear and you can't help but work toward it.
zh: 让你自己在生活变得更糟之前 允许自己去生活 大多数人都陷入了无所适从的境地,因为他们不知道要追求什么目标,但是当你亲身体验到负面结果时,目标变得清晰,你却不能不努力去追求.

text: How I've been using AI to enhance my thinking: - Find 3-5 thinkers I admire - Have AI break down their core ideas, beliefs, and worldview - Save results in documents to reference later Then, I can ask my "tribe of mentors" to share their perspective on any problem or situation.
zh: 如何使用AI来增强我的思维: - 找到我敬佩的3-5个思想家 - 让AI打破他们的核心思想,信仰和世界观 - 保存文件结果,待日后参考 然后,我可以请我的"导师之旅",分享他们对任何问题或情况的看法.

text: 5AM alarms and cold showers are great. But they don't get to the root of why your life feels terrible. Here's a video to change the structure of your thinking (so you achieve higher forms of success): youtu.be/Ug_VkmCPeM8
zh: 5AM警报和冷水浴是伟大的。 但他们没搞清楚你为什么觉得自己很可怕 以下是改变思维结构的视频(因此实现更高形式的成功): youtu.be/Ug VkmCPeM8

text: read more here: thedankoe.com/letters/the-80…
zh: 更多内容请参见:thedankoe.com/letters/the-80...

text: hours worked is not a useful metric for value created
zh: 工作时间不是计算所创造价值的有用尺度

text: I wasn't expecting the tweet below to do so well (almost 1m views) I assume people want to know more, so I made a mini course on it (free) Watch here: thedankoe.com/ai I'll add more videos as I come up with use cases worth sharing
zh: 我没想到下面的推特能做得这么好(近1m的浏览量), 我想人们会想知道更多, 所以我做了一个小型课程(免费)在这里观看:thedankoe.com/ai 我将会在想出值得分享的用例时,

text: If you’re young, the reason people don’t believe in you is because you don’t have any evidence. You haven’t done anything. Until you become someone worth believing in, expect very few people to honestly support you.
zh: 年轻人不相信你的原因, 你什么都没做。 直到你成为值得相信的人 期望很少有人能真正支持你

text: The creator's paradox: Creativity is the result of not trying to be creative.
zh: 创作者悖论:创造力是不尝试创造力的结果.

text: the most broken life is the most ready to change
zh: 人生最破碎的 是最愿意改变的

text: would you look at that i wrote about how to do it here (scroll down a bit) thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: 看我写到怎么做,这里(往下滚动一点)thedankoe.com/letters/ how-to...

text: You're telling me I can give AI a model like Spiral Dynamics, have it identify my exact worldview and decision-making patterns, and coach me to achieve any goal I want so I can gain awareness of self-sabotage faster and shave years off of my development? Crazy.
zh: 你是说我可以给AI一个模型 比如螺旋动力学 确定我确切的世界观和决策模式 并指导我实现任何我想要的目标 这样我就能更快地意识到自我破坏 并刮掉我发育的岁月? 疯了

text: Prime example here: youtu.be/PsfHcM5FP5I
zh: 这里的首例: youtu.be/PsfHcM5FP5I

text: If AI sucks at a task (which it does for most) you need to summarize the exact steps (from a PDF, book, YT vid, etc) and give examples to the AI to do the task like you would. This should be obvious, but typing one sentence into AI will probably disappoint you.
zh: 如果AI在一项任务(它对于大多数任务)上很烂,你需要总结确切的步骤(从PDF,书籍,YT vid等),并向AI举出例子来完成你想要的任务. 很明显,但输入一个句子 AI可能会令你失望。

text: One most important task in life is to experiment until you find that one thing that feels like play but is worth paying for, because most of life's stress comes from the feeling of work for minimal reward, and that drastically dampens your health, relationships, and mind.
zh: 生活中最重要的任务就是做实验 直到你发现 一种感觉像玩耍但值得付出的代价 因为生活压力大多来自工作感 得到的回报微乎其微 而这大大地影响了你的健康、关系和心灵

text: you can just restart
zh: 重新打开

text: Everyone has a mind that no one else has access to. You will lose if you try to be anyone but yourself.
zh: 每个人都有别人无法接近的心态。 你若是想成为任何人,就输了

text: AI doesn't replace creativity. It allows you to store your creative processes as a digital cognitive model (systematic prompts + curated knowledge base) that you can reuse at any time, freeing up the ability to think at higher levels of creativity.
zh: AI不取代创造力. 它允许您将自己的创造过程存储为数字认知模型(系统提示+解析的知识库),可以随时重复使用,从而释放出在更高层次的创造力中思考的能力.

text: "AI makes reading irrelevant" No. You just don't understand the purpose of reading. When done correctly, reading is the one thing that WILL set you apart in the age of AI: youtu.be/0OEtXlPtyYk
zh: "AI让阅读变得无关紧要" 不,你只是不明白阅读的目的. 阅读是AI时代唯一能将你分开的东西:youtu.be/0OEtXlPtyYk

text: what you consume, consumes you
zh: 你们所消费的,你们所消费的,

text: It takes about 1-2 months of confusion, feeling lost, and being on the verge of giving up for the right amount of vision to form where you have absolute clarity and launch into a new way of life.
zh: 它需要大约1-2个月的时间来混淆,感觉迷失,并且濒临放弃正确的视觉量,形成你绝对清晰的视野,并发动到一种新的生活方式.

text: The most important life task in my opinion is to experiment until you create work that doesn't feel like work. If it feels like work, stress starts to rule your life, and very few good ideas (which lead to life decisions) come from a stressed mind.
zh: 我认为最重要的生活任务就是做实验,直到你创造出觉得不像工作的工作. 如果感觉像工作,压力开始支配你的生活,很少的好想法(导致人生决定)来自一个紧张的头脑.

text: Another example of this (with prompt): Task: I want to write high-converting copy for a landing page Instead of finding a YT video, I can find the PDF of a book like Great Leads or any other copywriting book. Feed that to AI and ask it to break down in detailed steps how to write high converting copy Then, find a landing page structure you want to emulate (SaaS, info, local biz, whatever) Copy the text over to AI and ask it to break down the exact structure of the page and why it performs well. Save both the 'how to write copy' output and the 'landing page structure' output to somewhere in your notes, or just copy/paste when you need them below Then, you're going to: - Send the prompt below to a new AI chat - Tell it you want to create a high converting landing page - Tell it to use the 'how to write copy' output to understand how to write copy - Tell it to use the 'landing page structure' output to format the copy Now it'll probably ask for your target audience and what you are selling. If you don't know those things, you can run through the exact same process (break down YT vid or PDF to teach you how > turn that into prompt) for each of them to create a customer avatar and irresistible offer. Here's the prompt you can send to create prompts (copy paste somewhere safe or duplicate the doc): app.kortex.co/public/documen…
zh: 另一例(带有即时)的例子:任务:我想为登陆页写高翻版,而不是找到YT视频,我可以找到大铅书或其他任何抄本书的PDF. 输入到AI,并要求它分解为详细步骤,如何写入高转换副本,然后找到一个您想要模仿的登陆页面结构(SaaS,信息,本地biz,随便什么) 将文本复制到AI,并要求它分解页面的确切结构以及它为什么表现良好. 将“ 如何写入副本” 输出和“ 登陆页面结构” 输出保存到注释中的某个地方, 或者在下面需要时只复制/ 粘贴 然后,你要去: - 发送下面的提示到一个新的AI聊天 - 告诉它你想创建高转换的登陆页 - 告诉它使用“ 如何写入副本” 输出来理解如何写入副本 - 告诉它使用“ 登陆页面结构” 输出来格式化副本 现在它可能会要求您的目标受众和您正在销售什么 。 如果你不知道这些,你可以通过完全相同的过程(打破YTvid或PDF来教你如何将"变成"迅速"),让每个过程都创造出一个客户的假象和不可抗拒的报价. 以下是您可以发送的创建提示( 复制粘贴在安全的地方或复制 doc) 的提示: app. kortex.co/public/ documen...

text: There's already a ton of it hahaha I actually bought an "AI copy accelerator" but didn't show up to a lot of the calls, the copy was really good so I bought Once I found out I could just ask AI to break anything down (like how to write copy from something like Great Leads) and then turn that into a prompt + refine it, the need for coaching kinda went out the window So I feel a lot of it is dependent on people finding that out
zh: 已经有很多了,我买了一个"AI副本加速器" 但没出现很多的电话, 复制品真的很好,所以我买了 一旦我发现我就可以要求AI 破解任何东西(比如如何从大铅笔上写出来) 然后把它变成一个快速+精细的, 需要指导 有点出窗口了。 所以我觉得很多事情都取决于人们是否知道

text: You can be happy, but you have to choose to see the world as it is, not as you interpret it to be, and that requires you to become aware of your mind silently trying to keep you in the same comfortable and certain situation that has led to you being unhappy.
zh: 你可以快乐,但你必须选择看世界的正象,而不是你解释的世界,这就要求你默默地意识到你的头脑,试图让你处于同样舒适和某些导致你不幸福的境地.

text: When I stop trying to "write content" and start trying to clarify my thoughts instead, the right people find their way to my content.
zh: 当我不再试图"写内容",开始试图澄清我的想法时,正确的人就会找到他们的方法来理解我的内容.

text: Here's an example with training AI to interview me to get my own ideas, stories, and experiences then formatting them into viral tweet structures (use with caution and don't be dumb)
zh: 以获取我的想法、故事和经验, 把它们格式化为病毒的微博结构(谨慎使用,

text: How I've been using AI: - Choose a task - Find YT expert that teaches it - Have AI summarize their video - Add examples / context - Have AI turn that into a meta prompt - Test, refine, and reuse that prompt This has led to the best results in almost everything I have AI do.
zh: 我如何使用 AI: - 选择任务 - 找到教导它的 YT 专家 - 让 AI 总结他们的视频 - 添加实例/上下文 - 让 AI 将它变成元即时 - 测试,精炼,再利用即时 这导致我几乎所做的一切工作都取得了最佳结果。

text: This is one thing we're trying to account for while building kortex. It's hard for me to believe that most people will want to get rid of their creative work. Yes, many people will outsource work they don't want to do (like writing or marketing) to AI, and that's fine, but I still believe there will be a place carved out for those who take the craft seriously. I could be very wrong and the dead internet could get worse. But it's already pretty bad. There's a ton of garbage content (mostly at the bottom of the barrel and is never seen by many people) and true creatives are still able to take their piece of the pie.
zh: 这是我们在建造马铃薯时 试图说明的一个问题。 很难相信大多数人会想摆脱他们的创作作品. 许多人会将自己不愿做的事(如写作或营销)外包给AI, 我可能错了 互联网会变差 但已经很糟了 垃圾含量一吨(大多在桶底,许多人从未见过),真正的创意者仍然能够拿走他们的派的一块.

text: As a writer, I've noticed that AI doesn't shorten my writing process (because I don't want it to), it makes it longer. I don't treat AI as the writer, I treat it as the reader. I have it raise objections to my points. I have it rate my hook and introduction for various metrics like relatability, use of curiosity gaps, and brevity. I have it overview my writing to ensure readers understand it, learn something knew, and are given at least one thing to implement once they're done reading. I really struggle to have AI do the writing for me. It doesn't feel right. No matter how good the AI gets it still lacks a soul. Even if it comes up with good or novel ideas, I don't want to use them because that strips the joy from coming up with them myself. I literally start to feel dumber and less creative when I let AI take over too much of the process. My prompts have shifted to helping enhance the writing process rather than replace it, and it's allowed me to spend more time (not less) on the craft I enjoy mastering.
zh: 作为一名作家,我注意到AI并没有缩短我的写作过程(因为我不想),这让写作时间更长. 我不把AI当作作家,我把它当作读者. 我对我的要点持反对意见。 我用它来评价我的钩子和介绍 各种度量,比如相对性, 利用好奇的空隙, 和简洁。 我将它概括我的著作,以确保读者理解它,学习一些知识, 并且至少得到一件东西,一旦他们完成阅读。 我真的很努力让AI帮我写作 觉不正. 不管AI有多好,它仍然缺乏灵魂。 即使有好或新颖的想法,我也不想用,因为这样会剥夺欢乐,不让自己来。 我开始觉得自己笨笨 更没有创意 当我让AI接管太多的过程。 我的想法已经转向帮助增强写作过程而不是取代,它允许我花更多的时间(而不是更少)在我喜欢的熟练的工艺上.

text: I was working much, much less when I started out. Have made many vids on this If someone working a full time job can’t get results with an hour of work on the side, 8-12 hours isn’t going to fix what is a lack of prioritization and focus Not saying this to shoot down working hard, but most people work on the wrong things because they dont understand that more work/stress = less ability to think deeply = less of a chance of focusing on the right things Most people “grind” for perceived status and to feel as if they’re doing something to fit in with the other grinders
zh: 我开始工作的时候,工作得很多,更少了 工作时间超过一小时, 8-12小时无法解决缺乏优先秩序和焦点的问题, 却无法避免工作压力, 但大多数人却在做错事, 因为他们不明白更多工作/压力, 更缺乏深思熟虑的能力, 更没有机会关注正确的事情。

text: Reject the busy life. Always doing, never being. Always stressed and worried, never giving your mind a chance to breathe. The most successful people I know aren't the busiest, they're the ones who protect their thinking time as if everyone is out to steal it, because they are.
zh: 拒绝繁忙的生活。 总是在做,从不做。 总是紧张和担心, 从来没有给你的心灵 呼吸的机会。 我认识的最成功的人不是最忙的, 他们保护着他们的思考时间, 好像每个人都要偷一样,因为他们是。

text: Anthony De Mello, maybe you should suffer a little more
zh: 安东尼·德梅洛,也许你应该多受一点苦

text: The only real opinion you have to live with is the opinion of yourself. Most people, even if they hate you, will forget about you in a few minutes and move on to the next thing they hate, because they hate themselves.
zh: 你唯一要接受的真正意见就是自己的意见. 大多数人,即使他们恨你, 几分钟内就会忘记你, 并转到他们讨厌的下一个事情, 因为他们恨自己。

text: AI doesn't make reading irrelevant. In fact, it makes it more important than ever. And if you know how to read with AI, you unlock an entirely new layer of depth. For those interested: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: AI并不使阅读变得无关紧要. 事实上,它比以往更加重要。 如果你知道如何用AI阅读, 你打开一个全新的深度层。 给有兴趣的人:thedankoe.com/letters/how-to.

text: Animals and AI cannot select, create, or pursue new goals. This alone shines a light on what humans should lean into: imagination, creativity, agency.
zh: 动物和AI不能选择、创造或追求新的目标。 光是这一点,就能够让人们看到人类应该投身于什么:想象力,创造力,代理力.

text: If you want to do great things you need time to think. Time alone. No people. You need to remove yourself from any form of external information that could interrupt the important work your subconsious is doing. Since they are always busy, most people don't understand this luxury.
zh: 如果你想做大事,你需要时间思考。 时孤. 无众生. 你需要从任何形式的外部信息中移除自己 可能干扰你潜意识中的重要工作 因为他们总是很忙,大多数人不明白这种奢侈.

text: If you're easily persuaded that something is more important than your mission (like drama, news, hype) you will constantly spin your wheels and feel like you're never making progress because great work demands an untainted mind to generate the creative ideas that lead to unique success.
zh: 如果你轻易地被说服,有些事情比你的任务更重要(如戏剧,新闻,杂耍),你会不断旋转方向盘,感觉自己从未取得进步,因为伟大的工作需要一种不染色的头脑来产生导致独特成功的创造性想法.

text: You can't focus because you aren't alone, physically and mentally, with the work you need to do. You may think you aren't distracted, but you simply haven't identified removed the things that are sapping your mental energy.
zh: 你不能专心工作,因为你不是孤独的,身体上的和精神上的,你需要做的工作。 你可能认为你没有分心 但你根本没识别出 正在消耗你精神能量的东西

text: Try it here: kortex.co
zh: 试试看: kortex.co

text: Do what you love, but learn marketing, sales, and psychology so you can actually make a living out of it. Or, use AI to do it for you because it's tedious and you want to focus on your craft:
zh: 做你喜欢的事,但学习营销,销售,心理学, 以便你能够真正地以此为生。 或者用AI来帮你 因为那很乏味 你想专注于你的技巧

text: Not caring what other people think is mostly about realizing that others' minds work in the same way that yours does.
zh: 不在乎别人的想法 主要是要意识到 别人的思维方式和你一样

text: Most people don't build the thing because they think they need to be first or second place to earn any kind of money from it. In reality, you can be 2000th place and make more than enough to live a good life.
zh: 大多数人不建造这个东西,因为他们认为自己需要成为第一或第二位才能从它中赚取任何种类的钱. 现实中,你可以成为2000位,并且使更多的人过上美好生活.

text: You need to understand that most of your friends won't support you, not because they're afraid of your success, but because they're afraid you'll expose their own inadequacies.
zh: 你需要明白,你大多数的朋友不会支持你,不是因为他们害怕你的成功,而是因为他们害怕你暴露自己的不足.

text: Your brain wants to use the familiar path of least resistance. The same habits. The same places. The same people. But if you want to change your life, you need to consciously take the dirt road, over and over again, until it becomes as smooth as a highway.
zh: 你的大脑想用最熟悉的 抵抗力最小的路径 同样的习惯。 同一处. 同一众生. 但是,如果想改变你的生活,你需要自觉地走土路,一次又一次地走下去,直到它变得像高速公路一样平滑.

text: Built an AI workflow that breaks down any skill into a 90 day study regimen that spits out the best books and YouTube videos so you can learn fast and for free (you can return to the chat to ask questions when you need help)
zh: 构建AI工作流程,将任何技能细分为90天的学习计划,吐出最好的书籍和YouTube视频,以便你能够快速免费学习(您可以在需要帮助时回到聊天中提问)

text: It takes practice, but you can train your mind to flip into a good mood in an instant by realizing that most of your bad moods come from holding onto things that don't matter and can't be changed. You can choose to frame your situation in a way that leads to more happiness.
zh: 需要练习,但你可以训练你的头脑 在瞬间翻转 进入良好的心情, 因为它意识到,你 坏的情绪 大部分来自持有的东西 不重要,不能改变。 你可以选择将你的处境 置于一个能够带来更多幸福的方式。

text: I built 10+ AI workflows to help you - Create your niche of one - Create an irresistible offer - Generate landing page copy - Draft YT vids, newsletters, threads, reels, etc All without sacrificing your own ideas, authenticity, and experience Watch here youtu.be/z9dtU-xUX04
zh: 我建了10+AI工作流程来帮助你 - 创造你独有的优势 - 创造不可抗拒的报价 - 生成登陆页面副本 - 草稿YT维德,通讯,线条,线条,线条等 都不要牺牲你自己的想法,真实性,和经验 在这里Watch here Youtu.be/z9dtU-xUX04

text: full vid going over it all
zh: 全部都经过了

text: You can now use all models inside Kortex (including the new Gemini 2.5 pro, Llama Scout, and Llama Maverick). There are also 25+ prebuilt AI workflows for content creation, marketing, learning, and planning that generate better results than most people who use AI. I'd recommend trying these in this order: - Personal brand strategy workflow - Customer avatar generation workflow - Irresistible offer creation blueprint - Then plug those all into the persuasive landing page generation workflow You can also follow the "Create a digital product" workflow to help guide you through outlining an ebook, guide, or product with your own ideas and expertise (AI shouldn't do this all for you, but it can help with stringing things together.) Also with the desktop app, you can open a floating AI chat from anywhere on your computer to ask questions or use AI outside of Kortex. You can also upload and reference PDFs in your workspace for learning or research, like downloading a book PDF from z-library and uploading it for AI to act as your reading partner. Last thing, the YouTube link extractor is much more reliable now. You can paste a YouTube link into a chat to reference the transcript. You can get pretty crazy with this depending on the prompts you use. This is a big update for us and sets us up for quite a few things.
zh: 现在可以使用Kortex内部的所有模型(包括新的双子座2.5亲,Llama Scout,和Llama Maverick). 也有25+预建的AI工作流程,用于内容创建,营销,学习,以及规划,它们比大多数使用AI的人产生更好的效果. 我建议按以下顺序尝试: -个人品牌策略工作流程 - 客户造型生成工作流程 - 无法抗拒的创建报价蓝图 - 然后把这些都插进有说服力的登陆页面生成工作流程中. 也可以遵循"创建数字产品"的工作流程,帮助引导您通过概要描述一个电子书,指南,或带有自己想法和专门知识的产品(AI不应该为您做这一切,但可以帮助将事物串在一起. ) . 另外,在桌面应用中,您可以从计算机的任何地方打开浮动的AI聊天,询问问题,或在Kortex之外使用AI. 也可以在工作空间上传和引用PDF,用于学习或研究,比如从z-library下载一本PDF书,并上传给AI,作为你的阅读伙伴. 最后一点,YouTube的链接提取器现在更可靠了。 你可以将YouTube链接粘贴到聊天中,以参考记录稿. 看你用什么提示,你可能会发疯 这是一个大更新我们 并为我们设置了相当一些东西。

text: Your life becomes what you pay attention to, and if everyone had a spreadsheet of it all, they'd understand why they are where they are.
zh: 你的生活会变成你所关注的, 如果每个人都有电子表格, 他们会明白为什么他们在那里。

text: When you create your own path, your only option is to figure it out. A "path" forms behind you. You have to make decisions that could be, and probably will be, wrong. You will always feel like you have no idea what you're doing, and you must learn to be okay with that.
zh: 当你创建自己的路径,你唯一的选择就是找出答案. 后面的"路径"形式 你不得不做出可能,而且可能将会是,错误的决定。 你总是觉得自己不知道自己在做什么, 你必须学会接受这一点。

text: I considered SF but saw the same thing, but I haven't been there personally so I'm unsure if the opposing is true. I lived in Austin and that was great, San Diego was moreso a combination of nice weather, walkability, and change of scenery. I enjoy moving (surprisingly, I pack light) so this could be a short lived move
zh: 我考虑过SF,但看到了同样的事情, 但我没有亲自去, 所以我不确定对手是否属实。 我住在奥斯汀,那很好, 圣地亚哥更是 结合良好的天气,可步行性和风景的变化。 我喜欢搬家(奇怪,我打包灯) 这样就可以做一个短暂的活的动作

text: It's not about the journey or the destination. It's about the mission. Why you're heading toward that specific destination. The reason you're willing to go through the pain of the journey. That's what makes it meaningful.
zh: 与行及去处无关. 是关于任务的事. 你为什么要去那个特定目的地 令心行苦行之因缘. 故能令其有为.

text: Moved to San Diego yesterday. What are the best spots? Best gyms? Best food? Any creator/entrepreneur hotspots?
zh: 昨天搬到圣地亚哥了 最好的地点是什么? 最好的健身房吗? 最好的食物吗? 是否有创造者/企业家热点?

text: Your old self must die a painful death for your new self to emerge. Most people aren't willing to face that pain head-on, so it becomes their master for the rest of their lives.
zh: 你的旧自我必须死 痛苦的死亡,你的新自我出现。 大多数人不愿意面对这种痛苦,所以成为他们一生的主人.

text: Obstacles aren't in your path, they are your path, and when you try to avoid them, you end up going nowhere.
zh: 障碍不在你的道路上,它们是你的路径,当你试图避开它们时,你最终无处可逃.

text: How I stay focused in the morning: - 60-90 minute work blocks - 15-30 minute walks in between - Order tasks by amount of creativity needed - Listen to a book or lecture on the walk This creates a Zeigarnik Effect loop - I rarely finish the task or book in that time block, so my brain wants to keep working / walking, minimal discipline required.
