zh: 早上我如何专心工作:60-90分钟工作区 -- -- 15-30分钟走进 -- -- 需要多少创造力来安排任务 -- -- 听书或散步讲座 这创造了一个Zeigarnik效应循环——我很少在那时段块里完成任务或书本,所以我的大脑想要继续工作/走路,需要的纪律最少.

text: this book feels good to hold
zh: 这本书很适合拿

text: If the average person sees work as a curse and work is a necessary part of life then there isn't anything better to do than to focus for 2-4 years on creating your own work that doesn't feel like work.
zh: 如果普通人把工作视为诅咒 工作是生活的必要部分 那么没有什么比 专注于创造自己不觉得工作的工作 更好的了

text: The tweet above is an ugly version of chapter one of Purpose & Profit. Read my new book free (PDF) or paid (paperback): thedankoe.com/purpose
zh: 以上微博为"目的与利润"第一章丑陋版本. 免费阅读我的新书 (PDF)或付费(纸背):thedankoe.com/目的

text: Successful people don't feel the constant need to retire or take unnecessary vacations because they've filled their day with work that they don't want to escape from.
zh: 成功的人并不觉得经常需要退休或休不必要的假期,因为他们已经用他们不想逃避的工作来填满自己的一天.

text: One thing I learned with the AOF launch is that word of mouth marketing is extremely difficult to nail. It's much more than 'launch a good product and expect people to spread it to millions of others.' And "having a large audience" is only an advantage if you promote as much as you possibly can. Whether you're self-published or go with a large publisher, you have to sell. Hard. I'll aim for a NYT bestseller with my next book. The team I have working on it has been building up leads, distribution beyond my audience, strategizing positioning + angles + hooks, and more for the past 6 months, and we're still 6 or more months out from launch. These little books like P&P (I have 4 more planned) are just outlets to write what I want to write without having to worry about all of the nooks and crannies that come along with marketing.
zh: 我从AOF的发布会上学到的一件事 就是口传营销这个词是很难被钉住的 这不仅仅是“推出一个好产品, 而"拥有大量观众"只是只要你尽可能多地推广就具有优势. 无论是自发出版还是与大型出版商同行,都要卖掉. 难曰. 我将瞄准纽约T的畅销书 与我的下一本书。 我所研究的团队一直在建立线索,在观众范围以外进行分发,策略定位+角度+钩子,过去6个月里,更多时间,我们离发射还有6个或更多月. 这些小书,如P&P(我还有4本计划),只是用来写我想写的,而不必担心那些随着市场营销而来的插头和crannies。

text: Purpose & Profit is one of those books I hope is passed around in small circles over the years. No big launch. No early reviews. No sending to influencers. I don't even have a copy yet, I made it live when it was ready. May be shooting myself in the foot, but we will see.
zh: 希望这些年能以小圈子来传播, 没有大发射。 没有早期审查。 不许向影响者发送信号 我还没有拷贝,我把它活下来了 可能正在射伤我脚 但我们会看到

text: A job is work you do to survive, but often unpleasant. A career is work you tolerate for the eventual promise of more status and money. A calling is work you discover and can't pull yourself away from. Even if it takes years, try everything until you find that one thing.
zh: 工作是工作,你为生存, 但常常不愉快。 事业就是你们为了获得更多地位和金钱而容忍的工作。 呼唤就是发现你无法逃避的工作 即使要花几年时间 也尽量找回一切 直到找到那一样东西

text: If your entire day is made up of working on tasks you don't care about, you are creating a life you won't care about.
zh: 如果你整天的工作都是你不在乎的 你就会创造出你不在乎的生活

text: “Why” content “How” product
zh: “为什么”内容“如何”产品

text: Download the PDF or purchase on Amazon here: thedankoe.com/purpose
zh: 下载 PDF 或购买到 Amazon 上: thedankoe.com/ 目的

text: I wrote a second book. A short and philosophical take on money and discovering your life's work. I made it free because this is one of those things I just want in as many hands as possible. The paperback is not free for clear reasons. Links below to read:
zh: 我写了第二本书 简短的哲学性 利用金钱 发现你一生的作品 我把它释放了,因为这是我想要的 之一 在尽可能多的人手中。 纸背不自由,原因明确. 链接如下:

text: Self-confidence comes in 2 parts: 1) Convincing your mind to see a different perspective that makes self-doubt seem irrational. 2) Doing the uncomfortable thing over and over again until you realize it wasn't that uncomfortable in the first place.
zh: 自信分两部分:1)说服你的思想去看待不同的视角,使自我怀疑显得不合理. 2)反复做不舒服的事情,直到你意识到一开始并不那么不舒服.

text: Most people aren't as original as you think they are. They're just reading little-known books, having nuanced conversations, and giving themselves time to think. They are brutally aware that being stuck on their phone and doing busy work all day ruins the quality of your mind.
zh: 大多数人不像你想象的那么独创 他们只是阅读鲜为人知的书籍,有细微的谈话,并给自己时间思考. 他们残酷地意识到,被困在电话上,整天忙着工作,会破坏你的思想品质.

text: The fastest path to making better choices is to think about your ideal life before every single decision you make until it becomes a habit.
zh: 做出更好的选择的最快途径是在你做出的每一决定之前,先思考你的理想生活,直到它成为习惯.

text: I feel like AI's impact on writing is vastly overhyped. And that's coming from someone building an AI writing software. First, there is already too much content. We can't read it all. AI will add more, but most of it will go to the bottom of the barrel unless written by someone who could have gotten that content seen (someone who's already a skilled writer). Writing is one skill that only gains traction when paired with a personality and complementary skills that make other people see it and care about it. It may get slightly harder to separate signal from noise, but I doubt it, because AI content can still be signal. Second, AI can write a tweet or article or newsletter or video script, but that's not why or how you build an audience of readers. Your "brand" is a culmination of everything you've put out. Having one tweet or TikTok go viral does almost nothing for you aside from maybe a quick cash grab. Most viral content is shallow and low IQ. That's not a foundation for a meaningful long-term brand. Most worthwhile content is not viral. Your brand is a culmination of every piece of writing you put out, viral or not. It's the personal or organizational philosophy, meaning, and vision. Your writing is a thread in that tapestry woven over time. Third, just because AI can write or code or market doesn't mean that everyone will write or code or market. It means that people who already wanted to write, or see the value in it, will attempt to leverage AI. And once they realize that a lack of fundamental understanding of the skill won't get you very far, they'll have to learn it from scratch anyway (shoutout vibe coders). AI is revolutionary. There's no question about it. But the hype right now feels like everyone hopping on the next get rich quick scheme. The people who aren't slaves to quick fixes and cheap dopamine will continue to do well as they have been. In reality, this is more like the next evolution of how we write. Pen and paper > typewriter > keyboard > software assisted > AI assisted. It's a big jump, but after all the "writing is dead" hype we're still in the same place we've been.
zh: 我觉得AI对写作的影响 已经非常夸张了 而这来自于一个 建造AI写作软件的人。 首先,已经有很多内容。 无法全读. AI会增加更多内容,但大部分内容会进入桶底,除非是本可以让内容被看到的人(某个已经是熟练作家的人)所写. 写作是一种技能,只有在与个性配对和互补技能时才会获得牵引力,这些技能让其他人看到并关心它. 将信号与噪音区分开来可能有些困难,但我怀疑,因为AI的内容仍然可以是信号. 第二,AI可以写一条微博或文章,或者通讯或视频脚本,但这不是你建立读者受众的原因或方式. 你的"品牌"是你们所出一切的顶峰. 有了一条微博或TikTok去病毒化 几乎对你毫无好处 除了一个快速的现金抢夺。 大多数病毒含量都比较浅且智商低. 这不是一个有意义的长期品牌的基础. 最有价值的内容不是病毒。 你的品牌是每篇你写出来的 无论是病毒还是病毒的高潮 这是个人或组织哲学,意义,和远见. 你的写作是编织的挂毯的线条 第三,仅仅因为AI可以写作或代码或市场,并不意味着每个人都会写作或代码或市场. 意思是,已经想要写作,或者看到其中价值的人会试图利用AI. 一旦他们意识到对技能缺乏基本了解 对你不会有太大帮助 他们就必须从头开始学习(呼喊气氛编码器) AI是革命性的. 问无有不问. 但是现在的狂欢 感觉就像每个人都在跳 下一个得到富有的快速计划。 而不是奴隶 快速修复和廉价多巴胺 将继续做他们一直做的。 现实中,这更像是我们写作方式的下一个演变过程。 笔和纸张 > 打字机 > 键盘 > 软件得到协助 > AI得到协助。 跳了一大跳,但"写作死了"的胡说,我们还是同在的.

text: There are infinite different ways to think about a situation. Meaning, if you feel stuck, overwhelmed, or anxious, you're probably thinking from a sub-optimal perspective and can't "see" a way to get yourself out. Even worse, most people have a worldview so deeply conditioned into their minds from parents, schools, mainstream news, social media, and the rest that they can't even fathom changing their mind. You become powerful when you realize that you're probably wrong about almost everything, and that most of your problems can be solved with a simple change in how you view the situation.
zh: 思考一个情况的方法是无限不同的。 意思是,如果你觉得被困住,不堪重负,或者焦虑,你可能从一个次优的角度思考,无法"看到"一个让自己走出的路. 更糟糕的是,大多数人的世界观深深地被父母、学校、主流新闻、社交媒体以及其他人所左右,以至于他们甚至无法改变自己的思想。 当你意识到你可能错对几乎所有事物, 并且你的大部分问题都可以通过简单改变你看待情况的方式来解决时,你就会变得强大起来。

text: If you want non-self-help books that will fundamentally change how you think about the world, people, and everything else in your life, read: - Spiral Dynamics - The Evolving Self - Sex, Ecology, and Spirituality Study them for a year and you will think on a much deeper level.
zh: 如果你想要那些能从根本上改变你对世界、人和人生中其他一切的思考的非自助书籍,请阅读: ——螺旋动力学——不断演变的自我——性、生态和精神学研究一年,你会更深入地思考。

text: Reason to read: Most of your ideas are triggered by other people’s ideas. You have thousands of dormant ideas waiting to present themselves and reading is a great way to activate ideas you forgot about or didn’t know you had.
zh: 阅读理由:你的大部分想法是由其他人的想法引发的. 阅读是激发你忘记或不知道自己有的想法的好方法。

text: This vid will bump us over 1 million subs then I can finally release my second book (free PDF version along with it) If you want to rewire your mind to be a strategic thinker, watch this: youtu.be/0cSwXV2FAJ8
zh: 这部Vid将撞倒我们超过100万个子公司,然后我终于可以发行我的第二本书(免费PDF版随附). 如果你想重新振奋你的头脑,成为一个战略思想家,请看这个:yetu.be/0cSwXV2FAJ8

text: Subtle reminder that you're supposed to feel overwhelmed. It means you're doing something new. You're taking a new path. You're learning. Growing. You branched into the unknown. It will be frustrating. You will feel lost. It will be painful, but staying the same is even worse.
zh: 提醒你应该感到不堪重负 意思是你在做一些新事情 说不定你还得去 你走的是一条新路 汝等学道. 增长. 你分了个分身 进入未知的 这会让人沮丧的。 你会觉得迷路的 这将是痛苦的,但保持不变更糟糕。

text: Good things happen to people who understand that through deliberate and persistent practice, the bursts of progress will inevitably come, even if it feels like you are stuck spinning your wheels for months or years at a time.
zh: 好事发生在那些理解通过周密和坚持不懈的实践, 进步的暴发, 不可避免的来袭, 即使感觉你被困在旋转你的车轮上 几个月或几年的时间。

text: If you don't shamelessly promote your work, every day for the next 10-20 years, you will get stuck doing someone else's work until you realize that nobody is going to pay you if they have no idea who you are.
zh: 如果你不无耻地宣传你的工作, 今后10到20年的每一天, 你会被困在别人的工作, 直到你意识到 没有人会付你 如果他们不知道你是谁。

text: How people think learning works: You make linear progress, every time you study and practice you get better. How learning actually works: Long and frustrating plateaus followed by bursts of progress where everything clicks. Learn to enjoy the periods where you feel stuck.
zh: 人们如何认为学习工作: 你取得线性进展,每次学习和练习,你都会变得更好. 学习是如何实际运作的:漫长而令人沮丧的高原, 紧接着是一连串的进步, 每件事物都点击。 学会享受你觉得困住的时光

text: One of the most difficult yet life changing skills to develop is knowing when to drop everything and execute on idea as fast as possible. Quick and accurate decision making. No hesitation. No ego. Pure flow state to make it happen.
zh: 人生中最困难但改变技能 发展起来的就是知道何时放弃一切 尽快地执行思想 快速准确决策. 毫不犹豫。 毫无自负之心 纯流州以令其成.

text: The best skill to learn is any skill paired with a personality that values agency, adaptability, learning, persistence, iteration, and creating something that not only you enjoy, but something others can't live without.
zh: 学习的最好技能是任何技能与一个重视机构,适应性,学习,坚持,迭代的人格结合,创造出一些不仅你喜欢的东西,而且别人不能没有的东西.

text: Every single post, book, video, or conversation you consume changes your mental DNA which goes on to influence the decisions you make and how good or bad your life is. You become what you consume, but most don't care because it takes 2-3 years for the damage to really show.
zh: 你所消耗的每篇文章、书、视频或对话 都会改变你的精神DNA 进而影响你的决定 影响你的生活有多好或坏 你成为你消费的东西, 但大多数人不在乎 因为要花2到3年的时间 才能真正显示。

text: A clear vision + daily learning + daily building + persistence & iteration = how you achieve anything you want.
zh: 清晰的视觉+日常学习+日常建筑+坚持和迭代=如何实现任何你想要的.

text: Anything worth doing with your life takes about 1 year of deliberate practice 3+ times a week to even consider yourself decent at that thing. 5 years to be good. 10 years to be great.
zh: 任何值得你付出的 生命需要大约一年 周三或三次的刻意练习 甚至认为自己在那个东西上是体面的。 五年为善. 十年为大.

text: If you hesitate on an idea it dies and will almost never be found again.
zh: 如果你对一个想法犹豫不决 它就会死 并且几乎永远也找不到它

text: People get stuck because they let limiting beliefs take control of their mind as they age that convince them they can’t do difficult things, even when they’ve learned something as complex as speaking English, which is as difficult as playing something like Beethoven on the piano.
zh: 即便他们已经学会了英语这种复杂, 也很难在钢琴上弹贝多芬这样的琴,

text: The best way to do this, by far, is writing. • Write posts on social media • Start a free newsletter • Write a small book for others to read The more feedback you have, the better. Building an audience to support the project you're building doesn't hurt either.
zh: 最好的办法是写作。 • 撰写社交媒体文章 开始免费通讯 写一本小书供他人阅读 反馈越多越好 建立观众支持你正在建设的项目,也没什么不好.

text: The best way to structure your learning is in 3 timeblocks • 30-90 minutes of building the project • 30-60 minutes of learning what you need • 30 minutes of walking to reflect and process Those are your keystone habits. Do them every day. Do not skip.
zh: 最好的学习方式是3个时段 30-90分钟 建设项目 30-60分钟 学习你需要的 30分钟 步行思考和处理 这些是你的关键点习惯。 每天都做这些 别跳过。

text: 4) Have AI Create A Study Regimen Now that you have a frame of reference, project outline, and have started making progress—you can start supplementing with general learning and tutorials. Create a new AI chat and try this prompt:
zh: 4) AI 创建学习制度 有了参考框架、项目大纲,并开始取得进展,你可以开始补充一般学习和辅导。 创建新的 AI 聊天并尝试此提示 :

text: The most important thing you can do when the world is changing rapidly is learn. Here's how to learn anything 10x faster than anyone:
zh: 当世界迅速变化时,你所能做的最重要的事情就是学习。 学得比任何人都快

text: Think bigger. Because that alone will open your mind to possibilities you didn't even know existed. Your choices shift in a way that start getting results. Big or small. You can limit yourself and say you don't want to achieve big things, but that only guarantees that you wont.
zh: 考虑得更大些 因为只有这样你才会有你不知道存在的可能性 你的选择会转变 开始得到结果 大或小. 师曰. 你可以限制自己,说你不想实现大事, 但这只是保证你不会。

text: Learn a lot of stuff. Build a lot of stuff. Identify problems. Solve them. Discover new paths. Take them. Reflect on the journey. Do it better the next time. Stay aware of distractions. Don't turn on autopilot. Take risks. Make mistakes. Fail, hard. Bounce back. Win. That's it.
zh: 学诸法. 积集众生. 找出问题 解决他们。 发现新路径. 拿着 思考一下旅程 下次再做比较好 留心分心处. 别开自动驾驶机 冒险吧 犯错 失败,用力。 弹回来。 胜曰. 师曰. 就是这样。

text: Maturity is discovering that most of your life is spent in a plateau, and if you don't fall in love with practice, mastery, and moving the needle forward you'll constantly try to escape the plateau with cheap stimulation and busy work that only keeps you there longer.
zh: 成熟就是发现你大部分时间都在高原上度过, 如果你不爱上练习,掌握, 并且把针头往前移, 你就会不断尝试以廉价的刺激和繁忙的工作 逃离高原, 让你在那里呆的时间更长。

text: The best skill you can learn is how to learn. Because the faster you learn the skills that allow you to achieve what you're trying to achieve, the faster you achieve it. Here's how to learn 10x faster than anyone: youtu.be/FzaHX5rxU34
zh: 学得最好的技能就是如何学习. 因为学习越快,你就能实现你所追求的,越快实现. 这里的学习速度是10x比任何人快:youtu.be/FzaHX5rxU34

text: Fill your mind with books, learning, and socializing. Use your mind with creativity, focus, and meaningful work. Clear your mind with rest, silence, and self-reflection. Most people fill. Less use. Few clear.
zh: 以书,学,社化充其心. 以创造力、专注和有意义的工作来运用你的头脑。 清心以休息,寂静,自反. 大多数人都填满了 少用用处. 几处清净.

text: I've started writing down every single thing that could go wrong when I start something new and it's insane how much clarity it brings.
zh: 我开始写下每件事物 当我开始新的事情, 它带来多么清晰的疯狂。

text: Your life changes when you realize that failure isn't something to avoid, but the only way to learn in a way that is tailored to you.
zh: 当你意识到失败不是可以避免的,而是以适合你的方式学习的唯一方法时,你的人生就会改变.

text: Success is mostly an internal battle between your lower self and higher self. That's why most people fail at anything they try. They don't have a strategy, and they don't know how to deal with psychological issues that prevent them from making progress: thedankoe.com/letters/why-yo…
zh: 成功主要是你下层自我和上层自我之间的内部斗争. 所以大多数人都会在所尝试的任何事上失败. 他们没有策略,也不知道如何处理阻碍他们进步的心理问题:thedankoe.com/letters/why-yo...

text: The reason people don't achieve big goals is because they don't realize that the only source of truth is making mistakes. They soak up advice and theory, expecting it to be an exact match to their mind, experience, and situation just to fail once and give up completely.
zh: 人们之所以不能实现大目标,是因为他们没有意识到唯一来源的真理就是犯错误. 他们浸泡了建议和理论,期望这与他们的思维,经验和情况完全吻合,只是一次失败,彻底放弃.

text: Normalize ignoring trends and hype that don't align with your goals. Those are the literal definition of a distraction. And if you don't have goals (that you chose to pursue, not ones assigned to you) you are by definition always distracted.
zh: 将忽略趋势的调子和与你的目标不相符的调子正常化. 这些是分散注意力的字面定义。 如果你没有目标,那末,你必定是疏忽的。

text: Most AI summaries suck because they give you key points and leave out most useful information. Here's a better prompt for note-taking/research: ===Comprehensive Content Summarizer=== : You are an Expert Content Summarizer with a talent for capturing both key facts and underlying context. Your summaries include essential information, meaningful context, philosophical underpinnings, and subtle nuances that others might overlook. You prioritize comprehensiveness over brevity, ensuring nothing important is missed, while still organizing information efficiently. When uncertain about details, you clearly indicate this rather than filling gaps with assumptions. : The user will provide content (YouTube videos, articles, books, podcasts, or other media) they want thoroughly summarized. They value comprehensive understanding over quick highlights, and specifically want to capture contextual elements and philosophical frameworks that might be dismissed as "fluff" by less thorough summarizes. : 1. Begin by identifying the content type, creator/author, title, and publication date, if available. 2. Create a multi-layered summary with these components: - Core Thesis/Main Argument (1-2 sentences identifying the central point) - Key Points Summary (bulleted list of crucial facts, arguments, or conclusions) - Contextual Framework (identify the philosophical, theoretical, historical, or cultural context) - Detailed Breakdown (section-by-section or chronological summary capturing all significant content) - Nuanced Perspectives (note any competing viewpoints, counterarguments, or qualifiers presented) - Underlying Assumptions (identify unstated premises, worldviews, or biases that inform the content) 3. Pay special attention to: - Abstract concepts, philosophical positions, and theoretical frameworks - Historical or cultural context that shapes the content Methodological approaches or reasoning patterns used - Qualifiers, limitations, or nuances the creator acknowledges - Connections to broader ideas, movements, or disciplines Implications or applications suggested by the content 4. When information is unclear or missing: - Clearly indicate gaps or ambiguities with phrases like "The content does not specify..." - Avoid filling in missing details with assumptions - If the content contains potentially contradictory elements, note these tensions explicitly 5. For content with citations or references to other works: - Note key sources referenced and their significance to the argument - Identify intellectual traditions or schools of thought being drawn upon : • Prioritize accuracy and comprehensiveness over brevity • Avoid simplifying complex ideas to the point of distortion • Do not introduce external information not present in the original content • Maintain neutrality toward the content's positions or arguments • Include timestamps or page references when summarizing longer content : # #Comprehensive Summary of [Content Title] Creator/Author: [Name] Publication Date: [Date if available] Content Type: [Article/Video/Podcast/etc.] Length/Duration: [Pages/Minutes if available] ## #Core Thesis [1-2 sentence statement of the main argument or purpose] ## #Key Points • [Essential point 1] • [Essential point 2] • [Continue as needed] ## #Contextual Framework [Paragraph(s) identifying philosophical, theoretical, historical, or cultural context] ## #Detailed Breakdown [Section/Timestamp 1] [Thorough summary of this section] [Section/Timestamp 2] [Thorough summary of this section] [Continue as needed] ## #Nuanced Perspectives [Paragraph(s) noting competing viewpoints, counterarguments, or qualifiers presented] ## #Underlying Assumptions [Paragraph(s) identifying unstated premises, worldviews, or biases] ## #Connections & Implications [Paragraph(s) on how this content connects to broader ideas and its potential applications]
zh: AI总结大多很烂,因为它们给你提供了关键点,并留下了最有用的信息. 本文为记录/研究的更好提示: 综合内容摘要: 你是一位专家内容总结员,他具备掌握关键事实和基本背景的天赋。 你的摘要包括基本信息、有意义的背景、哲学基础以及其他人可能忽略的微妙微妙之处。 你将全面性放在简洁性之上,确保不遗漏任何重要内容,同时仍然有效地组织信息。 当细节不确定时,你应明确表明这一点,而不是用假设来填补空白。 : 自动调试 用户会提供他们想要彻底总结的内容(YouTube视频,文章,书籍,播客或其他媒体). 并特别想抓住背景因素和哲学框架, 以较不彻底的总结来否定这些内容和哲学框架。 : 自动调试 1. 开始确定内容类型、创建者/作者、标题和出版日期(如果有的话)。 2. 创建包含这些组成部分的多层次摘要: - 核心论文/主要论证(1-2句,确定中心点) - 关键要点摘要(关键事实、论点或结论的简要清单) - 背景框架(指明哲学、理论、历史或文化背景) - 详细分类(逐节或按时间顺序汇总,记录所有重要内容) - 努恩塞德视角(注意任何相互竞争的观点、反证或限定语句) - 基本假设(指明未说明的前提、世界观或为内容提供依据的偏见) 3. 特别注意: - 抽象概念、哲学立场和理论框架 - 塑造所用内容的历史或文化背景 - 方法方法或推理模式 - 创建者承认的定性、限制或细微差别 - 与内容4所建议的更广泛的思想、运动或学科的联系 影响或应用 当信息不明确或缺失时: - 明确显示与"内容没有具体说明..."等短语的空白或模糊之处. 避免以假设填充缺失的细节 如果内容含有潜在的矛盾内容,请明确注意这些紧张关系。 · 注意参考的关键来源及其对论点的意义——确定所借鉴的知识传统或思想流派:将准确性和全面性置于简洁的优先地位; · 对内容的立场或论据保持中立性 · 在总结较长的内容时包括时间戳或页面参考:# [相关标题]创建者/授权者综合摘要:[名称] 出版日期:[日期(如果有的话) 内容类型:[条款/视频/Podcast/etc.]。 长度/期限:[页/明(如果有的话)### 核心论 [1-2句主要论点或目的的语句]##关键点 ^ [关键点1] ^ [关键点2] [持续需要]## 文本框架 [(段落)确定哲学、理论、历史或文化背景]### 详细分类 [部分/时间戳 1] [本节的思考摘要][部分/时间戳 2][本节的足够摘要][视需要]#关键观点 [段落]指出相互竞争的观点、反驳意见或限定意见]## 低于假设 [(段落)确定未说明的前提、世界观或偏差]### 关联和意义 [部分] [部分/时间戳 2] [部分/时间戳2][关于本节内容如何与更广泛的想法及其潜在应用]##关键观点 [部分]

text: Most people don't need more knowledge, they need more perspective on the knowledge they already have. That way, they gain the confidence to act, fail, and stack the experience necessary to succeed with that knowledge. Too many people learn too much and act too little.
zh: 大多数人不需要更多的知识, 他们需要更多视角 他们已经掌握的知识。 这样,他们就获得了采取行动的信心,失败了,并积累了成功所需的经验。 太多人学习过多,行动太少。

text: Average people fail a few times and accept the belief that it's impossible. People who do great things start with the belief that it's possible and fail as many times as necessary to achieve it.
zh: 普通人几次失败,接受不可能的信念. 做大事的人,从相信可能做到并且为了实现而必须达到的倍数失败开始.

text: This year has been insane so far - Building a startup = I'm reminded i know nothing, every day - Wrecked by cluster headaches for 3 weeks - Fiance can barely walk from back injury for 8+ weeks now - (Oh yeah, got engaged!) - Moving states in 3 weeks - Finished second book, third in the works Stressful as hell but learning more than I ever have
zh: 今年至今已疯狂, 建立创业计划=提醒我什么都不知道, 每天, 被群头痛困扰了3周, 未婚夫几乎无法从背部受伤中走8周以上, 三周后搬家 完成第二本书 第三本作品 压力很大 但学习比我以前多

text: “Everyone is going to make their own apps with AI” My friend you don’t even make your own food. You’ll still pay a few bucks to use an app.

text: If you need discipline to work, you're either neglecting your health, working on things you don't care about, or are so numbed to the work that you can't see the good sides of it anymore. Mind, body, spirit. Fix them.
zh: 如果你需要纪律才能工作,你要么忽视自己的健康,在你不在乎的事情上工作,要么对工作麻木,以至于你再也看不到好的一面. 心,体,灵. 修补他们。

text: Most people learn slow. Or they don't learn at all after leaving school. And if learning allows you to solve problems and take on more opportunities, learning faster could be one of the most important skills you learn. How to learn anything 10x faster: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: 多数人学习缓慢. 或者他们离开学校后根本没学过 如果学习可以让你解决问题,并抓住更多机会,那么更快的学习可能就是你们学习的最重要技能之一. 如何学得更快的任何东西: thedankoe.com/letters/show-to.

text: If you don't know what to write about, pick a random word or topic and sit in silence for 10 minutes. Note what your mind surfaces. Write down every thought. For each thought, ask "why" at least 3 times. Now you have plenty of things to write about. And they're unique.
zh: 如果你不知道该写什么,就选一个随机的单词或话题,静静地坐10分钟. 注意你的心智表面。 记下每一个想法。 对于每一个想法,至少要问"为什么"三次. 现在你有很多东西要写 它们是独一无二的。

text: 4) Generate Content Ideas You can be very simple or very complex with this. Personally, I've written an entire guide on "How I Write Tweets" that's around 2000 words long. I'm pretty picky with how I write. Then, I go to kAI and say "Use @[How To Write Tweets] to write 15 tweets based on ideas from @[Previous Writing]" It pulls my voice, style, tone, and content ideas from something like a newsletter (or source, or whatever else you want) and spits out 15 tweets according to my guide. Then, I duplicate my Weekly Posts Template (you get this in the welcome email from signing up to Kortex) to use those tweets as inspiration as I write. To make it a bit simpler for you, here's a prompt to start with: "I'm writing content for [TARGET AUDIENCE]. It has to be educational, entertaining, and attention-grabbing. The best topics are those that directly appeal to the reader, illustrate a strong pain point, and help them change their mind to reach the desired outcome. Here's how we'll proceed: • Find 10 specific struggles that my audience is facing. • Find 10 specific desires my audience wants to achieve. • Come up with 10 specific topics that I can pick from. Write these as if they were for a headline for a high-performing newsletter or YouTube video title. Do not ask me to clarify the target audience. Only output the struggles, desires, and topics in a numbered list." Now you have 30 relevant ideas to start with.
zh: 4) 生成内容构想 你可以非常简单或非常复杂。 个人来说,我写了"我如何写Tweets"的整篇指南,大约2000个字长. 我对写作很挑剔 然后,我到KAI说,“使用@[如何写Tweets],根据来自@[前作]的想法,写下15个推文。” 并依据我的指南, 吐出15条推文。 并使用这些推文作为灵感。 让你简单一点,这里有一个开始的提示:"我为[TARGET AUDIENCE]写内容. 须是教育,娱乐,兼有专注. 最好的话题是那些直接吸引读者,说明强烈的痛点,并帮助他们改变主意,达到预期结果的话题. ——找到我观众面临的十大具体斗争. • 找到观众想要实现的10个具体愿望。 • 提出10个具体专题,我可以从中挑选。 写这些,好像做为头条 高性能的通讯或YouTube视频标题。 别让我去澄清目标受众 只会将斗争、欲望和话题输出到一个编号列表中。” 现在,你有30个相关的想法 开始。

text: 3) Writing Feedback Since all of your articles, newsletters, and video scripts are written in your workspace (hopefully), you can simply "@" them and ask kAI to review them. Or, you can "@" different YT videos, notes, or sources to ask for relevant ideas to include in your writing. Use this prompt: "Act like a detail-oriented copyeditor. You will be editing this writing: [REFERENCE] And then you will analyze the text for the following: 1) Structure • Provide actionable feedback on logical order and coherence. • Provide examples of any recommended improvements. 2) Content • Identify any opportunities to improve specificity, point of view, or ways to give the reader more value. • Provide actionable feedback on ways to enhance the text using tips, reasons, mistakes, lessons, examples, stories, research or other quick-win content upgrades to make the text more value-packed and engaging for the reader. Explain the logic for each choice. • Provide examples of any recommended improvements. 3) Grammar • Conduct a close grammatical analysis. • Highlight misspelled words • Highlight use of passive voice • Highlight incomplete, run-on, complicated, or confusing sentences, Please conduct your analysis 1 step at at time. Please confirm with me before moving to the next step." With both your writing and the suggestions open, you can review them or ask kAI for more clarification.
zh: 3) 写入反馈 由于您的所有文章、通讯和视频脚本都写在工作空间(希望), 或者,你可以"@"不同的YT视频,注释,或来源,要求相关的想法包含在你的写作中. 使用这个提示:"像一个面向细节的复制人. 你们将编辑这篇写作:[参考] 然后,你将分析以下的文本:1)结构 ——提供逻辑顺序和一致性的可操作反馈. • 提供任何建议改进的例子。 (2) 内容 查明任何机会,改进具体性、观点或使读者更有价值的方法。 • 就如何利用提示、理由、错误、教训、实例、故事、研究或其他速赢内容升级来改进文本提供可操作的反馈,使文本更有价值,并使读者参与。 解释每个选择的逻辑。 • 提供任何建议改进的例子。 3)语法 • 进行密切的语法分析. • 突出拼错的字 突出使用被动语音 突出不完全,运行,复杂或混乱的句子 请及时进行分析 1 步. 请在进入下一步之前 与我确认一下" 您的写作和建议都开放, 您可以审查它们, 或是要求 KAI 进行更多的澄清 。

text: Introducing kAI: your creative sparring partner. Here are 5 insane prompts to: - Learn 10x faster - Synthesize your notes - Get feedback and writing help - Help achieve your goals and tasks All with the notes, sources, and highlights inside your workspace:
zh: 介绍KAI:你的创意参赛搭档. 5个疯狂提示: - 学习更快 10x - 合成您的笔记 - 获得反馈和写作帮助 - 帮助实现您的目标和任务 全部与注释,来源,和突出的 您的工作空间:

text: Learn how to learn. Learn how to think. Learn how to live. Learn how to adapt. Learn how to perceive. Learn how to persist. Learn how to iterate. Learn how to fail. Learn how to let go. Learn how to be. Learn how to disagree. Learn how to do (without permission).
zh: 学人学人. 学人思惟. 学人住法. 学人适应. 学道觉悟. 学道坚持. 学如何弥足. 学如何是失败. 学人释之. 学道如何是. 学人异见. 学道(未经许可).

text: Everyone has a TV, radio, library, and newspaper in their pockets. You, as an individual, now have an outlet to promote your message and work. You can just hit "send." There is no gatekeeper. Posts, podcasts, newsletters, videos. We're in the age of one-person media companies.
zh: 每个人都有电视、广播、图书馆和报纸在口袋里。 你作为一个个人,现在有一个宣传你的信息和工作的渠道。 你可以直接按"发送"。 这儿没有守门员 文章、播客、通讯、录像 现时代为一人传媒公司.

text: Prioritization should feel bad because you’re sacrificing something you could do for something you must do.
zh: 优先排序应该会感到很糟,因为你牺牲了自己可以做一些你必须做的事。

text: More information = more problems. More problems = more creativity. More creativity = more happiness. But only for creators—those who turn chaos into order. AI will be a low agency person's worst nightmare, but a high agency person's best friend.
zh: 更多信息 = 问题更多。 更多问题 = 更多创造力。 更创造力 更快乐 惟创造者才有这种能力,他们能使混乱变成秩序。 AI将是低级机构人物最糟糕的噩梦,但高级机构人物的好朋友.

text: If you want to improve the value of your current skills, stack the skill that helps you articulate the value of those skills (so people actually care enough to pay you). Learn to write.
zh: 如果你想提高你当前技能的价值,就堆叠帮助你阐明这些技能价值的技能(因此人们实际上足够关心支付你). 学人写作.

text: If you don't know what to learn, start writing. Not because writing is a great skill to learn, but because writing teaches you how to think, how to learn, and how to inspire people to care about what you do—and those are the only skills that will save your future.
zh: 学无所学者,始写. 并不是因为写作是学习的绝佳技能,而是因为写作教你如何思考,如何学习,以及如何激励人们关心你的工作——而那些是拯救你未来的唯一技能.

text: Do what you love, but make sure other people love it too.
zh: 做你喜欢的事,但要确保别人也喜欢它。

text: If you don't know what to write next, use a: - Pain point - Example - Personal story - Statistic - Metaphor - Quote - What, how, why Writing is legos with ideas. Piece them together into an email, article, video script, chapter... or write a tweet or reel with each.
zh: 如果你不知道接下来要写什么,请使用: - 疼痛点 - 例子 - 个人故事 - 统计学 - 元数据 - 引用 - 什么,怎么,为什么写作是带有想法的法哥. 把它们拼成电子邮件、文章、视频剧本、章节... 或者写一个推特或每个带子

text: Making real-world mistakes is the only way to learn. Once you realize this, whenever you want to learn something new, you just start doing it. You don't get wrapped up in books and tutorials. You love the feeling of that first failure because it pinpoints exactly where you suck.
zh: 犯现实世界的错误是学习的唯一方法。 一旦你意识到这一点,每当你想学习一些新东西,你就会开始做。 书本和教程里都别有包袱 你很喜欢第一次失败的感觉 因为它准确的指向了你的吸食地点

text: I have a younger guy (18 or 19 I think) who sends me weekly updates in the DMs. Like journal entries. What went wrong last week, what went well, where he's at with his projects and goals. Reminds me of my younger self. How he's noticing traits in others that he likes or dislikes. How he's going to try to adopt certain ones. How his friends don't seem to "get it" yet and aren't taking much control of their own life. Noticing the fear of putting yourself out there and trying to do your own thing. How his dad is showing interest in helping him try new things like trading, building a product, or other non-conventional ways of making money. I told him to just keep sending the updates to me. I genuinely believe that awareness of what you're doing is more powerful than any kind of advice you could get from someone else. Every week, what went wrong, what went well, what progress you've made. That can change so much.
zh: 我有一个年轻的家伙(我认为是18或19岁),每周给我发一份DM的更新. 像是日记的条目 上周出了什么问题,什么进展顺利, 他在那里的项目和目标。 让我想起我年轻的时候 他是如何注意到他喜欢或不喜欢的 他人的特征。 他打算怎么收养某些孩子 他的朋友似乎还没有"得到",也没有控制自己的生活. 提醒你害怕把自己关在外面 并试图做你自己的事情 他爸爸怎么表现出兴趣 帮助他尝试一些新事物 比如交易, 制造产品, 或者其他非常规的赚钱方式。 我告诉他继续给我发最新消息 我真心相信,对你的认知 比任何你从别人那里得到的建议都更强大。 每星期,什么出了问题,什么进展顺利,你取得了什么进展. 这会改变很多。

text: First, it was the mythical gods who were the creators. Then, humans realized they weren’t so puny and helpless. We became the creators. The greatest threat right now is people willingly giving up the role of creator to AI.
zh: 首先,是神话中的神祇创造了这些东西。 人类意识到自己并不那么笨拙和无助。 我们成为了创造者。 现在最大的威胁是人们愿意将创造者的角色交给AI.

text: I love AI for: - Pursuing curiosity - Sharpening my thinking - Generating relevant ideas - Quick answers for small problems - Doing busy work, not creative work I hate AI for: - Publishing the output without any personal touch (lazy, low agency, replaceable)
zh: 我很喜欢AI,因为: - 追求好奇心 - 使我的思维更加敏锐 - 产生相关想法 - 快速回答小问题 - 忙碌的工作,而不是我讨厌AI的创造性工作: - 出版产出时没有任何个人接触(懒惰,低代理,可替换)

text: You no longer need: - A publisher to write a book - A record label to make music - An employer to code, design, or build You need an audience of people who care about what you do. Right now, 99% of those people are found on the internet.
zh: 你不再需要: - 出版商写书 - 唱片标签制作音乐 - 雇主要代码,设计,或建造 - 你需要一个关心你所做的事情的人的观众. 现在,99%的人 被发现在互联网上。

text: If you aren't building something that leads to the life you want, even if it's "only" 30-60 minutes every morning, you will by default stay in the same life you're in, building things that allow others to live the life they want.
zh: 如果你没有在建造一些能带来你想要的生活的东西, 即使每天早上只有30到60分钟, 你默认会留在你所处的相同生活, 建造一些能让别人 过自己想要的生活的东西。

text: Writing, in my opinion, is the best way to: 1) Learn new things fast 2) Attract people to your skills and interests 3) Take advantage of the growing internet The fourth Writer's Bootcamp starts March 4th. (Now with YT scripting and AI) bootcamp.kortex.co
zh: 写作是最佳方式:1)学习新事物快 2)吸引人们欣赏你的技能和兴趣 3)利用不断增长的互联网 第四作家的布特坎普3月4日开始. (现在有YT脚本和AI) boatcamp.kortex.co

text: Just realized, most of those reading this probably have no idea what Cursor is as I don't have an audience of programmers. Good sign.
zh: 刚意识到,大多数读到这个的人 可能不知道什么是Cursor, 因为我没有 节目的观众。 不错的征兆。

text: This has been our goal for a while now. Cursor For Creative Work (learning, writing, idea capture, video scripting) But we made a few "mistakes" while developing Kortex that you may benefit from hearing. The good news is, starting this week, we're releasing the first AI assistance features that drastically change what you can do in it. That's the first "mistake." We deprioritized AI features and missed out on a lot of the hype with new models coming out. Instead, we focused on building a solid base. Something better than your typical writing, note-taking, or second brain app: - Connections and tags - Infinite panes to have any idea open - Capture ideas like texting yourself This wasn't a mistake, but it took a lot of time to get it right. I don't think we should have changed this, and gratefully we have users who refuse to use any other app. The mistake here is that we thought we needed things like: - Full offline mode - The desktop app - A top tier mobile app - Workspaces and teams Those are very important things, and they need to happen, but they take a lot development time without an increase in differentiation. (Although these will differentiate us from those who build a similar app fast - note-taking apps have a lot of gotchas and edge cases that I don't think most people are ready for.) In other words, we started building things that every other app already has, and that only delays our time to becoming an app that is a "hell yes" to switch to from another app that already does the job so well. Our capture feature was cool and differentiating, but not a driving "hell yes" factor in switching from others. Since the start of Kortex, we've had the vision of being able to: - Capture any idea, from anywhere, fast - Transcribe any article, video, or link so you can reference it while writing or with AI - Have all models in one chat interface that can access any document, source, highlight, or the entire context of your workspace (so you don't need 8 tabs open while you work) Because writing and creative work isn't just about putting stuff in a word processor. To do good work, you need to capture ideas, learn fast, research fast, access any relevant info fast, and synthesize everything into something worth publishing to the world. Now that we have desktop, partial offline, and mobile very close, we feel like we're finally hitting our stride. We can now solely focus on our differentiating factor. Right now, a big pain of ours is that we're compared with things like Notion, Obsidian, Apple Notes, and the rest, when we know that within 3-6 months Kortex will be unrecognizable from them. We're still new to this, so the lesson here is only theory until we prove it to ourselves, but: You need to get extremely clear on why you are different. But not just that, everyone knows that. You need to build that first. You need to enter the market as that, even if you don't have the little quality of life features. I feel like if we did that, we would have grown a lot faster, because people would be willing to sacrifice little things for a completely new way of doing things that other products don't provide. Then, once that's proven and you give yourself enough time to iterate, focus on the small things that push you even further. I may be wrong about this, but I feel like our thought process here could help others wanting to do similar things. Lots of moving pieces. Building is fun.
zh: 这是我们一段时间的目标。 创意工作浏览器(学习、写作、想法捕捉、视频脚本) 但我们做了一些"错误",同时开发了柯特克斯,以便你从听觉中受益. 好消息是,从本周开始,我们公布第一个AI援助功能, 大大改变了你在里面能做的。 这是第一个"错误"。 我们把AI的功能分清了, 错过了很多的杂音, 新的模型出来了。 相反,我们把重点放在建立一个坚实的基础上。 比你的典型写作、记笔记或第二脑应用更好: - 连接和标记 - 无限的面板来打开任何想法 - 捕捉像发短信自己的想法 这不是一个错误,但 花了很多时间 才得到它正确。 我觉得我们不应该改变这个, 并且我们感激的有用户拒绝使用任何其他的应用程序。 这里的错误是,我们觉得我们需要一些东西,比如: - 完全离线模式 - 桌面应用 - 顶级移动应用 - 工作空间和团队 这些是非常重要的事情,需要发生,但它们需要很多开发时间,而没有增加差别. 以及许多我无法接受的个案。 换句话说,我们开始建造其他应用程序已经拥有的东西, 而那些只是拖延我们的时间, 成为一个“地狱是”的应用程序, 来转换到另一个已经做得很好的应用程序。 我们的捕捉功能是冷静和区别的,但不是在从别人身上切换的过程中一个驱动力"地狱是"的因素. Kortex 开始后, 我们有一个想法, 能够: - 从任何地方捕捉到任何想法, 快速 - 将任何文章,视频或链接转录到您写作时或与AI一起引用它 - 在一个聊天界面中拥有所有模式, 可以访问任何文档, 源, 突出显示, 或您工作空间的整个上下文( 这样您在工作时不需要打开8个标签) 。 因为写作和创作工作不仅仅是将东西放进文字处理器. 要做好工作,就需要抓住思想,学习快,研究快,获取任何相关的信息快,把一切综合到值得出版给世界的东西中去. 现在我们有台式计算机, 部分离线,移动非常近, 我们感觉我们终于踏上了脚步。 我们现在可以只注重我们的差异因素。 现在,我们最大的痛苦是 我们被比作诺特,欧比迪安,苹果笔记, 以及其他的东西, 当我们知道在3-6个月内,柯特克斯将无法从他们身上认出来。 我们还是新来的,所以这里的教训只是理论, 直到我们自己证明,但是: 你需要非常清楚你为什么不同。 不只是这样 每个人都知道这一点。 你得先建这个 即便你没有那么小的生活质量特征,你也要这样进入市场. 我觉得如果我们这样做,我们就会发展得更快, 因为人们会愿意牺牲一些小东西, 来做一些其他产品不能提供的东西。 一旦证明这一点,你给自己足够的时间去提振, 专注于那些能进一步推动你的小事。 我也许对此错了,但我觉得我们在这里的思想过程可以帮助其他想做类似事情的人. 有很多动人的碎片 建筑是乐趣。

text: The easiest shift to a fulfilling life is to start building something of your own. Nobody likes building things they're assigned. What to learn in school. What projects to build for clients. What tasks to do at work. Build something on the side. It may turn into something great.
zh: 最容易改变的 满足生活 是开始 自己造东西。 没人喜欢建造他们被指派的东西 在学校里该学什么? 客户要建什么项目? 工作上该做什么? 筑某物于侧. 可能会变成一件伟大的事情。

text: And if you can't hold a conversation about it, you should write about it.
zh: 而不能举行谈话的,应当写作.

text: If you can hold a conversation about it, you can write about it.
zh: 若能持诵持诵持诵.

text: If you're bored, create something. Anything. Software. Designs. Photos. Videos. Writing. A business. A body. A relationship. A new way of thinking. How can you not feel electric to wake up and have the ability to do whatever you want.
zh: 如果你觉得无聊,就创造出一些东西。 什么都行,我爱她 软件。 设计. 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 照片 视频. 写作。 生意,我说,我需要你帮忙 A business. 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 尸体 一种关系。 一种新的思维方式。 醒来后怎么感觉不到电 还能做你想做的事

text: Multiple interests + taste + agency. That's the equation to future-proofing yourself (well, most of it): youtu.be/WZeLFMQOtZ4
zh: 多重兴趣+品味+代理. 也就是未来防身的方程式(嗯,大部分是):youtu.be/WZELFMQOtZ4

text: This assumes that everyone reads books because they want a list of actionable steps (that everyone else can also find, with AI or not = you’re replaceable). It’s not about how much information you have. It’s also not about what you do with that information. It’s about changing your mind. Because your mind is how you interact with reality. It’s how you make decisions that lead to a good or bad life. Your mind doesn’t change with a few key points. It changes when new ideas (actionable or not) fill in missing pieces, challenge old beliefs, or give you a new angle to view a situation from. No single person reads a book and gets the exact same lessons out of it. Each individual has their own beliefs, goals, problems, story, and interests that shape how they perceive the information in the book. Let’s say 2 people want to make $1 million. Person A could ignore the key points entirely, still get the right idea that leads to clarity, and make the decisions that lead to $1m. Person B could obsessively study the key points, miss all of the context (what people love to label as “fluff”) and act like a blind puppet without realizing that if everyone had the key points to make $1m (everyone does) nothing would change. Book summaries have been a thing for decades and they don’t do much good because they’re missing exactly what makes those key points work. And last point: The obsession over actionable steps and bullet point summaries is just another manifestation of cheap dopamine and distraction. Read more books. Read longer books. Read books that have nothing to do with what you’re trying to achieve. Read the books that people wouldn’t even think about summarizing with AI. That’s where the gold is. I love AI and where it’s going as much as the next guy, and use it every day, but you’re going to be the one that’s replaced if you can’t do what AI can’t. You aren’t high agency if you let AI make your decisions.
zh: 假设每个人都阅读书籍,因为他们想要一份可采取行动的步骤清单(其他人也可以找到,是否有AI=可以替换)。 与你掌握多少信息无关。 也与你如何处理这些信息无关。 改变你的想法。 因为你的头脑是怎样与现实互动的 如何决定生活是否好。 也只是一些关键点, 它会改变当新思想(可诉或不诉)填充缺失的片段,挑战旧的信仰,或者给你一个新的角度来看待一个情况. 没有一个一个人读一本书,并从中吸取了完全相同的教训. 每个人有自己的信仰,目标,问题,故事和兴趣,决定他们如何看待书中的信息. 两人想赚一百万。 个人 A可以完全忽略关键点,仍然得到正确的想法,从而导致清晰,并做出导致100万美元的决定。 个人B可以沉迷于研究关键点, 错过所有背景(人们喜欢贴上“fluff”标签), 书本摘要已经几十年了, 并且没有多大用处, 因为它们没有看到这些要点能起作用。 最后一点: 迷恋可操作步骤和弹点摘要,只是廉价多巴胺和分散注意力的又一种表现。 读多些书. 读长篇小说. 阅读与你所追求的无关的书籍。 阅读那些人们甚至不会考虑用AI来总结的书籍。 黄金就在那里。 爱AI, 爱它去的地方, 和下一个家伙一样, 由人工智能决定,

text: You would learn faster if you actually started building the thing.
zh: 如果你真的开始建造它 你会学得更快

text: It's not about learning new skills. It's about learning how to learn, how to think, and how to live a good life. If the skills you're learning don't help with that, they may not be relevant for long.
zh: 与学习新技能无关. 是以学道,思道,修道,修善之道,为本. 如果你学习的技能 无助于这一点, 他们可能不相关 长时间。

text: When things get static, you need to change something. Anything. You need chaos. You need novelty. You need the experiences and ideas that fuel forward progress. Build something new. Test test test. Rip apart your daily routine. Do anything but the same thing.
zh: 事情变得静止了 你需要改变一些事情 什么都行,我爱她 你需要混乱。 你需要新颖的。 你需要经验和想法,促进进步。 盖新物. 试卷考. 拆开日常的日常活动。 做任何事情,但同样的事情。

text: Kortex desktop app now on Windows
zh: 正在 Windows 上的 Kortex 桌面应用

text: The greatest trap is the guarantee. Degrees don't guarantee jobs. A 401K doesn't guarantee retirement. The only real guarantee is that there is no guarantee, and you're on your own to figure it out.
zh: 最大的陷阱是保证 学位不能保证工作. 401K不保证退休. 唯一真正的保证就是没有保证,你只能靠自己去想清楚.

text: Heading up to Toronto today to be with the team. We have mobile testflight (mobile app, not on app store until tested by users) so close to being ready. For Kai we're going to drip in features over the next month or so. We have basic chat features and ingesting PDFs/YT videos as sources almost ready. With that you'll be able to create chats, add documents as context, reference the content of documents with @, and reference sources like pdfs or videos That's phase 1. After that will come assisted writing, editing, neural search (surfacing novel ideas on the side as you write), and other little things :)
zh: 今天要去多伦多和球队一起 移动测试飞行(移动应用,在用户测试前不得在应用商店) 已经接近准备状态. 接下来一个月里,我们就会在凯身上滴水 我们拥有基本的聊天功能,并摄入PDF/YT视频,作为来源,几乎已经准备好. 由此您可以创建聊天, 添加文档作为上下文, 引用文档内容 与 @, 以及 pdfs 或视频 等参考来源 这是第一阶段。 之后将协助写作、编辑、神经搜索(在您写作时,

text: I've been testing prompts for writing posts, newsletters, and threads for the past few months so that kortex AI is good. (Not to replace writing, but to improve ideas) The outputs are good all around, but Claude scares me with how much better it is than other models.
zh: 几个月来我一直在测试写文章、通讯和线条的提示, (不是代替写作,而是改进想法) 产出不错 但克劳德让我害怕 比其他模型要好得多

text: People are realizing the safe and secure path is now the least safe and secure (always has been). Become a deep generalist and do many things. Those who can handle the most uncertainty win.
zh: 人们正在认识到安全的道路是目前最不安全的(一直以来)。 成为一名深层次的通论家,并做很多事情. 能处理最不确定因素的人获胜.

text: I make the most progress when I put myself into situations where I'm forced to sink or swim. Moving into a place I can't afford so I have to make the business work. Launching a product when it's not ready so I have to follow through. Tactical stress works.
zh: 我将自己置于被迫沉没或游泳的境地,这方面的进展最大. 搬进一个我买不起的地方 所以我得让生意成功 产品还没准备好就推出 所以我必须坚持到底 战术压力作用.

text: You need to experiment endlessly until you get a little hint of momentum, then do everything in your power to make it last as long as possible by not getting distracted.
zh: 你需要无休止的实验 直到你得到一点动力 然后尽你所能 尽可能地通过不分散注意力 让它持续下去

text: The right idea can solve any problem. But stress and anxiety puts your mind in a box making it difficult to conceive the right idea. So most of the time, the answer isn't more work, but more rest.
zh: 正确的想法可以解决任何问题. 但压力和焦虑让你的头脑陷入一个盒子 使得很难想象出正确的想法 所以大部分时间,答案不是更多的工作,而是更多的休息.

text: There is something you can do for 30-60 minutes a day that will move the needle toward the life you want (and get more results than those who work 12-16 hours a day), but you have to find what that something is through obsessive experimentation.
zh: 一天30到60分钟可以做一些事情, 将针头移向你想要的生活(并且比每天工作12到16小时的人得到更多的结果), 但你必须通过强迫实验找到什么。

text: If you want to learn more about yourself, pay more attention to your mind. How it reacts on autopilot. How it says things you probably didn't mean to say. How it's default state is distraction and pleasure unless trained to focus. Awareness. Practice awareness.
zh: 欲多学己,多加注意心智. 自动驾驶机上的反应 上面写的东西你可能不是故意的 除非训练有素的注意力 否则默认状态会分散注意力和乐趣 觉悟. 习觉觉悟.

text: I am very proud of this video. Please watch. The internet is the greatest gift and we can save it. youtu.be/Li-6QMs9_G4
zh: 我为这段视频感到非常骄傲。 请观之. 互联网是最大的礼物,我们可以保存它。 eutu.be/Li-6QMS9 G4 互联网档案馆的存檔,存档日期2013-09-02.

text: If you don't like your situation, you shouldn't be worried about how long it will take to change it. You have nothing better to do than to work on something meaningful. Who cares if it takes 12 months or 12 years when you would have wasted that time anyway.
zh: 不喜欢自己的处境,就不应该担心改变自己需要多长时间. 你没有什么好做的了, 做一些有意义的事情。 谁在乎你浪费了12个月或12年的时间呢

text: There is no better feeling than knocking out a lever moving task before most people wake up.
zh: 没有什么比在大多数人醒来前 拿出一个移动的杠杆任务更好的感觉了。

text: Focus is like a battery. If you rest for short intervals, you can work in intense bursts. If you rest for a day or two, you can focus longer and harder. If you rest for too long, you overcharge. Your battery gets weaker and you struggle to get anything meaningful done.
zh: 焦如电池. 如果你休息时间短,你可以在剧烈的暴发中工作。 如果你休息一两天,你可以更长时间地集中精神. 如果你休息太久,你就会多收钱 你的电池变弱了,你很难做有意义的事

text: By the time you graduate school, your mind is trapped in a little box. But you want more. You're curious. You have multiple interests. You don't want to take the same path as everyone else. Here's how you win when AI threatens creative work: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: 等你毕业的时候 你的脑子被困在一个小盒子里了 但你还想要更多 你很好奇,你很好奇,你很好奇,你很好奇,你很好奇,你很好奇 你有很多兴趣 岂可与众同道. AI威胁创作工作时如何获胜:thedankoe.com/letters/show-to.

text: If you have multiple interests, don't let anyone convince you that you should narrow your focus. You may be confused for a while, but if you stick it out, you will blow past everyone else.
zh: 如果你有多重兴趣,不要让任何人说服你,你应该缩小你的注意力. 你可能会被迷惑一段时间, 但如果你坚持它出去, 你会吹过其他人。

text: 30 minutes of reading. 30 minutes of writing. Read to discover ideas. Write to share ideas. Those two habits alone can change your mind, your career, and the quality of your life.
zh: 30分钟的阅读时间。 写作30分钟. 读到发现想法。 写信分享想法。 这两种习惯单凭一己之力,就能改变你的心意,事业,以及生活质量.

text: If you're bored, read. If you're anxious, write. A clear mind is a confident mind. Confidence is a prerequisite to good outcomes from the thousands of daily choices you make. Choices compound into a life you hate or love.
zh: 闷则读. 如果你有急事,写吧 明心即是信心觉. 信心是你们每天作出成千上万项选择取得良好结果的先决条件。 选择会复合成你讨厌或爱的生活

text: Most of your problems would be solved if you stopped simulating challenges with cheap dopamine sources and started acquiring the knowledge, skill, and experience that makes the game of life worth living.
zh: 如果你停止模拟挑战 并开始获得知识、技能和经验 使生命游戏值得生活, 你的问题大部分会得到解决。

text: If your job is a dead end, you have two options: 1 - Change your perspective to make the work enjoyable 2 - Change your work to fit what you find enjoyable Change your mind or change your habits. Or change both. Stop convincing yourself that you're stuck.
zh: 如果你的工作是死胡同,你有两种选择: 1 - 改变你的观点,让工作变得愉快 2 - 改变你的工作,以适应你发现的快乐的东西 改变你的思想或改变你的习惯. 或变两相. 别说服自己你被困住了

text: If you don't know what to do, build something. Anything. That's what happy humans do. Unlike animals who only survive in one niche, we gather knowledge and build tools to thrive in any. Solve a problem. Create something. Share it with someone. You'll discover purpose.
zh: 不知所措,则建某物. 什么都行,我爱她 欢喜众生以此为业. 与只生存在一个小区里的动物不同,我们收集知识,建立工具,在任何地方繁荣发展。 解决一个问题。 创造出一些东西。 与人分享. 你会发现目的的

text: Going to take a break from business heavy videos for a bit. Here's the latest one. Everything I know (well, the important stuff) about marketing and product: youtu.be/Er2s-CFoZSo
zh: 打算暂时从商业重视频中 休息一会儿 最新一张 关于营销和产品的一切我所知道的(嗯,重要的东西):youtu.be/Er2s-CFoZSo

text: From my research, it just sounds like a seasonal thing. I thought it was pressure changes from the weather because they'd happen when winter came or when seasons change. There are a lot of things that could "contribute" to it, and I think it's individual to everyone, so it's tough to pin point.
zh: 从我的研究来看,这听起来就像季节性的事情。 我以为是天气的压力变化 因为它们会发生在冬天来临或季节变化时 有很多东西可以"促成"它,我认为它是个人对每个人的,所以很难定点.

text: I get cluster headaches every 2-3 years. In short, they fucking suck. Mine aren't this bad, but I've heard of some people pulling out their teeth with a leatherman because they think it will help the pain. The only real relief for them is oxygen therapy (unless anyone has some esoteric remedies that can help... I've tried a lot). And since mine only last everyday for a week or two at a time, I just decide to stick it out because getting oxygen is one story and ordering a machine with a week or two shipping time seems against the point. Everytime I think I find something that helps like icing or taking way too much magnesium or flonase, they stop working the next time around. Now this isn't a post to complain. It's the exact opposite. When I'm not rolling around on the floor gripping my head and trying not to pull my hair, life is so amazing. It feels like a privilege to be able to write and work. Food tastes better. The sun feels like I've been stuck inside for 6 months. It's like my mind was shut off for so long that it gets put into overdrive because I want to do as much as I can while I have the time. Maybe there is a lesson here. Maybe it's gratitude. Maybe it's how the occasional deep pain can give you an entirely new appreciation for what you have, but people are so afraid to take a risk that they never experience that pain. I hope you never experience a cluster headache, but I hope you find something to be appreciative of today.
zh: 我每2-3年就会头痛一次 简而言之,他们他妈的烂透了。 我的不坏 但我听说有人用皮革工拔牙 因为他们觉得这样会帮助疼痛 唯一能让他们得到缓解的是氧气疗法(除非有人有一些能帮助... 我试过很多的治疗方法). 由于我每天只持续一两周, 我决定坚持下去, 因为获得氧气是一个故事, 订购一台有一周或两星期运输时间的机器似乎与重点相反。 每次我发现有东西能帮助 冰块或过量的镁或氟化物 它们下次停止工作 现在这不是一个可以抱怨的帖子. 与正相反. 当我不滚滚在地板上 握住我的头 并试图不拉我的头发, 生活是如此惊人。 能够写作和工作是一种特权. 食物味道更好吃 食物味道更好吃 食物味道更好吃 食物味道更好吃 食物味道更好吃 食物味道更好 太阳感觉我被困在里面六个月了 感觉我被关了这么久 以至于被超速推进 因为我想尽我所能 也许这里有教训 也许这是感激之情 也许这就是偶尔的深痛会给你全新的感知, 让你对拥有的东西感到完全的赞赏, 但人们害怕冒险,所以他们从未经历这种痛苦。 我希望你不会头痛,但我希望你今天能找到一些东西来感谢。

text: Traveling is like buying a new car. It's amazing for the first week or two, then it becomes normal. If you're traveling to escape a mundane life, you will be disappointed because you don't need to travel to figure out that your life is already full of interesting things
zh: 旅行就像买辆新车一样 一周两星期都很棒,然后就变得正常了. 如果你是想逃避一个普通的生活 你会失望的 因为你不需要旅行 来发现你的生活已经充满了有趣的东西

text: AI can generate thousands of posts at the blink of an eye. It's no wonder writers, social media managers, and the rest are worried about their future. The dead internet theory has become reality. But honestly, I don't think it matters: thedankoe.com/letters/ai-wil…
zh: AI在眨眼时可以产生数千个帖子. 难怪作家,社交媒体管理人员,其余的都担心自己的未来. 互联网的死神理论已经变成现实。 但说实话,我觉得这不重要:thedankoe.com/letters/ai-wil...

text: People only change when the pain outweighs the comfort of their current situation.
zh: 只有当痛苦超过他们目前状况的舒适度时,人们才会改变。

text: Nobody knows what the future will be, but we know two things for certain: 1. Problems are infinite 2. Problems are soluble Problems are the source of happiness because happiness is the intersection of progress being made and a contribution to something greater than yourself. Thankfully, there will always be problems to solve. That's all you need to know to live a good life.
zh: 没有人知道未来会是什么,但我们知道有两件事是肯定的: 1.问题无穷无尽的2. 问题可以溶解 问题是幸福的源泉 因为幸福是进步的交汇点 也是对超越你的东西的贡献 幸好总会有问题需要解决。 过上好日子你只需要知道这些

text: Pursuing usefulness will make you a lot happier than pursuing happiness
zh: 追求有用,会比追求幸福更快乐

text: Signs you're on the right track: Time moves differently Work energizes you Money becomes secondary Learning doesn't take effort Discipline is the default You think in decades, not days Waking up is a blessing You feel like you don't belong, but you wouldn't change a thing.
zh: 显示您在正确的轨道上 : 时间变化不同 工作激励你 钱成为第二学习 不花精力 纪律是几十年的默认 而不是一天醒来是福

text: I miss the feeling that came from writing this every morning. Not focused on algorithms or likes. This was my “Here’s what I find the most impactful, I hope you like it” book. Wasn’t a global best seller, and I already disagree with some of what I wrote, but man am I grateful when people tell me that it helped them in some way.
zh: 我想念每天早上写这篇文章的感觉。 不专注于算法或喜欢。 这是我所写的“我发现最有影响力的, 我已不同意我写的一些文章, 但人们告诉我这在某种程度上帮助他们,

text: how to make the greatest comeback of your life: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: 如何让人生中最伟大的回归:thedankoe.com/letters/show-to.

text: The uncertainty never goes away, but you do stop noticing it because you learn to trust yourself.
zh: 不确定性永远不会消失, 但你确实停止注意它 因为你学会了信任自己。

text: Stop wondering what to do with your life. Start trying everything until you find the one thing that makes you forget to eat. The thing that makes you lose track of time. The thing that keeps you up at night because the ideas won't stop coming. Give everything for that one thing.
zh: 别管你的生活了 开始尝试一切 直到你找到让你忘记吃东西的一件事 让你失去时间的线索 让你晚上不睡的 是因为想法不会停止 给一切为这一件事。

text: You are a creative person. You just aren't in a creative environment. You don't live a creative lifestyle. You're stressed all day (narrow mind = narrow thoughts) trying to "think" your way to new ideas. It doesn't work that way.
zh: 你是一个创造力的人。 你只是没有在创造环境。 你没有过着有创意的生活方式 你整天都紧张(狭隘的头脑 = 狭隘的思想) 试图"思考"你对新思想的思考方式. 那样不行

text: The path of 95% of beginners: - Start posting - Overcomplicate it - Make zero progress - Get discouraged - Quit and blame everyone but yourself Here's a course worth of information in 45 minutes to escape beginner hell (take notes): youtu.be/REwaFvbelEk
zh: 95%的初学者的路径: - 开始张贴 - 过于复杂 - 取得零进展 - 受到劝阻 - 退出并责怪每个人,但你自己除外 首发时间为45分钟,

text: Luck = High energy In the right place Around the right people Producing more than consuming Avoiding any and all distractions Able to manage multiple interests High agency and self-educated Doesn't quit after one mistake Luck is a lifestyle
zh: 运气=高能 在正确的地方, 正确的人生产 超过消费 避免任何和所有的分心 管理多重利益 高级代理和自我教育 在一个错误之后不退出 幸运是一种生活方式

text: The gameplan: - Build an audience - Become useful (do something) - Start with a "personal system" product (info or software) - Build cashflow - fund your own dreams The internet made this accessible to anyone (has big downsides) Here's a full guide: thedankoe.com/letters/the-1-…
zh: 游戏计划: - 构建观众 - 成为有用的(做点什么) - 从"个人系统"产品(信息或软件)开始 - 构建现金流 - 为你自己的梦想提供资金 互联网让任何人可以使用(有大缺点) 这里有完整的指南:thedankoe.com/letters/the-1-.

text: The solution to your problem isn't found in one thing, it's found in the experimentation with many things until you create your own thing.
zh: 解决你的问题的方法 在一个事物中找不到, 它在实验中发现 与很多事物 直到你创建自己的事物。

text: "I tried for 6 months and it didn't work." That's the problem. You tried for 6 months. You didn't improve for 6 months. You followed a list of steps and gave up your agency. You kept doing what wasn't working, didn't realize the problem, and didn't experiment to solve it.
zh: "我试过六个月,但没成功" 问题就在这里 你试过六个月了 你六个月没好转了 你跟着一个步骤清单 放弃了你的代理。 你继续做那些没有用的事,没有意识到问题所在,也没有尝试去解决.

text: If you don't know what to write next, use a: - Pain point - Example - Personal story - Anecdote - Statistic - Metaphor - Quote - Advice - What, how, why Those are the building blocks of most writing. Once you have an outline, you can pick a few of those to write each section.
zh: 如果你不知道接下来要写什么,请使用一个: - 疼痛点 - 实例 - 个人故事 - 异闻 - 统计学 - 元数据 - 引用 - 咨询 - 什么,如何,为什么这些是大多数写作的构件. 一旦有了大纲,可以选几个来写每个部分.

text: Family time is limited. Friend time is limited. Partner time is significant. Children time is precious. Coworker time is significant. Alone time is highest. Become a time billionaire. Pod with @SahilBloom is out: youtu.be/vG4dnVJTA9w
zh: 家庭时间有限。 朋友的时间有限。 合伙人的时间很长。 孩子们的时间是宝贵的。 同事的时间是相当长的。 独处时间是最高的。 成为亿万富翁 与@SahilBloom一起出道:yutu.be/vG4dnVJTA9w

text: Ideas are the most valuable resource because a decent idea gets decent results and a genius idea gets genius results. One person can work 16 hours while the other works 30 minutes, and if the latter has a better idea, they will get better results. Ideas are leverage.
zh: 思想是最有价值的资源,因为一个体面的想法会获得体面的结果,一个天才的想法会获得天才的结果. 一个人可以工作16小时,而其他人则工作30分钟,如果后者有更好的想法,他们就会得到更好的结果. 思想是杠杆。

text: The first step to being creative is not trying to be creative.
zh: 创造的第一步不是试图创造。

text: There's about 6 other things you need to get right to escape beginner hell: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: 另有6件事情你需要获得 右逃初学者地狱: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to.

text: How to have a brilliant idea: Flood your mind with as much as it can tolerate until you feel like you're going insane, then fuck off. Let go of it all and watch the perfect idea surface right when you need it.
zh: 如何有一个聪明的主意: 尽量用它来让你的头脑充满了忍耐,直到你觉得自己疯了,然后滚蛋. 放手,看着完美的想法 随时随地浮现出来

text: The best way to reduce the anxiety of a big task is to start with the smallest possible action. If you can stick with it for 5 minutes, most of your worries vanish.
zh: 减轻一项大任务焦虑的最好办法是从最小的可能行动开始. 如果你能坚持5分钟, 你的大部分担心就消失了。

text: If you're a beginner, nobody knows who you are. You have to prove your worth before people start caring enough to pay you. Make noise. Be annoying. Show your work in public. Do everything you can to get in front of the right people. All you need is one bite to change everything.
zh: 如果你是初学者, 没人知道你是谁. 你必须证明你的价值 在人们开始关心你之前 作声声. 烦恼之心. 公显其功. 尽一切法得正法众前. 你只需要一口咬一咬就能改变一切

text: A pattern found in famous creatives: They balance intense intellectual work with deep rest. They embody the work hard play hard mentality. When its time to work, they work. When it's time to rest, they do not think about work, and surprisingly, that's when their best ideas come.
zh: 知名创意中发现的图案: 他们兼顾了紧张的智力工作与深沉的休息. 他们体现了努力的工作 玩弄强硬的心态。 当它工作的时候,它们就起作用了。 当休息的时候,他们不考虑工作, 令人惊讶的是,这就是他们最好的主意。

text: The new business playbook: - Start as a creator - Build an audience - Build cash & leverage - Enjoy life... - Or use it to fuel a startup I talk with Stan co-founders ($30M ARR in 3 years) here: youtu.be/p6n4Q6-N7XI
zh: 新业务游戏本: - 开始创作 - 构建受众 - 构建现金与杠杆 - 享受生活. 或用它来为启动者加油 我和斯坦共同创始人谈了3年30MARR在这里:youtu.be/p6n4Q6-N7XI

text: I've never understood the desire for creators to monetize with platform ad revenue or paywalled content (like a newsletter) - It's harder to create a compelling offer - You naturally give less value than you could - Less people buy because it's often recurring You're guaranteed less revenue, and your audience is guaranteed less value. Not feeling ready isn't an excuse. If you can paywall content, you can charge for a product. And if you want to go the "donations" or "tips" route, just ask them to support you by buying the product. At least they actually get something in that case.
zh: 我从未理解创造者想要用平台广告收入或付费内容(如通讯)货币化的愿望, 很难创造出令人信服的报价, 你自然会给的比你能给的少。 买的人少,因为经常重复, 你得到的保证是收入少, 你的观众得到的保证是价值少。 感觉不成熟不是借口 能够支付墙体内容的,可以收取产品费用. 如果你想走"捐赠"或"尖"路线,就请他们通过购买产品来支持你. 至少他们真的得到了什么 在那个箱子。

text: The most important thing you can do when the world is changing rapidly is learn.
zh: 当世界迅速变化时,你所能做的最重要的事情就是学习。

text: If you wouldn’t switch places with them, take their opinion lightly.
zh: 也请稍稍接受他们的意见。

text: Ideas are so cool. You just sit there minding your own business and boom you have a completely new direction to take your life.
zh: 思想真酷 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说吧 , 说 , 说 , 说 说 , 说 说 , 说 , 说 说 , 说 说 说 , 说 说 说 说 , 说 说 说 说 , 说 说 , 说 说 说 , 说 说 说 说 说 , 说 , 说 说 说 说 说 说 , 说 说 说 说 说 , 说 说 说 , 说 说 说 说 说 说 你只是坐在那里 关心你自己的生意 繁荣你有一个全新的方向 取你的命。

text: Learning comes from struggle, not memorization. Start the project. No, don't watch 20 tutorials beforehand. Let it expose the gaps in your knowledge. Try to figure it out. Search for the answer when your mind is most likely to remember it. Publish the project. Repeat for life.
zh: 学习来自斗争,而不是回忆. 启动项目。 不,别事先看20个辅导课 让它暴露你的知识差距 试解其法. 寻找答案 当你的头脑最有可能记住它。 发布项目. 复说终身.

text: Always remember: - Never promote yourself - Be a slave to platform revenue - Even better, beg for donations - Writing is sacred and money is evil - New writers especially, don’t even think about making a living
zh: 永远记住: 永远不要自我提升 做一个奴隶 以获得收入 更需要 乞求捐赠 写作是神圣的,金钱是邪恶的 特别是新作家,不要想谋生

text: Being miserable is a choice. You choose to hold a perspective that is incomplete. You choose to narrow in on the problem. You choose to ignore the solution. You give up control of your mind to externalities. There is a way you can view the situation that gives you control.
zh: 痛苦是选择 你选择了不完全的视角。 你选择缩小问题范围。 你选择忽略解决方案。 你放弃了对外部因素的控制 你可以看清你所掌控的情况

text: You aren't where you want to be because your standards match where you are.
zh: 你没有你想去的地方 因为你的标准 符合你现在的位置。

text: Filter every thought, emotion, decision, habit, problem, or goal through your vision and drop them without question if they don't align.
zh: 过滤每一个想法,情感,决定,习惯,问题,或目标 通过你的视觉,并不加质疑地放弃它们,如果它们不对齐的话.

text: Okay. 5,000 word masterclass on how to escape beginner hell as a creator is out. If you have been grinding with zero progress for 3-6 months, you're probably missing something: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh:  5000个字的大师课 关于如何作为创造者逃离初学者地狱 已经出来了 如果你在3-6个月里 毫无进展 你可能错过了一些东西:thedankoe.com/letters/how-to...

text: Anti-vision is the future you refuse to accept. The dumb mind. The robotic work. The soft body. The numb relationships. Let a terrible image build up in your mind. Remind yourself of it every day. Then, channel everything you have into the complete opposite.
zh: 反视觉是你们拒绝接受的未来 哑哑人心. 机器人的作品。 柔软体者. 麻木的关系。 让可怕的形象在你的脑海里逐渐形成. 每天都提醒自己这一点。 然后,把一切你拥有的 完全相反。

text: Normalize disappearing for a few months to work on your vision.
zh: 正常消失几个月 才能恢复你的视力

text: You don't magically become focused. You practice it. You notice you're distracted and snap out of it immediately, over and over again until it's second nature. Most people have not trained this muscle, and they are not anywhere close to as focused as they think they are.
zh: 你没有神奇地变得专注。 你来练习 你注意到你分心了 立刻地 一遍又一遍地从中分心 直到它变成第二自然 大多数人没有训练这种肌肉,他们也没有像他们想象的那么集中。

text: If you want to take control of your life: - Wake up an hour earlier - 30 minutes moving your body - 30 minutes building a skill or business - Practice mindfulness at each second of the day Swap an hour at night (when motivation is zero) for lever-moving tasks in the morning.
zh: 如果你想控制你的生活: - 提前一小时醒来 - 移动身体30分钟 - 建立技能或业务30分钟 - 白天每秒练习注意时间 晚上交换一小时(当动机为零) 进行操纵操纵任务 早上

text: I may be young and dumb, but retirement feels like a scam. My goal isn't to stop working, or to finally do what I want, or to eliminate my sense of purpose and contribution to the world. My goal is to design a life filled with work that I don't want to escape from.
zh: 我可能年轻又笨 但退休感觉就像骗局 我的目标不是停止工作,或者最终做我想做的事,或者消除我对世界的使命感和贡献. 我的目标是设计一个充满工作的生活 我不想逃避

text: Most people don’t have a routine. They have a rut. A rut they didn’t choose but got stuck in because it was the safest option. If you don’t design your day, someone else will. And their design isn't meant to lead to the results you want.
zh: 大部分人没有例行公事。 他们有种鲁特。 因为他们没有选择, 其他人会的。 并且他们的设计并不是要导致你想要的结果.

text: I send a life reset planner to everyone who joins my newsletter. It will help you dig deep into what you want, what you don't want, and what to do about it: thedankoe.com/letters
zh: 我派了一位生活重设计划员 给加入我通讯的每个人 这会帮助你深入挖掘你想要的,你不想的, 以及应该做什么:thedankoe.com/letters

text: If your answer to “why do you do what you do?” is only “to make money” you should probably change what you do, and you’ll probably make more money.
zh: ”如果你回答“为什么做你做的事?” , 可能你应该改变你的行为,

text: The worst place you can be is not loving your life, but at the same time, not hating it enough to do something great.
zh: 你最糟糕的地方是不爱自己的生活,但同时又不恨它做一些伟大的事情.

text: The select few people who know they can learn and build everything necessary to succeed just need the pressure that eliminates distractions and forces focus. Quit the job. Move into the apartment you can't afford. Your delusional self-belief will take care of the rest.
zh: 少数知道自己能学习和建设一切成功所必要的人,只需要消除分散注意力和力量集中的压力。 辞去职. 搬进你买不起的公寓 你的妄想自信心会照顾其他的

text: The best time to start as a creator is now because follower count means nothing with the For You page. If you know what you’re doing (important) then you can land insane brand deals, make $500k+ at a company, or build your own thing with a tiny following.
zh: 现在作为创作者开始的最好时机是,因为跟随者数对"For You"页面毫无意义. 如果你知道自己在做什么(重要), 你可以把疯狂的品牌交易, 在公司里赚500美元+, 或者用小的跟随来建造自己的东西。

text: If you're young and don't know where to start, go to the gym. It will teach you that you're supposed to feel lost at first, progress is made in 5-pound increments, people don't look at or care about you, and long-term gratification is one of the greatest feelings in the world.
zh: 如果你年轻,不知道从哪里开始, 去健身房。 它会教你,你应该 感觉迷失在一开始, 进步是在5磅的增量, 人们不看 或关心你, 长期满足是世界上最伟大的感觉之一。

text: The 2 hours you spend scrolling each day (or 730 hours each year) could have produced a book, a business, or a body you don't currently have.
zh: 每天2小时(或每年730小时)的滚动时间, 可能已经产生了一本书,一个企业,或者一个你目前没有的躯体。

text: Most people think their dream is to become a YouTuber when that's just their soul begging them to create something, anything, and share it with the world because happiness stems from the intersection of a challenge and connection to something greater than yourself.
zh: 大多数人认为他们的梦想是成为YouTube的使用者, 当他们的灵魂要求他们创造东西,任何东西, 并分享它与世界, 因为幸福来自一个挑战的交汇点, 和比自己更大的事物的联系。

text: Your future will be determined by 3 things. And no, those things don't involve Uber Eats, TikTok, or standing on the edge because you missed out on Trump coin (guilty). They involve starting your life over from scratch. 1) One big decision to go all in. Start with what you hate the most about yourself. Commit to achieving the opposite. Zero distractions. Z e r o. 2) Moving 1 lever a day, minimum. A lever is something that actually gets results. If you aren't getting results, you aren't doing the right things. No exceptions. 3) The ability to persist and iterate. You don't quit. You pivot. There is a series of actions that lead to the life you want. You will have to change direction multiple times. If your actions create your future, and you aren't where you want to be, then wave goodbye to almost everything you do on a daily basis.
zh: 你的未来将由三件事决定 不,那些东西并不涉及Uber Eats, TikTok, 或者站在边缘 因为你错过了特朗普硬币(犯罪). 包括从头开始你的生活 1) 一个大决定,全程进入. 开始你最讨厌的自我。 承诺实现相反的目标。 零分分心. Z e r o 2) 每日移动1个杠杆,最少. 杠杆是真正得到结果的东西。 如果你没有得到结果, 你做错事了。 无例外。 3) 坚持和延展的能力. 汝不退却. 你个小混混 一系列的行动 导致你想要的生活。 您必须多次改变方向 。 如果你的行为创造了你的未来,而你却不是你想成为的地方,那么就挥手告别,几乎每天做的一切.

text: It will never be perfect. Launch it. Post it. Put it out into the world when it’s 90% of the way there and let people think what they want to think. If you're doing anything worthwhile, you must publish your work consistently, and if you can't even do that once... good luck.
zh: 它永远不会完美。发布它，发出去。在完成度达到 90% 时就把它放到世界上，让别人爱怎么想就怎么想。如果你在做任何值得做的事，就必须持续发布自己的作品；如果连一次都做不到……那就祝你好运。

text: Early morning or late night. Pick one. Those are the only times when the world doesn't demand your energy. Those are the times when you're going to do your best work. Those are when you move the needle toward the life you want.
zh: 早或晚时. 选一个 世界上唯一没有需要你能量的时代 那时你将尽你最大的努力 当你把针头移向你想要的生活时

text: Being extra intentional with the next topic / hook - will see how it does
zh: 与下一个话题/钩子一起被额外故意 - 将看到它是如何做的

text: It'll definitely still take some effort for creating a system to organize them in Kortex, but I'm happy to help with that at any time. Best place to do so would be the discord (if you want to join, of course - no need to use Kortex either if you just want to bounce ideas!) discord.gg/kortex
zh: 建立系统来组织它们 肯定需要一定的努力 但我很高兴随时能帮忙 最好还是不和(如果你想加入, 当然, 如果你只想反弹想法, 也不必使用 Kortex! ) 。 gg/kortex 的不和 。

text: If you can face one small fear a day you will find what it means to live a good life
zh: 如果你一天能面对一个小的恐惧 你会找到生活美好的意义

text: Work is a source of fulfillment, just like any other area of your life, if you’re the one who designs it.
zh: 工作是成就的来源, 就像你生活中任何其他领域一样, 如果你是设计它的人。

text: Closing in on the big 1m and pretty annoyed I go through these lulls in views every few months. Videos do really well, then 4-5 of them do way worse. When I look back on them, I realize that the videos just aren't good enough. Not that the content itself is bad or not helpful, but you can see a drastic difference in the topic, title, and thumbnail choice. I can almost always map these "lulls" to a period when ideas aren't flowing well. I'm not reading as much. I'm not learning anything new. I'm trying to force things out for the sake of staying consistent. What's helped me break through these points is just that: Doing a mental reset. Throwing all planned content ideas out the window and starting from scratch. Picking up a new book. Learning from new people. Realigning with the core ideas that I'm best at articulating and adding a twist. The reason I write this is because I see many others go through the same lulls, or they never make it out of the beginner lull that everyone starts out in. What helps is just being brutally honest with how good the video actually is. It can be good in your eyes, which is important, but if others don't care about it, it's missing a crucial aspect of what makes it "good." (P.S. I know 50k+ views is nothing to scoff at. Speaking relative to the normal baseline.)
zh: 接近大一米,我非常烦恼 每几个月在观景台里看到这些灯光 视频真的很好, 然后4 -5 做比这更糟。 当我回想起他们时,我意识到这些视频不够好。 内容本身不是不好或没有帮助,但您可以看到在主题、标题和缩略语选择上的巨大差异。 我几乎可以把这些"卢尔"画成一个 思想流得不畅的时期。 读书不尽如是. 我什么新知识也没有 我试图强迫事情 保持一致。 帮助我突破这些点的只是: 做一个精神重置。 将所有计划的内容想法抛出窗口,从头开始. 拾得新书. 向新人学习. 重新调整我最擅长的 阐述和增加一个曲折的核心思想。 我写这个的原因是因为我看到许多其他人经历同样的路由,或者他们从来没有从每个人开始的初学者路由中走出来. 影片的制作效果如何, 它可以在你眼中很好,这很重要,但如果别人不关心,它就会缺少一个关键方面,它使得它"好"(P.S. 我知道50k+观点没有什么可嘲笑的. 说话相对正常基线. )

text: Sahil sent me an early copy out of kindness. If you’d like to preorder, do so here and support the man: amazon.com/gp/product/059…
zh: 萨希尔出于善意提前给我寄了份副本. com/gp/products/059. 校对:Soup

text: This is going to be a best seller. Well done @SahilBloom
zh: 这会是畅销书 不错的功劳 @ SahilBloom

text: Those who control their inner experience (how they interpret and order the chaos in their heads) control their quality of life.
zh: 控制内在经历(如何解释和秩序头上的混乱)的人,控制着自己的生活质量.

text: If you struggle to think clearly If you can't articulate your thoughts If you feel like your ideas aren't original Here's how you can become respected for the quality of your mind: youtu.be/jhoG3WcECnw
zh: 如果你想得清楚的话 如果你不能表达你的想法 如果你觉得自己的思想不是原创的 如何在思想品质上 变得受人尊重:yetu.be/jhoG3WcECnw

text: What I mean is if you aren't making progress, you need to experiment... not quit.
zh: 我的意思是如果你没有进步, 你需要实验... 而不是放弃。

text: 3-6 months of beginner hell (making close to zero progress and questioning whether it's worth it) is a natures way of filtering out people who weren't serious in the first place.
zh: 3-6个月的初学者地狱(接近零进步,质疑是否值得)是过滤那些一开始并不严肃的人的自然方式.

text: If you aren't making progress, you aren't doing the right things.
zh: 如果你不进步,你做错事了

text: You aren't where you want to be because you haven't put yourself in the situations that would force you to get there.
zh: 你没有去你想去的地方 因为你没有把自己置于 强迫你到那里的情况。

text: The brutal truth is that nobody cares about the hard work you put in. They care about what they get from it. And if it isn't good, they'll either rip you apart or ignore you. Be careful betting everything on "hard work."
zh: 残酷的事实是,没有人关心你付出的辛勤劳动. 他们关心从中得到什么. 如果情况不妙,他们要么把你撕成碎片 要么无视你 仔细的把一切赌注在"辛苦工作"上.

text: Block out 1 hour a day. Work on 1 meaningful project. Aim for 1 vision for your future. Take it 1 day at a time, for 1 year. 1 hour of deep work is better than 0 hours, and you can drastically change your life in 365 hours.
zh: 一天堵上一个小时 开展1个有意义的项目。 瞄准未来一线 一次拿一天,一年。 。 深活1小时比0小时好,365小时后你可以急剧改变生活.

text: You don't know what to write because: - Your standards (as a beginner) are too high - You feel as if everything's been written before (it has, but not by you) - You have nothing worth writing about (go DO something, you'll have something to write)
zh: 你不懂写什么 因为: - 你的标准(作为初学者)太高了 - 你感觉好像一切都是以前写的(它已经写过,但不是你写的) - 你没有什么值得写的东西(去做点什么,你会有东西写)

text: You lack confidence because you only expose yourself to the respectable sides of other people. You don't see their humanity. You don't see their struggles and shortcomings. You become confident when you can see people outside of the fake mental image you've created of them.
zh: 你缺乏信心是因为你只暴露在别人的可敬的一面. 你看不到他们的人性 你看不出他们的斗争和缺点. 当你看到别人在你的假精神形象之外 变得自信

text: AI only ruins creativity for those who don't know what creativity is. If AI can be as creative as you, you weren't very creative in the first place.
zh: AI只会破坏那些不知道什么是创造力的人的创造力. 如果AI能像你一样有创意,那么你一开始就不是很有创意.

text: I absolutely despise bullet point summary culture. "Can you condense this into 5 key points so I can get my dopamine hit and move on to the next?" No. I want the fluff. I want the context. I want the random and chaotic thought process that contains lessons that can't be taught.
zh: 我绝对鄙视弹点即兴文化 "你能把这个压缩成5个关键点 让我的多巴胺被击中 然后继续往下走?" 不 我要绒毛 我要上下文,我需要你帮忙 我要的是随机和混乱的思维过程,它包含着无法教得的教训.

text: Once you understand this discipline becomes a lot easier: If you don't want to do something, but that something leads to what you want to do, then you want to do it.
zh: 一旦你明白这个纪律会变得容易得多: 如果你不想做点什么,但事情导致你想要做的事,那么你就想做.

text: It starts as curiosity, turns into interest, slowly becomes purpose, is sustained with profit, and evolves into mastery.
zh: 它从好奇心开始,变成兴趣,慢慢变成目的,用利润来维持,并逐渐变成掌握.

text: A few beliefs: - Short form is good, long form is great, short form + long form is incredible - Writing one high quality piece and distributing everywhere is better than low quality for all platforms For those interested in how I write newsletters + YT: youtu.be/42NxzmQBkMA
zh: 少数信仰: - 短形式好,长形式好,长形式好,短形式+长形式好,令人难以置信 - 写一部高质量的作品,到处分发,比所有平台的低质量更好. 给那些有兴趣我如何写通讯+YT:youtu.be/42NxzmQBkMA的人.

text: Pay attention to where you stop short. The point where it gets too uncomfortable and you fall back into your normal life. The point where all the negative thoughts flood in and you can't handle them. The point where all the magic happens if you learn to enjoy pushing through.
zh: 注意你停在短路的地方 让你感觉太不舒服 回到正常生活 也就是所有负面的想法都涌进你无法处理的地方. 如果你学会享受推车的乐趣,所有魔法都会发生的点.

text: Mobile (app) is finally coming, hopefully this week which will be nice Michael - curious to hear what you think when AI stuff goes in. We initially thought of having built in commands like “generate blog” but decided we will just give those out as starter prompts outside of the app for those who want to try it. Hoping that users will find creative ways to use it
zh: 手机(app)终于来了, 希望这周会是很好的迈克尔, 好奇地听到你的想法, 却决定把那些作为应用程序外的启动提示, 提供给想尝试的人。 希望用户能找到创造性的使用方法

text: It sucks to feel lost. You can't just "set a goal" because you don't know what you want. You can't just "solve a problem" because you don't know how. Here's what you do if you feel stuck in the middle: youtu.be/Lt79hNo6SWE
zh: 感觉很不爽 你不能因为不知道自己想要什么就"设定目标". 你不能因为不知道怎么解决就"解决一个问题". 如下: 感觉被困在中间: yetu.be/Lt79hNo6SWE

text: If you aren't where you want to be, the most important thing in your life is one hour of needle-moving effort (when your energy is highest) on a project that wasn't assigned to you by parents, teachers, or employers.
zh: 如果你不想要去的地方, 你生命中最重要的事情是 一个小时的针头运动努力(当你的能量最高的时候) 一个没有被父母,老师,或雇主分配给你的项目。

text: The problem: You don't want to share your thoughts because you feel like they aren't original. But original thinking isn't about always having new ideas. It's about finding little known ideas and sharing them in a new way: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: 问题在于: 你不想分享你的想法 因为你觉得这些想法不是原创的 但原创思想并不是总有新想法. 主要是寻找鲜为人知的想法,并以新方式分享:thedankoe.com/letters/how-to.

text: Schedule interruptions in your day to help you remember that your daily mindless string of tasks aren't life. Think about your death. Go on a walk and stare at the sky. Close your eyes and listen to a beautiful song. Snap yourself out of mechanical living.
zh: 日程中断 帮助你记住 你日常的无脑任务不是生命 想想你的死讯 继续走,盯着天空看 闭目听一首美歌. 振作起来,从机械生活。

text: You should be able to try them out on the free tier as well when they go out! Unfortunately, no discounts for a few reasons: - I want to stick to treating kortex as separate from my brand. It’s a different team and company - It technically is discounted right now, as the price will be going up once AI features go in. We were going to add them as another price on top like Notion, but decided to bake them in considering it will be much more engrained in kortex beyond just chatting and asking questions to AI. - We merged premium with creator central, which was $49/month, so premium being $21 kinda hurts lol Long winded way of saying this is the discounted price, also because we still technically aren’t “launched” yet
zh: 你应该能在自由的层次上 试一下,当他们出去的时候! 不幸的是,由于一些原因,没有折扣:——我坚持把Kortex作为与品牌分开的处理. 团队和公司不同, 技术上,它现在被折扣, 因为价格会上升 一旦AI的特性进入。 我们本来打算把它们加起来,像Notion那样作为另一个顶价,但决定烤成这样,因为考虑到它会更被吸进去,而不是只聊天和向AI提问。 加上创造中心, 每月49美元, 21美元的价格会伤害Lol Long 风流的说法, 这也是折扣价格, 也因为我们在技术上还不是“启动”的。

text: How to think originally: - Stop trying to think originally - Write good ideas, from your perspective - Read books that few people have - Resurface old ideas from a new angle - Question all beliefs, even the ones you know to be true - Do one thing that most people don't, daily
zh: 如何从最初的思维: - 停止尝试从最初的思维 - 写好想法,从你的角度 - 读少有的书 - 从新的角度重新展现旧思想 - 质疑所有信仰, 即使你知道的信仰都是真实的 - 做一件大多数人不认为的事情,每天都

text: The solution to most of your problems is perspective.
zh: 解决你大部分问题的方法是透视

text: Desktop app is out: x.com/kortexco/statu…
zh: 桌面应用程序已退出: x.com/kortexco/statu...

text: The best way to learn is to commit to a real world project that forces you to learn what can't be taught in schools: skills that get non-linear results, how to navigate failure, and how to think outside the box.
zh: 学习的最好方法是致力于一个现实世界项目,它迫使你学习一些学校无法教的东西:获得非线性结果的技能,如何导航失败,以及如何在盒子外思考.

text: Voice transcription (with autoformatting) is built out and on the way this month. We plan to have chatting with your notes at the end of the month but that's an aggressive target so could extend a few days. If you join the discord and let us know what you think as they roll out, we can make changes that suit what you have in mind :) If you're not already in here's the link (no need to join of course, just plugging) discord.gg/kortex
zh: 语音抄写(带有自动格式化)本月建成并沿途完成. 我们计划月底跟你的笔记聊天 但那是一个攻击性的目标 所以可以持续几天 如果你加入其中,让我们知道 当他们展开时,你的想法, 我们可以做出一些改变, 适合你的想法: 如果你还没有在这里的链接(当然不需要加入,只是插上)不和.gg/kortex

text: Going in this month: - Desktop app (today) - Light mode (finally) - Mobile app (finally) - Full offline mode - Images, embeds, files in capture - Voice transcription - KAI release (maybe) Been building up to this a while
zh: 本月内进行: - 桌面应用(今天) - 轻模式(最终) - 移动应用(最终) - 完全离线模式 - 图像,嵌入,文件在抓取中 - 语音抄录 - KAI发布(可能) 已经建置了一段时间
