text: I know that everyone wants to build a note-taking/writing/productivity app. I know that we're stupid for doing it. I know we're going to waste a lot of time and money. I know that we could make 100x more, faster, doing something else. Don't care. Still doing it. Team is hungry.
zh: 我知道每个人都想建立一个记录/写作/生产力应用。 我知道我们这样做很愚蠢 我知道我们会浪费很多时间和金钱 我知道我们可以让100x 更快,做别的事。 无所谓 还在做呢 团队饿了。

text: You aren't lost. You're too used to what you know. New becomes normal very quick. When you feel lost, the solution is to become more lost. Throw yourself out of your current way of life and I promise you'll find something to grab onto.
zh: 你没有迷路 你对所知道的太习惯了 新的变得很正常,很快。 当你觉得迷失时, 解决办法就是变得更迷失。 离开你现在的生活方式 我保证你会找到东西抓住的

text: More on creating a life worth being productive for: thedankoe.com/letters/the-fa…
zh: 更多关于创造值得为生产而生活:thedankoe.com/letters/the-fa.

text: They won't believe in you. The business. The crazy idea. The aspirations so large that most people's minds don't have any other option but to call you delusional. And you are, but only if you don't make it happen.
zh: 他们不会相信你的 业. 疯狂的想法。 如此巨大的愿望,以至于大多数人的头脑除了叫你妄想之外,没有其他选择. 而你是,但只有你没有 让它发生。

text: do anything to find that one thing
zh: 做任何事情来找到一件事

text: A plan is an educated guess. A surface area for luck. A mental frame to keep chaos at bay. A lens to notice ideas that lead to progress. A strategy that changes as you act. A plan is not something you write down once and tell everyone about to feel like you're making progress.
zh: 计划是经过教育的猜测 地表面积,以示好运 一种能防止混乱的心理框架 一种能发现导致进步的想法的透镜. 一种改变你行为的方式 计划不是写下来的 告诉大家你正在进步

text: You can just start over. You don't need permission. You don't need to wait for the right time. You can stop what you're doing, right now, and move in a new direction. Your mind will do anything to convince you to stay the same, but you don't have to listen.
zh: 你可以重新开始了 你不需要许可 不必等正时. 你可以停止你正在做的事,现在, 并朝着一个新的方向移动。 你的思想会做任何事情来说服你保持原状,但你不必听.

text: The single thing that keeps most people stuck is that they do what they want but never self-reflect and realize they don't actually want that.
zh: 唯一能让大多数人困住的就是 他们做他们想要的,但永远不能自我反思,意识到他们其实并不想要。

text: Do what you want > reflect on what you don't want > refine what you want > do what you want > repeat until success
zh: 做你想做的事 > 反思你不想做的事 > 细化你想要的东西 > 做你想做的事 > 重复到成功

text: One of the biggest questions everyone is asking: How do I stay relevant? This is nothing new, we've been asking that since the dawn of time, but a future filled with AI seems different. Here's one way to make sense of it: youtu.be/6WbZPRzrE4o
zh: 最大的问题之一 每个人都问: 我如何保持相关性? 这没什么新鲜事,我们从时间的黎明开始就问起,但充满AI的未来似乎不同了. 有个方法可以说明: youtu.be/6WbZPRzrE4o

text: Don't look for goals. Look for interesting problems and the right goal will emerge.
zh: 别找目标 寻找有趣的问题,并找到正确的目标。

text: Your goals determine what opportunities you can see, but most people run on a set of unconscious goals installed by their parents and peers, so they never notice the ideas in everyday situations, conversations, books, and content that could change their life.
zh: 你的目标决定了你能看到的机会, 但大多数人都追求着他们父母和同伴 设置的一套无意识的目标, 所以他们从不注意日常情况下的想法, 对话,书籍, 和内容, 可能改变他们的生活。

text: If you want to see a quick jump in quality of life start going on a 15 minute walk first thing in the morning, right after you wake up, no matter what the weather is.
zh: 如果你想看到快速的跳跃 生活质量开始走 15分钟步行 第一件事,你醒来后, 无论天气如何。

text: You need a plan. There isn't any other way. Because if you don't have one, society does, and they've been planning your life for decades: thedankoe.com/letters/the-fa…
zh: 你需要个计划 没有什么别的办法了 因为如果你没有,社会有, 他们已经规划了你几十年的生活:thedankoe.com/letters/the-fa...

text: What I would personally do in this case for journaling thoughts: 1) Go to capture (this is where mobile starts off) 2) Type "@" followed by the name of the document you want to create. I'd name it "Brain Dump" - then click "Create document" You can always organize and rename that later 3) Start jotting down all thoughts and feelings here - kinda like texting yourself ideas I use Capture for messy ideas I want to jot down. I use Documents for organized notes/writing. If you want to organize all of those thoughts later or reference them while writing, you can always open up the "Brain Dump" document you created to do so
zh: 我个人会做一些什么 来记录一些想法: 1) 去抓取(这是移动开始的地方) 2) 键入"@"后,再键入您要创建的文档名称. 我把它命名为"Brain Dummp" - 然后点击"创造文件" 你可以总是整理和重命名 以后3)开始记录所有的想法和感觉, 有点像在发短信给自己的想法, 我用抓来编织一些我想记下来的混乱想法。 我用文档来做笔记/笔记。 如果你想在以后整理所有这些想法,或者在写作时参考这些想法,你总是可以打开你为此创建的"脑垃圾堆"文档.

text: When your vision is clear nothing else matters. You drop everything. Books aren't interesting. Routines hold you back. If your mind wants to focus on reaching the next peak, don't slow it down. Let it consume you.
zh: 当你的眼光清晰时,别的事就不重要了。 你放弃了一切 书不趣. 常规会阻碍你 如果你的心想专心去达到下一个高峰,不要放慢速度. 让它吞噬你

text: If you want to attract people to you, focus on your mission. People gravitate toward those who know what they want.
zh: 如果你想吸引人们来你身边 专心完成你的使命 人们向那些知道自己想要什么的人倾斜

text: The plan for 2025 is to write, walk, read, build, train, and adapt as usual.
zh: 2025年的计划是写作,步行,阅读,建设,训练,并照常适应.

text: The difference between where you are and where you want to be is knowledge because if you knew how to get there (and actually wanted to) you wouldn't be able to think or do anything else.
zh: 与你现在的位置和想要的地方的区别在于知识,因为如果你知道如何到达(并且实际上想要),你就不会有其他的想法或做其他的事情.

text: You need to write. Especially when most people outsource their thinking. Writing is like placing your thoughts as legos on a table, seeing how ugly they are, and rebuilding them from scratch. You are literally reprogramming how your mind thinks in a tangible form.
zh: 写作吧 特别是大多数人将自己的思想外包. 写作就像把你的想法当作腿放在桌子上, 看着他们有多丑陋, 并从头开始重建。 你正在重新编程 你的思维方式是有形的

text: It's so much easier to think while on a walk. There's a rhythm your mind falls into with each step. It's like your mind is walking through idea space. When you sit still, you can only grab the ideas that are close to you. Everything else is dark.
zh: 散步时想起来容易多了 每一步都有你的心跳 就像你的思想在走进思想空间 静坐时,只能抓住与你亲近的想法. 其它的都黑了 都黑了 都黑了 都黑了 都黑了

text: Productivity is about quality, not quantity. And if one idea results in 10x higher quality work, true productivity is dependent on creativity. With AI handling more repetitive work, ideas matter more than ever. Here's how you design a creative life: youtu.be/B18O2QtkyCE
zh: 生产力是质量问题,而不是数量问题。 如果一个想法能带来10x更高的质量,那么真正的生产力就依赖于创造力. AI处理的重复性工作比以往任何时候更加重要。 以下是你如何设计创作生活: yutu.be/B18O2QtkyCE

text: You need a plan. But most people don't understand what a plan is. A plan is a mental frame to help you focus on the right things. It's supposed to change and evolve as you go. What most people think is a plan is just a list of steps that won't be relevant in a week.
zh: 你需要个计划 但大多数人都不明白什么是计划. 计划是帮助你专注于正确事物的心理框架. 应该是随着你走的改变和进化的 大多数人认为计划只是列出一个一周内将无关的步骤.

text: It really does all boil down to doing whatever you want and error-correcting along the way.
zh: 事情真的会变得很复杂 随你便 一路都纠错

text: You don't build an irreplaceable skill stack doing replaceable work.
zh: 你没有建造一个不可替代的技能堆 做可替代的工作。

text: The AGI overlords are coming and everyone is losing their minds. Will we be insects to them? Will they surpass us? Will life as we know it change? Here's my take, I think it will help: thedankoe.com/letters/agi-wi…
zh: AI的霸主来了 每个人都疯了 我们对他们来说是昆虫吗? 他们能超越我们吗? 我们知道的生活会改变吗? 接下来我的想法是,我认为这将会有所帮助:thedankoe.com/letters/agi-wi...

text: People see the creator economy as some myopic new job where you "post content" to make a few bucks. When they realize it's a way to create—a fundamental human desire—without permission, they'll understand why it's the future, regardless if it's on what we call "social media."
zh: 人们把创造经济看作是一些微博的新工作,在那里你"贴上内容"赚了几块钱. 当他们意识到这是一种在未经许可的情况下创造人类基本欲望的方法时,他们会明白为什么这是未来,不管它是否出现在我们所谓的“社会媒体”上。

text: Start wide, try everything. Notice signal, ignore noise. Go deep, master it. Create something, anything. Share it with others. Enjoy and repeat.
zh: 开始宽,尝试一切。 注意信号,不要注意噪音 深究其义,师曰. 创造出什么,什么的。 与众共分. 享受和重复。

text: Nothing beats having a buildup of meaningful ideas to write about, throwing on headphones, blaring music so you're forced to drown it out, and emptying your mind just to do it all over again. Nothing beats being a vessel for creativity.
zh: 没有什么比 积累有意义的想法写, 扔在耳机, 高呼音乐 所以,你被迫淹死它, 并空心 只是为了做一遍。 没有什么比成为创造力的载体更能胜过它了。

text: The answers you're looking for are in the silence you're avoiding
zh: 你所寻找的答案是在沉默中 你躲开

text: It's liberating once you realize that the mind can be trained to do anything you want. That most of your lack is no more than a lack of skill, knowledge, action, and the logical sequence of steps it takes to achieve that thing. Unlike muscle on your body, your mind is infinite.
zh: 一旦你意识到 思想可以被训练成任何你想做的事, 就会解放 你最缺乏的只是缺乏技能,知识,行动, 以及实现这个目标所需步骤的逻辑顺序。 不像身体上的肌肉,你的头脑是无限的.

text: You don't need more knowledge because you already know what you need to do. You're studying level 5 at level 1 and it's only adding more things to forget about when you decide to start..
zh: 你不需要更多的知识,因为你已经知道自己需要做什么。 你正在学习5级 在1级, 它只是增加更多的事情 忘记 当你决定开始。

text: You don't find passion. You become passionate according to the amount of energy you invest in an interest.
zh: 你找不到激情,你没有 你变得热情 取决于你投入的精力 以利息。

text: Your audience doesn't want information. They want YOUR perspective on information. 10 people can share the same idea. Only one will say it in a way that changes minds.
zh: 你的观众不想要信息。 他们想要你对信息的看法 10人可以分享同样的想法. 只有一个人会用改变思想的方式说

text: You didn't start this to become another "personal brand." You started because you wanted freedom. Meaningful income. The ability to create work that sets your soul on fire. Take your mind, without the labels, and stream it where anyone can watch.
zh: 你开始这个不是想成为另一个"个人品牌" 你开始是因为你想要自由 有意义的收入。 创造工作的能力 让你的灵魂着火 带着你的头脑,没有标签, 流它在那里,任何人都可以观看。

text: Most "content creators" are just search engines with profile pictures. Publish work that makes people question their existence.
zh: 大多数"内容创建者"只是带有剖面图片的搜索引擎. 出版作品让人质疑自己的存在.

text: how to thrive in the future
zh: 如何在未来繁荣

text: I’m bullish on humans
zh: 我向人类发泄

text: You're allowed to be motivated by money. Scratch that, you are motivated by money. But you hide it because someone convinced you to think money is inherently impure. You work, right now, for money, to survive. Its a modern basic need. Don't apologize for wanting to live well.
zh: 允许你以金钱为动机 刮一下,你是为了钱 但你隐瞒了它 因为有人说服你认为钱是内在的不纯净 你现在工作是为了钱 是为了生存 这是现代的基本需要。 不想过上好日子就别道歉了

text: If you want to: - Write a book without a publisher - Make music without a record label - Code, design, or market without an employer Or turn any interest into independent work Social capital is what you're missing: youtu.be/DAgVDVHsApE
zh: ——没有出版商写作——没有唱片标签的音乐制作——代码,设计,或者没有雇主的市场. 或者将任何兴趣转化为独立工作 社会资本就是您所缺少的:yetu.be/DAgVDVHsApE

text: The future belongs to those who understand: - AI needs direction - Tools need masters - Data needs insight - Content needs context - Success needs vision Automation won't save you. Agency will.
zh: 未来属于理解者: - AI需要方向 - 工具需要主机 - 数据需要洞察 - 内容需要背景 - 成功需要愿景 自动化不会拯救你. 机构会的

text: The greatest lie of modern work: That all hours are created equal. That you're the same person at hour 12 as hour 1. That quantity beats quality. That busy means valuable.
zh: 现代工作最大的谎言: 时间是平等的 12点和1点是同一人 数量比质量高。 忙着意味着有价值。

text: You're not struggling with productivity. You're struggling with not having a project worth being productive for.
zh: 你没有在生产力方面挣扎 你正在努力争取一个值得我们做的项目

text: "The clever man may work smarter, not harder, they say, but the creative man doesn’t work at all." – Alex Pang The worlds most productive people were those who optimized for creativity, not productivity. Quality of work, not quantity. Try this: thedankoe.com/letters/the-fu…
zh: ”他们说,聪明人可能更聪明,而不是更努力,但创造力的人根本行不通。 ” 。 最具生产力的世界是那些最有利于创造力而不是生产力的人。 工作质量,不是数量. 试此:thedankoe.com/letters/the-fu... 试此.

text: Is it just me or are the holidays extremely boring if you aren't a child or parent. All I want to do is build and people look at me like I'm crazy for not laying on the couch all day.
zh: 无论是我还是假期 如果你不是孩子或父母,都是非常无聊的 我只要建起来 人们就看我一眼 我整天不躺在沙发上

text: Don't believe everything you think. Your mind is great at surfacing thoughts that don't matter as a test of your focus.
zh: 勿信一切想处. 你对表面思考的头脑很在行 而不是用来测试你的注意力

text: If I was starting from zero, here's what I would do: 1) Start on social media. I know I know. It's cringe and overblown at this point. But you have to understand a few points: - social media is the new media - media is where the attention is - media is how you attract people to your independent work - anyone can become a one-person media company - it's free, accessible, and arguably the highest leverage form of media today Whether you like it or not, if you want to do your own thing, social media is your best option. And no, it's not even close to saturated. Once you actually have experience in it, you see it. Last point: Building on social media forces you to *learn skills that actually matter.* You don't have to guess whether or not they are "worth learning" because you see the results. 2) Pair your personal interests with media-based skills. Start by attracting people to your interests. - Write about them while studying persuasion (posts, threads, newsletters) - Learn design basics for your banner, profile picture, and images - Supplement with education on audience building, storytelling, attention capture, etc. You learn faster when you have a project to apply your learning to. The cool thing about this: *You can apply almost any skill or interest you learn to social media.* Don't underestimate that power. 3) Monetize as soon as possible. You can wait to monetize an audience, or you can start right away. I recommend the latter. Why? Because the faster you fail, the faster you succeed. And, if you want it to do well, you need to again supplement with relative skills like offer creation. Your first product/service will suck whether you do it now or wait 6 months. - look at relevant products you've purchased in the past - look at who you follow and what they sell - think about the most impactful books you've read - look in your google drive or Kortex to see what you can slap a price tag on - create something similar, but with your own slight twist Business 101: sell what's already selling, but slightly better. Then, get unique and innovative with your next product if you want. If you want to learn how to create a micro-product or service, read this previous letter: thedankoe.com/letters/the-si…
zh: 如果我从零开始,我要做的就是:1)从社交媒体开始。 我知道 我知道 我认识他 I know I know. 此时正摇摆不定 但你们必须明白几点: 社交媒体是新媒体,媒体是关注的地方,媒体是你们如何吸引人们参与你们独立的工作,任何人都可以成为一家独人媒体公司,这是自由的,可及的,可以说是当今媒体的最高杠杆形式. 不管你喜欢与否,如果你想做你自己的事情,社交媒体是你们最好的选择. 和不,它甚至没有接近饱和。 一旦你真正有经验,你就能看到它。 最后一点:借助社交媒体,你不得不学习实际上很重要的技能。 你不必猜他们是不是"价值学习" 因为你看到了结果. 2) 以媒体为主的技能来评价你的个人利益。 首先吸引人们来关心你的兴趣 学习说服(海报、线条、通讯) 学习你旗帜、形象和图像的设计基础——补充关于观众建设、故事讲述、注意力捕捉等的教育。 有了应用你的学习的项目,你就会更快地学习. 很酷的是:*你可以将你所学的任何技能或兴趣应用到社交媒体中。* 别低估这种力量 3)尽快实现货币化. 你可以等观众货币化 或者你马上开始 我建议采用后者。 为什么? 因为失败的速度越快,成功的速度就越快. 如果你想要它做得很好, 你需要再次补充 相对技能,如提供创造。 你的第一个产品/服务会很烂 不管你现在还是等6个月 - 看看过去你买过的相关产品 - 看看你跟随谁,卖什么 - 想想你读过最有影响力的书 - 看看你的谷歌驱动器或Kortex 看你能拍什么价牌 - 创造类似的东西,但用你自己的微小扭矩 商業101:卖掉已经卖的东西,但稍好一点。 接下来,你需要的,就用你的下一份产品 来获得独特的创新。 如果你想学习如何创建微产品或服务,请阅读前一封信:thedankoe.com/letters/the-si...

text: Don't touch your phone in the morning. Releasing entropy right when you open your eyes is a great way to ruin the rest of your day.
zh: 明早别碰你的电话 睁开眼睛就放出 en是破坏余生的好方法.

text: If you don't know what to learn, try: - persuasion - storytelling - how to learn fast - emotional intelligence - audience building - attention capture - offer creation All of these amplify any other hard skill you can acquire and determine the results you get with them.
zh: 如果你不知道该学什么,试一下: 说服 - 讲故事 - 如何学习快速 - 情感智能 - 观众建设 - 注意力捕捉 - 提供所有这些扩展 任何其他的硬技能 你可以得到和决定你得到的结果。

text: It doesn't matter how long it takes. You started something new because you want a sense of zest in your life. The default mindless way of life wasn't cutting it for you. By quitting, you are effectively telling yourself that you aren't worth it. 4 years of school is fine. But 6-12 months building your own thing is too much? Because you have better things you could be doing? Really? What are those "better things?" More time to lounge around in front of a screen? Gain some fucking perspective. There is absolutely nothing more important than avoiding a robotic life.
zh: 何等为久. 你开始新事物是因为你想要一种 感性的生活。 那种没有头脑的生活方式 并不是为你而来 你辞职后,就告诉自己你并不值得 四年学无碍. 但是6到12个月造你的东西太过分了? 因为你有更好的事情 你可以做? 真的吗? 是什么"更好的东西"? 更多的时间休息 在屏幕前? 获得一些他妈的视角。 绝对没有比避免机器人生活更重要的了。

text: Writing is more than communication. Writing is how you practice thinking. Writing is how you document your development. Writing is how you attract supporters for your work. Writing will only go out of style when you outsource the creative ability of your mind.
zh: 写作不仅仅是沟通 写作是你们如何练习思考的。 写作是记录你的发展。 写作是吸引支持者参与你的工作的方式. 写作只有在你外包了自己心灵的创造能力时才会脱离风格.

text: You need 4 activities: Consume: learn something new. Create: build a meaningful project. Connect: with people and nature. Clear: your mind of rogue thoughts. Most of your problems stem from a stagnant mind that can be solved with a structure that encourages flow.
zh: 你需要4项活动: 康苏美:学习新事物. 创建:构建有意义的工程. 连线:与人和自然. 清:你对流氓思想的思念. 你们的问题大多来自于一种停滞的思维,这种思维可以通过鼓励流动的结构来解决.

text: Everyone should write a book at least once in their lifetime. Especially if you want to become a deep and articulate thinker. When you write a post or article, your mind expands to fit that context. You catch ideas that help you finish that specific project. But a book is different. It's extremely chaotic at first. You spill your subconscious into a blank document and start shaping it into chapters and sections. You create a little world for your mind to operate in. The longer you spend in that world, the more your mind expands to take that shape, and the more connections you start to make. When you're writing a book, you struggle a lot, but you'll find that your brain lights up with interest and excitement more than ever before. Almost any information or conversation you come across gives you an idea. You start to realize that your mind is only as perceptive as the project you're working on.
zh: 每个人都应该写一本书,至少一次在他们的一生。 尤其是你想成为 深奥而清晰的思想家 当你写文章的时候,你的思维会扩张,以适应这个背景. 你找到一些能帮助你完成这个具体项目的想法。 但书不同. 起初是极其混乱的. 你将你的潜意识 溢入空白文档 并开始将其塑造成章节。 你为你的心灵创造了一个小世界 在那个世界上花费的时间越长,你的心灵就越能扩展, 以达到这种形状, 和开始建立的联系就越多。 当你写书的时候,你挣扎得非常厉害, 但你会发现你的大脑比以往更充满兴趣和兴奋。 几乎任何你遇到的信息或谈话都会给你一个想法. 你开始意识到你的头脑 和你正在研究的项目一样敏锐

text: It's scary to admit that everything you believe is a story you've accepted or invented.
zh: 承认你相信的一切 都是一种你接受或发明的故事 是很可怕的

text: If you never contradict yourself, you're probably too attached to a limiting belief, and it's holding you back from reaching the next level.
zh: 如果你从未自相矛盾,你可能过于依赖一个有限的信仰,这让你无法到达下一个层次.

text: Learning from a mistake the first time is how you blow past everyone else who spend years in stagnation because their ego is too big to accept their faults.
zh: 第一次从错误中学习,就是 你如何吹过其他所有那些 多年来处于停滞状态的人, 因为他们的自我太大了,无法接受他们的过失。

text: Your feelings don't have to dictate your behavior. You can do the right thing, and you can do it well, even if you don't feel like doing it. This is the ultimate skill you can practice that spills over into the entirety of your life.
zh: 你不必用感情来决定你的行为 虽不觉行,亦能行其正,而能行其正,虽有不觉. 这是你所能够练习的 贯穿你整个生命的终极技能

text: I'll be writing about this in Saturday's Koe Letter. How maximum productivity is the result of maximizing creativity. Read this previous issue here and join to receive it: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: 我会在周六的"柯伊信"里写下这个 最大限度的生产力是如何实现创造力最大化的. 此处阅读前期并加入接收:thedankoe.com/letters/show-to.

text: Your life's work is to reach your potential. Plain and simple. Anything that leads to decay is a distraction that must be wrestled with and eliminated.
zh: 你毕生的工作就是发挥你的潜力. 简单明了 任何导致衰变的事物都是分散注意力,必须与之斗争并消除.

text: The only people who will ultimately be replaced by AI are those who are incapable (or unwilling) to learn how to run a business.
zh: 最终被AI取代的只有那些没有能力(或不愿意)学习经营企业的人.

text: The ultimate goal is to have a life so beautifully designed that work, rest, and play cease to exist. They all blur together and are indistinguishable.
zh: 最终目标是拥有一个如此美丽的生活 设计得如此美丽 工作,休息,游戏都不复存在。 都模糊在一起,无法区分。

text: You tried to fit in. You tried to follow the crowd. You tried to do the things that people told you would lead to success. But you're different. You're observant. You just lack clarity on how to create your own path. This may help: youtu.be/cG-brqeMrBw
zh: 你试图融入。 你试图跟随人群。 你试图做别人告诉你的事情 会导致成功。 但你不一样 汝等观察. 你只是缺乏 清晰如何创造自己的路径。 可能帮助: yetu.be/cG-brqe MrBw

text: You can't live a new life with your old actions. Once you finally realize that, you'll stop gripping on so tightly to the pleasures that don't serve you. If you really want to change, you will. Most people just fantasize about a better life as another source of pleasure.
zh: 以旧行不能过新生活. 一旦你终于意识到这一点, 你会停止紧紧抓住 与不为你服务的乐趣。 如果你真的想改变,你会改变的。 大多数人只是幻想着更好的生活 成为另一个快乐的来源

text: Lesson number 2: Contemplate what you don't want often. thedankoe.com/letters/im-28-…
zh: 课号2: 孔滕普尔,你不想经常要的东西. the. com. com. com.

text: The easiest way to figure out what you want in life is to make a habit out of reflecting on what you don't want. Then, all you have to do is master the art of saying no to that. Most people are stuck in their stupid busy lives. They don't register what they don't want because it's normal to them at this point. You need to sit down. Reflect on your month without getting distracted. Journal it on paper if you have to. It's incredibly difficult to change if you aren't aware of what to change.
zh: 生活中最容易想出你想要的 就是在思考你不想做的事时 习惯的习惯 那你只需要掌握拒绝的艺术 大多数人都困在自己愚蠢的忙碌生活中. 他们不登记他们不想登记的东西,因为此时对他们来说是正常的. 汝等应坐. 想想你的月份,不要分心。 有必要的话,请在纸上登报 如果你不知道要改变什么, 改变是很难的。

text: If you don't know where to start, start with yourself. Your fitness. Your nutrition. Your money. Your skill set. Your mindset. Solve one little pain point in your life and I promise you'll know what to do next.
zh: 不知从何起,自自始. ō砰 你的营养 你的钱。 诀穦 你的心态。 解决你生命中一个小问题 我保证你以后会知道该怎么做

text: Balance is found in the extremes. Pushing to failure. Prioritizing recovery. Deep focused work. Worry-free rest. Periods of intensity for rapid and rare progress followed by periods of consistency to maintain new baselines. Stuck in the middle is a slow and average death.
zh: 平衡存在于极端. 推为失败. 优先恢复. 深入聚焦工作. 忧恼无恼休息. 快速和罕见进展的强度时期,然后是保持新基线的一致性时期。 中途的粘滞是缓慢和平均的死亡.

text: Writing tip to stop overcomplicating it: Think about the essence of what you want to say. Say it. If it's short form, stop there. If it's long form, back it up with quotes, key points, stories, and anything else that strengthens what you said.
zh: 写小费以停止过度的重复:想一想你想说什么的本质. 说吧 如果只是短的,就停在那里。 如果它是长的形态, 备份它与引文,关键点,故事, 和任何其他能加强你所说的话。

text: It's all a mind game. It's not difficult. It's not overwhelming. It's not negative. Those are interpretations. You can't find the perspective that allows you to gain an advantage over that situation. Master your mind. It's the starting point from which you master anything else.
zh: 皆是智游戏. 亦不难. 岂非压倒. 亦不负. 这些是解释。 你找不到一个能让你 获得优势的视角 师作么生会. 这是你掌握其他东西的起点

text: Stop giving attention to things you don't want more of in your life. Negative thoughts. Behavior of other people. Anything you read. Anything you watch. Where you are now is the result of where you placed your attention in the past. Attention amplifies anything it touches, and since it's easy for your mind to get distracted by meaningless things, it's not a surprise why your life takes that shape.
zh: 别管你生活中不想做的事了 负面思想. 彼众生所作行. 随所读诵. 任何你看着。 你现在在哪里,是因为你过去把注意力放在哪里。 注意力放大了它触及的任何事物,由于你很容易被无意义的事物分心,所以你的生活为何会如此形状,并不奇怪.

text: You aren't disciplined because you keep putting yourself in environments that give you a chance to be undisciplined.
zh: 你没有纪律 因为你一直把自己置于 让你有机会失去纪律的环境里

text: You only encounter mistakes if you're actually doing something. For most people, they see this as a bad thing. They lack pain tolerance from extended comfort and quit before things start getting good. If you're making mistakes in a non-self-destructive direction, have faith for just a bit longer. You're on the right path.
zh: 你只有在做点什么时才会遇到错误 对大多数人来说,他们认为这是件坏事。 他们缺乏耐痛性 延长舒适度 并退出 事情开始好起来。 如果你在非自毁方向上犯错, 要有信心,只要再多一点。 汝当往诣正法道.

text: The only thing more difficult than pursuing the best version of yourself is not pursuing the best version of yourself.
zh: 唯一比追求自己最优秀的版本更困难的是,没有追求自己最优秀的版本.

text: You need to move. Your body. Your mind. Your projects. Your relationships. Your finances. Because if you don't, you don't stay the same, you slowly decay without realizing it until you reach the point where it's much, much harder to dig back out. Entropy waits for no one.
zh: 你得离开这儿 汝身众生. み稱 你的项目。 你们的关系 财经. 因为如果你不这样做,你就不会变的一样, 你慢慢地衰变,却不觉悟到它,直到你到达了它要挖出来要多,更难的地方. 仁义等无相.

text: I hate to break it to you, but most of your beliefs are wrong. If you don't look back on your mind 10 years from now and laugh, you are just like that closed minded person you secretly despise. Beliefs are tools. Grip onto them and act, but let go when they don't serve you.
zh: 我也不想告诉你 但大多数信仰都是错误的 如果你不回首十年后的思想 并笑起来, 你就像一个封闭的心智的人 你偷偷地鄙视。 信仰是工具,是人类的信仰,是人类的信仰。 抓住他们,行动起来,但当他们不为你服务时就放手。

text: Pain is a great teacher because it helps you realize two things: what's worth suffering for and what you never want to experience again. Pain gives you direction. That's why most people are lost.
zh: 痛苦是一个伟大的老师,因为它帮助你意识到两件事: 值得痛苦的东西, 以及你再也不想经历的东西. 痛苦让你有方向 故多见失道.

text: The best writing isn't about stringing words together in a way that sounds smart. It's about hunting for great ideas, letting them marinate in your subconscious, and synthesizing them into something worth reading.
zh: 最好的写作不是把词串在一起 听起来很聪明 是为了寻找伟大的想法, 让他们在你的潜意识中沉浸, 把它们合成一些值得阅读的东西。

text: We may reach out to a few of you to dig deeper into the story, so if you're up for it, keep an eye on DMs :)
zh: 我们也许可以联系你们中的一些人,深入探讨这个故事,所以如果你愿意,请留意DMs:)

text: Working on a project and need help. Has to do with going on a walk. How has walking helped with your ideas, creativity, health, relationships, general quality of life, or anything else? What's your "walking" story?
zh: 正在做一个项目,需要帮助。 和散步有关 走路帮助你的想法,创造力,健康,关系,一般生活质量等等? 你"行走"的故事是什么?

text: Your life changes when you finally realize that anything is possible (on a long enough time scale) as long as you build the steps that lead to it being possible.
zh: 当你终于意识到只要你建立导致可能实现的步骤,任何事情都是可能的(在足够长的时间尺度上),你的生活都会改变.

text: The most important thing you can teach your child: Agency. "the tendency to initiate action towards a goal without outside prompting, instruction, or permission." And if you aren't a child, this new podcast with @Devon_Eriksen_ may help: youtu.be/18ahFtnuBwI
zh: 最重要的事情是教孩子:机构。 "在未受到外界的鼓励、指示或许可的情况下, 开始采取行动实现目标的趋势" 如果你不是孩子,这个新播客在@Devon Eriksen 可能帮助:yutu.be/18ahFtnuBwI

text: "But how do I start it?" You tell me. Open up the video editor, record a video without overthinking it, start editing, and I guarantee you will find mistakes that you can start improving much faster than if you spent 10 hours studying and not building. Open a document and braindump some ideas. Write a post and post it even if it's terrible. Just start doing something. Anything. If you aren't moving you are decaying. If you don't, you're simply delaying the amount of time of getting your first terrible iteration out. Doesn't matter if you spend 5 minutes or 10 years on it. The. First. Iteration. Will. Suck. Hitting "send" is nature's filter for people who aren't serious about making it.
zh: "但怎样开始?" 你告诉我。 打开视频编辑器,录制一段视频而不过分思考,开始编辑,我保证你会发现错误,比花费10个小时学习而不建设,你开始进步的速度要快得多. 打开文件,并思考一些想法。 写个帖子,即使很可怕也要贴出来. 开始做点什么吧 什么都行,我爱她 如果你不动,你就会衰败 如果你不这样做,你只是拖延了 时间来取出你的第一个可怕的迭代。 5分钟或10年都无所谓 首先是那个 迭代经. 威尔 烂透了 攻击"发送"是大自然对不认真制作的人的过滤器.

text: One of the most important skills is how to learn skills fast. You need to be able to adapt going into a future of AI and automation. And learning one skill will only lead to you being replaced. But most people don't know how to learn. 1) Stop Learning "Skills" The first problem is that people think they learn skills by picking one and studying it. No. That's how you learn the same thing as everyone else and get placed in a job or business that can be replaced in a millisecond. Instead, you learn anything and everything that allows you to achieve your ideal lifestyle. Everything must be filtered through your vision. 2) Start The Project, Then Learn Most people get trapped in tutorial hell. They watch videos all day and after 3 months they still have no idea what they're doing when they decide to start. Start a project that will move the needle toward your vision. Start. It. Then, when you hit a roadblock, supplement with education. 3) Ship The Project Post the video. Send the newsletter. Launch the product. Pitch your service. Especially if you're embarrassed. The harsh reality is that if you don't have real people using your project, you have no idea if it's valuable. You don't determine its value. If you never hit send, you have no idea if you're moving in the right direction, and you will get lost. I'll end with this: You can't improve what doesn't exist, therefore you don't actually start learning a thing until you put your project in front of people to judge, criticize, and use.
zh: 最重要的技能之一是如何快速学习技能. 你需要能够适应未来 AI和自动化。 学习一种技能只会让你被取代 但大多数人不知道如何学习。 1)停止学习"技能". 第一个问题是人们认为自己通过选择一个并学习来学习技能. 没有 如此,你学会了与其他人一样的东西,并被安排到一个工作或生意中,在毫秒内可以被替换. 相反,你学到了一切 让你实现理想生活方式的东西 万事皆得通过你的视觉过滤 2) 启动项目,然后学习大多数人被困在教程地狱. 他们整天看视频,三个月后,他们仍然不知道自己在决定开始时在做什么. 启动一个能把针头移到你视力上的项目 开始 就是这样 然后,当你撞到路障, 补充教育。 3) Ship The Project张贴视频. 发通讯. 推出产品. 少管闲事 尤其是你觉得尴尬的时候 严酷的现实是,如果你没有真正的人利用你的项目,你根本不知道它是否具有价值. 价值不由你决定 如果你从未击中发送,你不知道你是否朝着正确的方向移动,你会迷路. 我最后要说: 无法改善不存在的东西,因此,你实际上不会开始学习某件事,直到你把你的项目摆在人们面前,来判断,批评,以及使用.

text: Most people don't know what to do with their life. So they float along until they end up in a place they feel like they can't escape. In an ideal situation, you wouldn't let this happen. But it did, so here's how you take back control: thedankoe.com/letters/how-to…
zh: 多数人不知如何生活. 所以他们一直漂浮着 直到最后他们觉得自己无法逃脱 在理想的情况下,你不会让这种情况发生。 但确实如此,所以你是如何收回控制权的:thedankoe.com/letters/ how-to.

text: Yeah they're decently comparable right now. We don't have databases (or care to) or other dashboard style features. Atm the differentiation is the capture system, pane layouts for writers, connections, and tags. Gamechangers for some, but Notion users may not care for them until they test their workflow out in it. In 4-6 months, I don't think they'll be comparable aside from document editing, like most editors or note-takers are. Not in the sense that we'll be better than Notion, that'll be tough haha. But in the sense that we will serve a different use case entirely and it will be complimentary to Notion if people want to do project management and stuff in there. We're heading more in the direction of capturing anything seamlessly, organizing it without wasting time, and surfacing unique and relevant ideas right when you need them for your writing, content, outlines, projects, etc. I will say, I do think our team will surprise a lot of people in that timeframe.
zh: 是啊,他们正像 可比现在。 我们没有数据库(或关心)或其他仪表板样式的特性. Atm的区分是捕捉系统,写作的窗格布局,连接,和标记. 一些人的游戏更改器,但Notion用户在测试其中的工作流程之前可能不会关心他们. 4到6个月后,除了文件编辑之外,我认为它们不会像大多数编辑或记录员一样具有可比性. 并不是说我们比诺特强 那可就很难了 但是,我们将会完全为不同的用途服务, 如果人们想在那里做项目管理和做一些事情,那将会是对“概念”的赞美。 我们正朝着无缝捕捉任何东西的方向前进, 组织起来而不浪费时间, 并展示出独特和相关的想法, 当你需要这些想法来写作,内容,大纲,项目等。 我想说,我认为我们的团队会在这个时间范围内给很多人带来惊喜。

text: figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out figure it out
zh: 寻其解其解,以 it其解其解.

text: Those who struggle with money struggle with awareness. You pay people daily. You do work that pays you some and others more. Your life is filled with items you paid for. You're a part of the process but not aware of the process, so you're dependent on it and can't replicate it.
zh: 与金钱斗争的人与意识斗争. 你每天给人钱。 你的工作能给你很多钱 你的生活充满了你付的钱。 你是过程的一部分,但不知道过程,所以你依赖它,不能复制.

text: You're overwhelmed because you're holding 1 year's worth of skill acquisition, work, and growth in your head rather than the 1 thing you can do right now to get there.
zh: 你被压在了你的头上 因为你持有一年的 技能获得,工作,成长的价值 而不是你现在可以做的一件事 才能到达那里。

text: You're unhappy because your mind becomes what you pay attention to, and you pay attention to what you think is important, and people have told you what you should think is important your entire life, but you haven't realized that maybe they don't know what they're talking about.
zh: 你感到不快乐是因为你的思维成为你所关注的事物,你关注你认为重要的事物,人们已经告诉你你认为的人生中很重要,但是你还没有意识到也许他们不知道自己在说什么.

text: Here is fine to write it (or in Discord) Since we're still in beta, dedicated iPad and mobile are in progress - takes a while to build those right. However, we will also have (good) image transcription where you can snap a pic of writing or a book and have it highlight / transcribe.
zh: 这里可以写( 或者在Discord) 因为我们仍在Beta, 专用iPad和移动正在进展中 - 需要一段时间来建立这些权利。 并让书能突出/转录。

text: Having a plan B is for people who don't trust their ability to learn, build, and adapt.
zh: 拥有B计划是给那些不相信自己学习,建设,适应能力的人.

text: The average person feels cheated by the dream they were sold. School didn't prepare them for this. The job didn't give them enough money, time, or security. Since that's all they were taught, they don't see another way to live, so they drown in anything that will numb the pain.
zh: 普通人觉得被卖掉的梦欺骗了 学校没有让他们做好准备 这份工作没有给他们足够的钱,时间,或安全. 由于他们只学习了这些,所以他们看不到其他的生活方式,所以他们溺死在任何会麻木疼痛的东西中.

text: The world is changing. Anyone, not everyone, with internet access can write a post, garner attention, fuel their startup, or monetize their creative work. Here's how to build social capital, the currency of the new rich: thedankoe.com/letters/social…
zh: 世界正在变化。 任何拥有网络的人, 而不是每个人, 都可以写文章、引起关注、为创业加油、或将创作工作货币化。 以下是如何建设社会资本,新富人的货币: thedankoe.com/letters/social...

text: If you can't solve a problem with hard work, you can probably solve it by going on a walk.
zh: 工作辛苦解决不了问题,可能通过散步解决.

text: Do whatever you want. When you do, you will make massive mistakes. At that point, you no longer want it, and slowly course correct toward what you really want. If you always do what others want for you, you will never discover what you want.
zh: 随所欲行. 当你这样做,你会犯大错。 此时,你不再想要它, 慢慢地走向正确 正确的东西你真正想要。 如果你总是为别人做你想要的事, 你永远不会发现你想要的。

text: If you struggle with mental clarity: - Pull out a piece of paper - Write down all of your thoughts - Cross out all of the distractions - Focus on what’s left A clear head can be as simple as putting your mind on paper and organizing it.
zh: 如果你用精神清晰的思维来挣扎: 拿出一张纸 — 写下你的所有想法 — 将所有的注意力都冲掉 — 专注于剩下的东西 — — 清醒的头脑可以像把思想放在纸上并组织起来一样简单。

text: You actually can do whatever you want whenever you want, and if you have a decent set of values, you should.
zh: 你随时可以做你想做的事, 如果你有一套体面的价值观,你应该。

text: there's nothing stopping you
zh: 没有什么能阻止你

text: You are learning every millisecond of the day. Your mind is a sponge soaking in information. Being programmed. Being conditioned. Being told what to do and how to do it. For most people, this is unconscious. If you were to learn, consciously, your life would turn around fast.
zh: 你每天每学一毫秒 你脑子里有一条海绵 浸透了信息 被编程。 被限制。 受戒行及如何是道. 对大多数人来说,这是无意识的。 如果你有意识地学习,你的生活会迅速转动.

text: Dopamine tells you when something is important to your survival-wired brain. Companies exploit this by testing combinations of fat, sugar, salt, fear, pleasure, and conviction to addict you. Very few things are important, and it's your duty to discover and pursue them.
zh: 多巴胺告诉你 当某件事对你生存线的大脑很重要的时候 公司通过测试脂肪、糖、盐、恐惧、快乐 和信念的结合来利用这些东西, 来测试你上瘾。 很少的事情很重要,你有责任发现和追踪它们。

text: Your past doesn't exist. It's just an image in your mind. But you decide to live as if that image were your reality. Even worse, you Photoshop that image. You make it worse than it was and it slowly dims the future image you could create.
zh: 你过去不存在 心念中只是一影像. 但你决定活得像那幅画一样 更糟的是,你把图像处理掉 你让它比以前更糟, 它慢慢地淡化了你能够创造的未来形象。

text: Most new wealth is being generated in the digital space because its barrier of entry is skill, not money or location.
zh: 大部分新的财富正在数字空间产生,因为它的进入障碍是技能,而不是金钱或位置。

text: Dopamine is the secret behind both the successful and the failures. You don't need to cut it out of your life. You need to learn how to leverage it: youtu.be/Bq9de46Uh4I
zh: 多巴胺是成功和失败背后的秘密. 汝今不须断其生机. 你需要学会如何利用它:youtu.be/Bq9de46Uh4I

text: You aren't fulfilled because your mind makes sense of the world in stories and you stopped living your own.
zh: 你没有满足 因为你的头脑 使世界在故事中有意义 你停止了自己的生活。

text: Go on more walks. Walk for no reason. Walk to solve a problem. Walk to blow off steam. Walk to get outside. Walk to listen, read, and learn. Walk to escape distractions. Walk to improve your health. Walk to think. A simple walking habit can change absolutely everything.
zh: 继续走 无故行走. 步行解决一个问题。 走着去吹蒸汽。 走出去,我们出去。 步行听,读,学. 走着走着去逃避分心 步行改善健康。 步行去思考。 简单的行走习惯可以改变一切

text: The death of your business happens when you obsess over a website, logo, LLC, and taxes before you actually start getting customers.
zh: 当你在开始获得客户之前 沉迷于网站、标志、专卖店和税收时,你的生意就会死去。

text: If you're okay with 10 cents in your bank account, you won't see that as a problem. If you're okay with 20 pounds of extra body fat, you won't do anything to change it. Your standards – your settling point – will ruin you if they are absymally low.
zh: 如果你在银行账户里有10美分钱,你就不会觉得有问题了. 二十斤多身肥,若能行,则不作任何改变. 你的标准--你的住处--如果他们低调,你必遭毁灭。

text: Most people don't start businesses. They start distractions. They focus on everything except for building a product or service and getting customers. If I were starting from scratch, here's exactly what I would do to avoid all the nonsense: thedankoe.com/letters/the-si…
zh: 大多数人不创业. 他们开始分心。 他们专注于一切,除了建立产品或服务,以及获得客户. 如果我从头开始,我就会这样做,以避免所有的胡说八道:thedankoe.com/letters/the-si...

text: Many successful people are born out of an overwhelming amount of negativity that they chose to use as fuel, not an excuse.
zh: 许多成功的人们是因为他们选择了以大量消极态度作为燃料而不是借口而生的。

text: Building a project will teach you more than watching a tutorial ever will.
zh: 建一个项目会教给你更多的东西 比看一个教程永远。

text: Normalize disappearing for 1 hour a day to build a better future for yourself.
zh: 正常化消失一天一个小时 为自己建立一个更美好的未来

text: How to start a billion dollar business: Don't. Start a one-person business. Build enough cash flow to leave your job. Build enough of an audience to fuel that dream company you want to start. Focus on the skills and interests you can already help people with.
zh: 如何开亿业务:别. 开一人行. 积累足够的现金流 以离开你的工作。 足够吸引观众 来为你想创办的梦中公司加油 专注于你已经可以帮助别人的技能和兴趣.

text: Most people end up 50 years old with the emotional maturity of a 15 year old.
zh: 大多数人最终50岁,与15岁的情绪成熟.

text: If you're in your 20s: - Experiment with new paths - Start your business failures now - Study the opposite of your beliefs - Challenge yourself with new interests The worst thing you can do is stop learning, growing, and changing your mind after leaving the school system.
zh: 如果你20多岁: - 尝试新的路径 - 开始你的生意失败 - 学习与你的信仰相反 - 挑战自己,以新的兴趣 最糟糕的就是停止学习,成长,离开学校系统后改变主意.

text: It's okay to disappear and build the thing you've been putting off (that will make you a better person when you come back).
zh: 消失和建造你一直拖延的东西(这会让你在回来后成为一个更好的人)是好的.

text: If you enjoyed this thread, consider sharing it so others can see the value they have collecting dust on their hard drive.
zh: 如果你喜欢这个线程,考虑分享它,以便其他人能看到他们在硬盘上收集的灰尘的价值.

zh: 秘密是有用的。 创造. 设计. 建楼. 写作. 卖,学 解脱. 假说. 合成经. 帮帮我 贡献比你们所接受的更多价值。 别作ech.

text: Why doesn't finding the "best" niche work? Well... just look at how many people don't quit, but there are other reasons. 1) By default, you're entering a space that you know nothing about with people you don't care about. 2) That niche isn't going to be profitable if you don't understand it inside and out. 3) You are already part of a niche. You already have skill and expertise that's helped you... so it can help someone else. So, stop trying to "find" a niche. Create a niche by attracting the people you can help the most. Another problem: When you focus on a niche, you box yourself in for no reason and limit yourself. When you focus on pain points, you resonate with anyone with those pain points. In other words, you gain more leverage and reach without sacrificing the benefits of niching down. The lesson: Focus on one burning pain point. (This answers the "why should they care" question) Create a product or service that resolves it. (This answers the "what can I help them achieve" question). Write content around that pain point to attract people that you can help the most. If you can persist through trial and error, I have no doubt you'll win. --- Ad (scroll away if you don't like ads): By the way, the One-Person Business Launchpad is 50% off until the end of Black Friday weekend. >> thedankoe.com/get/opbl Grab it here if you want arguably the simplest way to start a modern business.

(这解答了"他们为什么要关心"的问题) 创建解决它的产品或服务. (这回答了"我能帮助他们实现什么"的问题). 写内容围绕疼痛点 吸引人们,你可以帮助最多。 如果你能坚持通过审判和错误, 我毫不怀疑你会赢。 - – - 说到这里 - - 广告(如果你不喜欢广告的话,就滚开): 顺便说一句,"一人行"推出板的票价在黑色星期五周末结束前为50%. >> com/get/opbl 互联网档案馆的存檔,存档日期2013-09-02. 如果你想用最简单的方法 来创办现代企业的话 就拿过来

text: Stop trying to learn "skills." Nothing valuable is built with ONE skill. Instead, learn individual techniques that allow you to build a project that others find useful. Stack techniques, not skills, and you'll slowly reduce your chances of being replaced.
zh: 别再学"技能"了 任何有价值的东西都不是用一个技能建造的。 相反,学习个人技术,让你能够构建一个其他人认为有用的项目. 堆叠技术,而不是技能, 你会慢慢地减少你被替换的机会。

text: A little known truth: Skills are groups of techniques. What?? When you start trying to acquire a skill like Photoshop, you don't sit around studying the software all day. Instead, you start creating the project. Then you hit a roadblock. You don't know how to complete a certain part of it. So, you go on YouTube and look up how to do *that specific thing.* As you repeat this to build the project, you may not understand all of Photoshop, but you have a group of techniques that allow you to do quite a lot with the software. In something like Photoshop, you really only need to know a few things: selections, masks, and adjustment layers. Once you know those, you can create practically anything. The same holds true with every single skill imaginable. When you're building a business or side hustle, you don't acquire the skill of "business." You don't even acquire the skill of "marketing." You have a goal: land a customer. You study a technique: how to write an engaging social post. You study another: how to write a short promotion. You study another: how to build a simple landing page. You study another: how to accept payment. When you combine all of those, you achieve Level 1 of making money on your own. The lesson: Build more projects. Learn more techniques. Notice if you're getting closer to your goal. Try, fail, and persist until you achieve the goal, then repeat. --- After 4 years, I'm finally listening. The most common question I get is, "Okay, I like the concept of a one-person business, but how do I *actually* start one." Almost nobody is teaching the absolute beginner steps to get up and running to make your first $5K (when you have no idea where to start or what to sell). So, The One-Person Business Launchpad is 50% off through Black Friday weekend. You can pick it up here: thedankoe.com/get/opbl It's on pre-order right now, but I'll be releasing the curriculum in a few days. If you want to lock in the $49 price, check it out.
zh: 略为人所知的真理:技能是技术的组合. 什么? 说说说看 What? 当你开始尝试获得像Photoshop这样的技能时,你不会整天坐在这里学习软件. 相反,你开始创建项目。 然后你撞上了路障 路过一个路障 你不知道如何完成其中的某个部分. 所以,你上YouTube,看看如何做 具体的事情。 当你重复这个项目时, 你可能不理解所有 Photoshop, 但你有一组技术, 让你可以做很多软件。 在Photoshop这样的地方,你只需要知道一些事情: 选择,面具,以及调整层。 一旦你了解这些, 你就可以创造出几乎所有的东西。 每一种能想象的技能都一样。 当你在建企业或边事时, 你并没有得到"企业"的技能。 你甚至没有获得"营销"的技能. 你有一个目标: 租给客户。 你研究一种技术:如何写一个参与的社会文章。 你研究另一个:如何写一个短期的升迁。 你研究另一个:如何建立一个简单的登陆页。 你研究另一个:如何接受付款。 当你把这些都结合起来, 你就能达到第一级, 自己赚钱。 教训:建设更多项目. 学多点技巧 注意你是否越来越接近目标 尝试,失败,并坚持到达到目标,然后重复. - – - 说到这里 - - 四年之后,终于听了. 我得到的最常见问题是, “好吧,我喜欢一个人的生意概念, 但我怎么才能“实际”开始一个生意。” 几乎没有人教绝对的初学者步骤, 起来跑步, 让你的第一个5K(当你不知道从哪里开始或卖什么). 单人商业启动板 黑色星期五周末半停 您可以在这里取回: thedankoe.com/get/opbl 现在在预购, 但我会发布课程 在几天内。 如果你想锁定49美元的价格,就检查出来.

text: The problem isn't that you don't know what to work on. It's that you haven't tried anything outside of what your parents, teachers, and boss have assigned to you. You haven't experimented with life to the point of finding that one thing you can't help but work on.
